Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.ID: Identifier that must be unique across the game.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.OtherIDs: Legacy identifiers. You shouldn't normally need these.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.Range: How far the target can be when the projectile is shot at it.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.IdealRange: The range from which the AI will try to use this weapon.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.IdealRadius: The target radii, in tiles, that map onto [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.IdealRange]]. Against a target no larger than the minimum radius the AI prefers the near end of the ideal range, against one at least the maximum radius the far end, interpolating for sizes in between. Has no effect unless [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.IdealRange]] is set.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.Speed: How fast the projectile moves forward when spawned.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.ReceivableBuffs: The buff types the projectile inherits from the part that fired it. Buffs not listed here are dropped when the projectile spawns, so a buff must be listed for buffable values such as [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.Range]] and [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.Speed]] to respond to it.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.ForgetTarget: Whether the projectile discards the firing weapon's target when it spawns. Components and effect filters that check the projectile's target or targeted ship then treat it as having none.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.BulletRules.RandomRotation: Whether the projectile spawns facing a random direction.

Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.Penetration: The projectile's total penetration capacity. Each part it passes through subtracts that part's [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.InitialPenetrationResistance]] (or [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ContinuingPenetrationResistance]] for further cells of the same part), scaled by the penetration factor fields, and the projectile is destroyed once the capacity is used up. At `0` the projectile stops at the first thing it hits.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.PenetrationSpeed: Speed, in tiles per second relative to the hit ship, that the projectile slows to when it hits an operational part and starts penetrating. Left unset, the projectile keeps its normal flight velocity while penetrating.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.PenetratesOperational: Whether the projectile can penetrate into operational parts. When false it expends all remaining [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.Penetration]] on the first operational part it hits and is destroyed there.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.PenetratesStructural: Whether the projectile can penetrate into structural parts such as armor and structure. When false it expends all remaining [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.Penetration]] on the first structural part it hits and is destroyed there.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.PenetratesShields: Whether the projectile can pass through shields. When true a hit shield subtracts its penetration resistance times [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.ShieldPenetrationFactor]] from the remaining [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.Penetration]]. When false the projectile is stopped by the first shield it hits.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.PenetratesFriendlies: Whether the projectile keeps flying after hitting a ship it cannot damage, such as a friendly ship. When false it is destroyed at the point of that hit. Only relevant when [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.FriendlyShipLowCollisions]] or [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.FriendlyShipHighCollisions]] allow the collision to happen at all.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.PartCategoryPenetrationFactors: Extra multipliers on penetration resistance for parts in specific part categories. Every listed category the hit part belongs to multiplies the resistance subtracted from [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.Penetration]], on top of [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.OperationalPenetrationFactor]] or [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.StructuralPenetrationFactor]]. Values below 1 penetrate such parts more easily.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.OperationalPenetrationFactor: Multiplier on the penetration resistance of operational parts. Values below 1 let the projectile penetrate deeper through them, values above 1 drain [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.Penetration]] faster.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.StructuralPenetrationFactor: Multiplier on the penetration resistance of structural parts such as armor and structure. Values below 1 let the projectile penetrate deeper through them, values above 1 drain [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.Penetration]] faster.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.ShieldPenetrationFactor: Multiplier on the penetration resistance of shields the projectile passes through. Only applies when [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.PenetratesShields]] is true.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.FriendlyShipLowCollisions: Whether the projectile collides with the low (ordinary) part colliders of ships it cannot damage, including the ship that fired it. Setting this false lets the shot fly over friendly ships while still hitting their high colliders, which is how the deck cannon's bullet fires over its own ship.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.FriendlyShipHighCollisions: Whether the projectile collides with the high part colliders of ships it cannot damage. High colliders are those marked `IsHigh` on raised parts such as the deck cannon turret or sensor array.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.NonFriendlyShipLowCollisions: Whether the projectile collides with the low (ordinary) part colliders of ships it can damage. Setting this false makes the shot pass over enemy ships and only interact with their high colliders.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.NonFriendlyShipHighCollisions: Whether the projectile collides with the high part colliders of ships it can damage. High colliders are those marked `IsHigh` on raised parts such as the deck cannon turret or sensor array.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.SourcePartCollisionDelay: Time, in seconds after spawning, during which collisions with the part that fired the projectile are ignored. Vanilla missiles and mines use about 1.5 physics ticks so they can leave their launcher without immediately hitting it.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.ShieldCollisions: Filter deciding whose shields the projectile collides with. Defaults to enemy shields, plus a friendly ship's shields when that ship is the deliberate target, but never the firing ship's own shields. Allowed shield hits trigger [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.HitShield]].
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.BulletCollisions: Filter deciding which other targetable projectiles this projectile can collide with. Collisions are off by default. Allowed hits trigger [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.HitBullet]], and whether the projectile survives is controlled by [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.PenetratesBullets]].
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.PenetratesBullets: Whether the projectile keeps flying after hitting another projectile. When false it is destroyed on the spot. Only relevant when [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.BulletCollisions]] allows bullet hits.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.CrewCollisions: Filter deciding which crew in space the projectile collides with. Collisions are off by default. Allowed hits trigger [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.HitCrew]], and whether the projectile survives is controlled by [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.PenetratesCrew]].
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.PenetratesCrew: Whether the projectile keeps flying after hitting a crew member in space. When false it is destroyed on the hit. Only relevant when [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.CrewCollisions]] allows crew hits.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.FactorEffectsWith: Name of another component on the same projectile, such as a `Targetable` or `DamagePool`, whose remaining-strength factor scales this hit's effect intensity. The penetrating hit in turn exposes its own factor, the remaining fraction of [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.Penetration]], for other components to reference.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.HitOperational: Effects applied the first time the projectile hits an operational part. Further operational parts along the penetration path use [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.PenetratingOperational]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.HitStructural: Effects applied the first time the projectile hits a structural part such as armor or structure. Hitting an operational part also counts as the first structural hit, so further structural parts use [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.PenetratingStructural]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.PenetratingOperational: Effects applied to each further operational part the projectile penetrates after the first hit that triggered [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.HitOperational]].
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.PenetratingStructural: Effects applied to each further structural part the projectile penetrates after the first hit that triggered [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.HitStructural]] or [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.HitOperational]].
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.FinishedPenetratingOperational: Effects applied when the projectile runs out of [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.Penetration]] inside an operational part, its final stop. If that part is also the first operational part hit, [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.HitOperational]] plays instead.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.FinishedPenetratingStructural: Effects applied when the projectile runs out of [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.Penetration]] inside a structural part, its final stop. If that part is also the first part hit, [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.HitStructural]] plays instead.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.HitShield: Effects applied when the projectile hits a shield allowed by [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.ShieldCollisions]].
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.HitFriendly: Effects that replace the normal hit rules whenever the struck ship cannot be damaged by the projectile's source, such as a friendly ship. When unset, friendly hits use the regular hit rules.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.HitBullet: Effects applied when the projectile hits another projectile allowed by [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.BulletCollisions]].
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.HitCrew: Effects applied when the projectile hits a crew member in space allowed by [[Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetratingHitRules.CrewCollisions]].
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.HitsFriendlyStructure: Whether structural hit effects also trigger on ships the projectile cannot damage. When false, hitting a friendly ship's structural parts applies no effects.
Cosmoteer.Bullets.Hits.BulletPenetrating.CalculateWorldHitNormals: Whether the hit will calculate world-space part hit normals.

Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.CostPerCrew: The credits charged for each crew member hired. Also counts toward a ship's total value based on its crew capacity.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.MaxHealth: The hit points a crew member has while flying in space. Weapons that target crew subtract from it, and the crew dies when it reaches 0.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.BaseSpeed: Walking speed aboard a ship, in tiles per second. Multiplied by each part's crew speed factor and by the carried resource's `CarrySpeed`. Also used to turn path distances into travel-time estimates when crew pick resource sources.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.DoorOpenDistance: How close, in tiles, a walking crew must get to the door ahead of it before it holds that door open. Works with [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.DoorCloseDistance]].
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.DoorCloseDistance: How far, in tiles, a crew keeps holding a door open after passing through it. Beyond this distance the door is released and can close.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.JobAssignmentsPerSecond: How many queued normal-priority jobs per second a ship recomputes crew assignments for. Higher values make crew react to new work faster at more CPU cost. Low-priority jobs use [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.LowPriorityJobAssignmentsPerSecond]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.LowPriorityJobAssignmentsPerSecond: How many jobs per second from the ship's low-priority queue get their crew assignments recomputed. The normal queue uses [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.JobAssignmentsPerSecond]].
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ResourceSearchesPerSecond: How many resource sinks per second re-search the ship for possible supply sources. Rate-limits the expensive source search that feeds resource-delivery jobs.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ManualTransferJobExpensiveCheckInterval: How often each pending manual resource-transfer job runs its expensive validity check (completion and source reachability). Despite the name it acts as a rate, roughly this many checks per job per second, spread round-robin over all pending jobs.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SalvageJobExpensiveCheckInterval: How often each active salvage or deconstruction job runs its expensive finished check, including sight and distance tests. Like [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ManualTransferJobExpensiveCheckInterval]] it acts as roughly this many checks per job per second, spread round-robin.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.MaxCrewSearchIterations: Cap on pathfinding iterations when searching outward from a job for the nearest available crew. Limits how far the search expands before giving up.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.EqualPriorityJobDistanceThreshold: Distance bias, in tiles, in favor of a crew's current job. When two jobs have equal priority, a crew is only pulled onto the new job if its current job's remaining distance exceeds the distance to the new job by more than this.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.OffShipEqualPriorityJobDistanceThreshold: The equivalent of [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.EqualPriorityJobDistanceThreshold]] applied to crew that are currently off their ship.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.TotalPathTrafficIncrease: Traffic cost added to the cells along each walking crew's registered path, and removed again when the path is released. Path searches that account for traffic then prefer less congested routes.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.CrewUpdatesPerSecond: How many times per second each crew member's movement and job logic runs, staggered across ticks. Its inverse is the timestep a job receives each time a crew works on it.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SourceRefreshesPerTick: How many crew per physics tick re-validate their assigned crew source (the quarters part housing them), cycling round-robin through all crew in the simulation.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.HomelessWanderDistance: Radius, in tiles, around the spot where a crew became homeless within which it picks random wander destinations.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.FollowShipDistance: How far, in tiles, beyond the home ship's bounding circle idle or waiting crew in space position themselves, for example while idling outside or waiting for an oxygen refill.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.NewHomeSearchDistance: Radius, in tiles, a homeless crew searches for a ship of its player with spare crew capacity. The nearest such ship becomes its new home.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.NewHomeSearchInterval: Minimum and maximum seconds a homeless crew waits between searches for a new home ship. Each wait is randomized within the range.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.LoseHomeShipDistance: Distance from the home ship, in tiles, beyond which an off-ship crew member is abandoned and counted as killed. A warning shows earlier at [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.LoseHomeShipWarningDistance]].
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.LoseHomeShipWarningDistance: Distance from the home ship, in tiles, at which an off-ship crew shows the [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.DistanceWarningSprite]] indicator. Also used throughout the game as the working range for off-ship activity such as salvage, nugget collection, crew and resource transfers, and hiring between ships.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ExtraNuggetCollectDistance: Maximum distance, in tiles, at which a crew collecting resource nuggets picks a nearby follow-up nugget headed to the same destination as its next collection target.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.MaxNuggetCollectSpeed: Speed above which a drifting resource nugget is not offered as a collection job. Crew wait for nuggets to slow below this before flying out to grab them.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.Radius: Nominal body radius of a crew member, in tiles. Used for selection hit-testing, selection and highlight circles, and warning indicator sizing. The physics size in space is [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.CollisionRadius]].
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.CollisionRadius: Radius, in tiles, of the circular physics body created for a crew flying in space.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.Density: Density of the space crew's physics body. Together with [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.CollisionRadius]] it determines the body's mass.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.AccelerationOverDistance: Acceleration a crew flying in space applies toward its desired velocity. Blended between the two values based on the remaining distance to the destination, as mapped by [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.AccelerationOverDistanceRange]].
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.DecelerationOverDistance: Braking acceleration used instead of [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.AccelerationOverDistance]] when a flying crew needs to slow down. Blended over [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.DecelerationOverDistanceRange]] by remaining distance.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.AccelerationOverDistanceRange: Remaining-distance range, in tiles, over which [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.AccelerationOverDistance]] is interpolated. At or below the low end the first acceleration value applies, at or above the high end the second.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.DecelerationOverDistanceRange: Remaining-distance range, in tiles, over which [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.DecelerationOverDistance]] is interpolated.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.RotSpeed: Maximum turn rate, in radians per second, of a crew flying in space.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.LinearDamping: Viscous drag applied to a space crew's linear velocity. Higher values bleed off drift speed faster.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.AngularDamping: Viscous drag applied to a space crew's spin. Higher values stop rotation faster.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.DesiredVelocityPerDistance: Target flying speed per tile of remaining distance. A crew in space aims for a speed of distance times this value, so it naturally slows on approach. Floored by [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.MinDesiredVelocity]].
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.MinDesiredVelocity: Lower bound, in tiles per second, on a flying crew's target speed so it never fully stalls short of its destination.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.MinDesiredVelocityEnteringAirlock: Minimum target speed used instead of [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.MinDesiredVelocity]] while a crew is flying into an airlock, so it does not crawl on final approach.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.MinDesiredVelocityGrabbingNugget: Minimum target speed used instead of [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.MinDesiredVelocity]] while a crew is flying to grab a resource nugget.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SpaceDestinationArriveDistance: How close, in tiles, a crew flying to a point in space must get for the destination to count as reached.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SpaceDestinationArriveAngle: Maximum facing error at which a space destination that requires a specific rotation counts as reached.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.AirlockArriveDistance: Distance, in tiles, from an airlock's enter/exit point at which a crew flying in space boards the ship.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.AirlockExitOpenTime: Seconds an airlock is held open when a crew exits through it into space.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.AirlockEnterOpenTime: Seconds an airlock is held open when a crew enters it from space.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.AirlockExitTime: Seconds a crew waits inside an airlock before stepping out into space.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.OxygenSupply: Seconds of oxygen a crew's suit holds. In space the oxygen meter drains from full (1) to empty over this time, and the crew dies when it runs out.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.OxygenWarningLevel: Fraction (`0..1`) of remaining oxygen below which the [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.OxygenWarningSprite]] indicator shows and the crew tries to return to a ship to refill.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SalvageDistance: How close, in tiles, a crew in space must get to the spot it is salvaging before it counts as arrived and starts working.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ConstructDistance: How close, in tiles, a crew in space must get to the part it is constructing or repairing before it counts as arrived and starts working.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.WorkPerSecond: Rate at which a single crew performs construction and repair work, passed as the work-per-second rate to a part's construction and repair trackers.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ManipulatorBeamDistToAsteroid: Distance to its destination below which a crew salvaging an asteroid is no longer assisted by manipulator beams, unless it is still moving faster than [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ManipulatorBeamVelocityToAsteroid]].
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ManipulatorBeamVelocityToAsteroid: Speed above which manipulator beams may still assist a crew that is already within [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ManipulatorBeamDistToAsteroid]] of the asteroid it is salvaging.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.AvoidableDoodadTags: Tags of doodads (such as the sun) whose hazard areas crew refuse to work in. Salvage, resource-transfer, repair, construction and door-deconstruction jobs are not assigned when the target or the crew's ship lies inside such an area.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SalvageHitEffects: Hit effects applied to the part a crew is salvaging or deconstructing, once every [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SalvageHitEffectsInterval]]. Vanilla uses damage effects with a salvage damage type to break the part down.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SalvageHitEffectsInterval: Seconds between applications of [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SalvageHitEffects]] while crew work a salvage job.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.OxygenWarningSprite: Indicator sprite drawn over a crew in space whose oxygen has dropped below [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.OxygenWarningLevel]].
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.DistanceWarningSprite: Indicator sprite drawn over a crew that has strayed beyond [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.LoseHomeShipWarningDistance]] from its home ship.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.AtlasTextureParams: Parameters, such as mip levels, for the runtime texture atlas the game builds from all crew body, suit and resource-nugget sprites.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ShipCrewAtlasMaterial: Material used to draw crew walking inside ships and crew portraits in the GUI. Its texture is replaced at load with the generated crew atlas.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SpaceCrewAtlasMaterial: Material used to draw crew flying in space with scene lighting. Receives the crew atlas as its texture and the generated normal-map atlases as shader constants.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SpaceCrewNormalsAtlasMaterial: Material used to render space crew into the simulation's normals render target for lighting.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SpaceCrewDiffuseAtlasMaterial: Material used to render space crew into the simulation's diffuse render target. Vanilla reuses [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ShipCrewAtlasMaterial]] for this.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.BodyTypes: The body appearance variants crew can have. Each crew member is assigned one by a random seed and uses its walk, carry, operate and idle animations and its skin and hair color palettes.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SuitTypes: The spacesuit appearance variants. Each crew member is assigned one by a random seed and uses its `FlySprite` while flying in space.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.LostInSpaceDeathMediaEffects: Effects played at a crew's location when it dies in space, for example when its oxygen runs out or it is shot down.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.FireDeathMediaEffects: Effects played at a crew's location on the ship when it is killed by fire.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.Jets: Thruster jets on the spacesuit. Each jet anchors its `MediaEffects` at a location and rotation on the crew and plays them continuously in space, with intensity based on how hard the crew accelerates against the jet's direction.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ConstructionBeams: Beam effects drawn from a crew in space to the part it is constructing or repairing. Each entry positions one beam by location and rotation, with optional length and angle oscillation, its beam effects, and optional hit effects at the target.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.SalvageBeams: Beam effects drawn from a crew in space to the part it is salvaging. Same structure as [[Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.ConstructionBeams]].
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.CrewIconShader: Shader applied to each body type's crew icon sprite, which is cut from a frame of its walk animation, so skin, hair and shirt colors can be tinted in crew UI.
Cosmoteer.Crew.CrewRules.NameGenerator: Generator used to create random crew names, both for newly created crew and for the randomize button in the crew edit window.

Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.EffectBuckets: Defines the named buckets that media effects render into, listed as `LowerBuckets`, `InteriorSurfaceBuckets`, `MiddleBuckets`, `SurfaceBuckets` and `UpperBuckets` in draw order. Media effects reference a bucket by name to control their render order.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.LitSpriteShader: The shader used to render sprites that are affected by simulation lighting. Lit bullet sprites use it unless they specify their own shader.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.SpriteNormalsShader: The shader used to render sprites' normal maps for lighting. Bullet sprites use it as their default normals shader.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.LightBufferShader: The shader set while drawing lights into the simulation's light buffer during ship and scene rendering.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.IndicatorMaterial: The material used to render indicator sprites.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Widgets: The templates and shared styling for the game's GUI widgets. Vanilla points this at `gui/widgets.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Buffs: The buff types available to the game, keyed by their ID. Parts refer to these IDs when providing or receiving buffs. Vanilla points this at `buffs/buffs.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Statuses: The collection of ship status types together with data shared by all of them. Vanilla points this at `statuses/statuses.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Resources: All resource types in the game, each a [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules]] entry. Resource IDs must be unique across the list. Vanilla points this at `resources/resources.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.FtlFuelResource: The ID of the resource used as FTL jump fuel. Its icon is inserted into texts that show FTL fuel amounts. Vanilla sets `hyperium`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Nuggets: Global rules for resource nuggets, including the `Rarities` table that [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.Rarity]] refers to.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Nebulas: Rules that apply to all nebula types. Vanilla points this at `nebulas/nebulas.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Ships: The rule sets for each kind of ship in the game, referenced elsewhere by their IDs. Vanilla defines `Terran`, `Asteroid` and `Megaroid`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Doodads: All doodad types that can appear in the simulation, referenced elsewhere by their IDs. Vanilla points this at `doodads/doodads.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Factions: The rule sets of all factions in the game, referenced elsewhere by their IDs. Vanilla points this at `factions/factions.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.BuiltInShipsFolder: Path of the folder holding the game's built-in ship design files. It backs the Built-In ship library. Vanilla sets `builtin_ships`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Crew: Global rules for ship crew, including suit types, jets and crew death effects. Vanilla points this at `crew/crew.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Roles: Rules about crew roles. Vanilla configures the default and new role colors and the allowed role color ranges here.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Commands: Rules governing ship commands. Vanilla points this at `commands/commands.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Formations: The fleet formations built into the game, as opposed to player-created ones. Each entry has a display name key and an icon.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.CustomFormations: The slots available for player-created custom formations, one entry per slot.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Game: General game-level rules. Vanilla configures the in-game GUI, the backgrounds, map transition timing and the maximum FTL fuel purchase here.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Simulation: General simulation rules. Vanilla configures the simulation GUI, physics update rate, camera behavior, the build grid display and the selectable game speeds here.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.GalaxyMap: Rules for the galaxy map. Vanilla points this at `galaxy_map/galaxy_map.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.CareerMode: Rules for the main career mode. Vanilla points this at `modes/career/career.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.CreativeMode: Rules for the creative mode. Vanilla points this at `modes/creative/creative.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.EliminationMode: Rules for the multiplayer elimination mode. Vanilla points this at `modes/pvp/pvp_elimination/pvp_elimination.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.ArenaMode: Rules for the multiplayer arena mode. Vanilla points this at `modes/pvp/pvp_arena/pvp_arena.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.DominationMode: Rules for the multiplayer domination mode. Vanilla points this at `modes/pvp/pvp_domination/pvp_domination.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.BuildBattleMode: Rules for the multiplayer Build and Battle mode. Vanilla points this at `modes/pvp/pvp_buildbattle/pvp_buildbattle.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Codex: Rules for the game's codex. Vanilla points this at `codex/codex.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.ShipAIs: The ship AI rule sets available to the game, keyed by their ID. Vanilla points this at `ai/ai.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.BuiltinShips: The database of built-in ships that can be spawned by the game. Vanilla points this at `builtin_ships/builtins.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Planets: General rules for all planets. Vanilla points this at `planets/planets.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Menus: General rules for the game's menus. Vanilla points this at `gui/menus.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.TextSprites: Named sprites registered with the text system at load. Each entry can then be inserted into any game text with `` markup, using the map key as the image name.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.PostShaders: The full-screen post-processing shader options offered in the graphics settings. The first entry is used when no valid option is selected.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.Music: Rules for the game's music. Vanilla points this at `music/music.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.DecalLayers: The number of decal layers every ship has for painting. The paint toolbox lists one entry per layer. Vanilla uses `3`.

Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.BackgroundStyleID: The background style rendered behind the galaxy map, referenced by ID from the game's background styles. Vanilla uses `bg_galaxy`.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.Camera: Camera settings for the galaxy map view, such as the zoom limits and the pixel-to-world scale factor. Vanilla inherits the simulation camera and tightens the zoom range.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.TargetNodeFocusSpeed: Speed factor for the camera pan to the newly arrived node when traveling without the FTL flight animation. The pan speed scales with the remaining distance, so the camera eases in as it approaches.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeSprite: The sprite drawn for each node on the galaxy map. Nodes the game mode reports as unvisited use [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeUnvisitedSprite]] instead.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeUnvisitedSprite: The sprite drawn for map nodes the player has not yet visited, replacing [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeSprite]]. Vanilla tints it gray.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeSpriteRotSpeed: Continuous spin rate of each node sprite, in degrees per second. Every node starts at a random rotation offset so they don't spin in unison.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeNameFontSize: Font size of the name label drawn next to each map node.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeNameFadeRange: Camera zoom scales between which node name labels fade in. At the first value the label is invisible and at the second it is fully opaque. Mousing over a node always fades its label in regardless of zoom.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeNameWorldOffset: Offset of the name label from the node's center, in galaxy map world units. Rotates with the camera.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeNamePixelOffset: Additional offset of the name label in screen pixels, applied after [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeNameWorldOffset]] and scaled by the UI scale.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeNameMouseOverLerpTime: Seconds for a node's name label to blend to its moused-over state (full opacity and fixed screen size) when the cursor enters the node, and back when it leaves.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeClickRadius: Distance from a node's center within which the mouse cursor counts as over that node, in galaxy map world units. The effective radius is half this value and grows with the square root of the camera zoom.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeFtlPulseSprite: The pulse sprite that expands from every jumpable node while the player is choosing an FTL jump destination.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeFtlPulseInterval: Seconds between successive FTL pulses on jumpable nodes. Each pulse animates through [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeFtlPulseScaleRange]] and [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeFtlPulseAlphaRange]] over this time.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeFtlPulseScaleRange: Start and end scale multipliers applied to [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeFtlPulseSprite]] over each pulse. Vanilla grows the pulse from 1 to 3 times its size.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.NodeFtlPulseAlphaRange: Start and end opacity multipliers applied to the pulse sprite's own alpha over each pulse. Vanilla fades the pulse from fully visible to invisible.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.ConnectionLine: The line drawn between every pair of connected map nodes. Its texture repeat is scaled with the camera zoom so the pattern keeps a consistent on-screen density.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.ConnectionLinePulseSpeed: Speed at which the connection line's texture scrolls along the line, creating a slow pulsing movement. Higher values scroll faster.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.ConnectionClickDistance: Distance from a connection line within which the mouse cursor counts as over that connection, in galaxy map world units. Grows with the square root of the camera zoom.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.CourseLine: The line drawn along the plotted FTL course from the current node to the selected destination, one segment per hop of the path.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.CourseLinePulseSpeed: Speed at which the course line's texture scrolls, making the course appear to flow. Higher values scroll faster.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.CurNodeSprite: The marker sprite drawn around the node the player currently occupies.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.UnscaledCurNodeWorldSize: Base size of the current-node marker in galaxy map world units, before the pulse scale and camera scaling are applied.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.CurNodePulseInterval: Seconds per pulse cycle of the current-node marker as it oscillates between its begin and end scale and color.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.CurNodeBeginScale: Scale multiplier of the current-node marker at one end of its pulse. The marker oscillates between this and [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.CurNodeEndScale]].
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.CurNodeEndScale: Scale multiplier of the current-node marker at the other end of its pulse. See [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.CurNodeBeginScale]].
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.CurNodeBeginColor: Color of the current-node marker at one end of its pulse. The marker blends between this and [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.CurNodeEndColor]].
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.CurNodeEndColor: Color of the current-node marker at the other end of its pulse. See [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.CurNodeBeginColor]].
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeSprite: The marker sprite drawn around the node currently set as the FTL travel destination.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.UnscaledDestNodeWorldSize: Base size of the destination marker in galaxy map world units, before the pulse scale and camera scaling are applied.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodePulseInterval: Seconds per pulse cycle of the destination marker as it oscillates between its begin and end scale and color.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeBeginScale: Scale multiplier of the destination marker at one end of its pulse. The marker oscillates between this and [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeEndScale]].
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeEndScale: Scale multiplier of the destination marker at the other end of its pulse. See [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeBeginScale]].
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeBeginColor: Color of the destination marker at one end of its pulse. The marker blends between this and [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeEndColor]].
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeEndColor: Color of the destination marker at the other end of its pulse. See [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeBeginColor]].
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeFromTime: Seconds of the arrival animation played when a new destination is selected. Over this time the marker eases from [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeFromColor]], [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeFromScale]] and [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeFromRot]] to its normal appearance.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeFromColor: Tint the destination marker starts from when a new destination is selected, blending to untinted white over [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeFromTime]].
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeFromScale: Scale multiplier the destination marker starts from when a new destination is selected, easing to 1 over [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeFromTime]]. Vanilla starts at 3 times normal size.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeFromRot: Rotation offset, in degrees, the destination marker starts from when a new destination is selected, easing to 0 over [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.DestNodeFromTime]]. Vanilla's 360 spins the marker in with one full turn.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.FtlShip: The ship icon that flies along the jump path on the galaxy map during the FTL travel animation. The camera follows it from node to node.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.FtlShipSpeed: Speed of the FTL ship icon along the jump path, in galaxy map units per second.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.FactionBorderSprite: The sprite used to render the borders around faction territories. Only its height matters and sets the border thickness. It is tinted with each faction's [[Cosmoteer.Factions.FactionRules.BorderColor]].
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.FactionBorderRadius: Distance from each faction-controlled node, in galaxy map units, at which its territory border is drawn. Borders between adjacent territories run along the intersection of these circles.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.FactionBorderImaginaryNodeDistance: Distance, in galaxy map units, at which invisible helper nodes are placed in eight directions around each node where no real node is nearby. They push the border inward around nodes on the perimeter of a territory.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.FactionMaxMissingBorderAngle: Largest angular gap, in degrees, allowed between adjacent border segments around a node. Wider gaps are bridged with extra arc segments at [[Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.FactionBorderRadius]].
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.FactionBorderPointUnifyTolerance: Distance, in galaxy map units, within which nearby border points are merged into one so segments from neighboring nodes join up cleanly.
Cosmoteer.GalaxyMap.MapRules.FactionCurveSegmentsPerDistance: Number of straight segments used per map unit of length when tessellating the smoothed border curves. Higher values make rounder borders.

Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.MoneyTickUpSound: Looping sound played while the money counter in the HUD is animating upward toward the player's actual funds. The loop stops when the displayed amount catches up.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.MoneyTickDownSound: Looping sound played while the money counter in the HUD is animating downward toward the player's actual funds. The loop stops when the displayed amount catches up.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.FameTickUpSound: Looping sound played while the fame counter in the HUD is animating upward. The duration of its first sound also scales how long the count-up animation takes.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.FameTickDownSound: Looping sound played while the fame counter in the HUD is animating downward. The duration of its first sound also scales how long the count-down animation takes.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.Colors: Customizable allegiance color rules for the gameplay UI, including the default player, enemy, ally, and neutral colors and the colorblind presets offered in the accessibility settings. The defaults are used whenever the player has not chosen a custom color.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ObjectIndicators: Rules for the indicator icons drawn over ships and map objects, covering off-screen edge arrows, pulse and ping effects, ship dot sprites per allegiance, FTL point and gate icons, and the ship icon glow rendering.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.Build: Rules for the ship build and repair GUI, including the part editor groups, blueprint overlay sprites, and the error flash settings for the money and resource displays.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.Paint: Rules for the ship painting GUI, covering the paint toolbox, decal editing states, and the paint scheme dialog.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.Brushes: Brush shape entries for the brush menu shared by the build and paint toolboxes. Each inner list forms one group of brush buttons and a separator is drawn between groups, with every entry defining a brush shape, name, icon, tooltip, and optional default hotkey.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.Crew: Rules for the crew management GUI, including the crew toolbox, roster and role edit windows, crew role sprites and drag lines, and related sounds.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.Resources: Rules for the resource management GUI, covering the resources toolbox, resource transfer lines and animations, supply and consumption toggle buttons, storage grid rendering, and related sounds.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.Minimap: Rules for the minimap widget, including the friendly, enemy, and neutral pip sprites drawn for ships and other objects.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.PartNetworks: Rules for the part network overlay drawn in blueprint mode, defining the route lines, network port and hub icons, and the material and atlas parameters used to render them.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.PartStats: Ordered list of part statistic definitions used to build part tooltips. Each entry pairs a stat ID with a localization format key and optionally a built-in stat that computes a default value from the part itself, and stats appear in the tooltip in this list's order.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.PartFeatures: Maps each part feature ID to the localization key of its display name, used when a part tooltip lists the part's features. Feature IDs without an entry fall back to showing the raw ID.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DeveloperToolbar: Rules for the developer mode toolbar, currently just the icon that opens it.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CloseHudButtonIcon: Close icon overlaid on HUD toggle buttons while their panel is open, including the toolbox mode buttons and the galaxy map button. Also used by the close button of an expanded part group in the build toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.RoofToggleOnIcon: Icon of the interior toggle button on the ships card and parts card while ship roofs are shown and interiors are hidden.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.RoofToggleOffIcon: Icon of the interior toggle button on the ships card and parts card while ship interiors are shown and roofs are hidden.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.FormationIcon: Icon of the formations popout button in the ships card command box, which opens the formations panel when multiple ships are selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CancelFormationIcon: Icon of the cancel button in the formations popout panel, active while a selected ship is currently flying in a formation.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DirectControlOnIcon: Icon of the direct control toggle button on the ships card while direct control mode is active.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DirectControlOffIcon: Icon of the direct control toggle button on the ships card while direct control mode is inactive. Also shown with a distance value in the ship info area while a direct control command is in effect.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.MoveIcon: Icon of the move command button in the ships card command box, which enters the movement ordering mode for the selected ships.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.RotateIcon: Icon of the rotate command button in the ships card command box, which enters the rotation ordering mode for the selected ships.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.StrafeIcon: Icon of the strafe command button in the ships card command box, which enters the strafe ordering mode for the selected ships.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.AttackIcon: Icon of the attack command button in the ships card command box, which enters the attack ordering mode for the selected ships.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.SalvageIcon: Icon of the salvage command button in the ships card command box, which enters the salvage selection mode.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.FtlJumpIcon: Icon of the FTL jump command button on the ships card and of the plot course button on the galaxy map.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.HaltIcon: Icon of the halt button in the ships card command box, which cancels the current orders of the selected ships.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.SummonCrewIcon: Icon of the summon crew button in the ships card command box, which recalls crew for each selected ship.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.SpeedometerBacking: Background dial of the speedometer shown in the ships card info area when a single ship is selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.SpeedometerNeedle: Needle of the speedometer in the ships card info area, rotated to indicate the selected ship's current speed.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.SpeedometerNeedleSpeedRange: Speed range mapped onto the needle sweep defined by [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.SpeedometerNeedleAngleRange]]. The ship's current speed is converted to a normalized position within this range, and speeds outside it extrapolate linearly rather than clamping.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.SpeedometerNeedleAngleRange: Rotation range of the speedometer needle. The minimum angle corresponds to the minimum of [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.SpeedometerNeedleSpeedRange]] and the maximum angle to its maximum, and speeds outside that range rotate the needle linearly past these angles.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CrewEvaIcon: Indicator icon in the ships card info area shown with a count while the selected ship has crew outside the ship and no construction is queued.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.UnderConstructionIcon: Indicator icon in the ships card info area shown while the selected ship has queued construction that is proceeding normally.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ConstructionBlockedIcon: Indicator icon in the ships card info area shown while the selected ship has queued construction that is blocked.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.NoControlIcon: Indicator icon in the ships card info area shown while the selected ship cannot execute commands, either because it is junk or because its command point usage exceeds the points provided.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.NewShipIcon: Icon of the new ship button in the top menu bar, available in game modes that allow creating a ship from scratch, which enters the new ship placement mode.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.MoveCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the move command mode is active and the player is choosing a destination for the selected ships.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.StrafeCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the strafe command mode is active.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.RotateCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the rotate command mode is active.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.AttackCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the attack command mode is active.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.TargetCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the weapon targeting mode is active, and when hovering a part that the selected weapons can target, including in the miniview.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DirectControlAimCursor: Mouse cursor shown while directly controlling a ship with weapon fire enabled, used for aiming the ship's weapons.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DirectControlAimDisabledCursor: Mouse cursor shown while directly controlling a ship with weapon fire disabled.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DirectControlMaxTargetDistance: Maximum distance from the mouse cursor at which a ship can become the direct control weapon target, multiplied by the camera's current uniform scale so the pickup radius follows the zoom level. Ships beyond this distance are not targeted and weapons aim at the raw cursor position.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DirectControlSoftTargetCircle: Circle drawn in direct control mode around the closest potential target ship near the cursor while no target is locked. It is tinted with the enemy color when the ship can be damaged and the neutral color otherwise. See [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DirectControlHardTargetCircle]] for the locked variant.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DirectControlHardTargetCircle: Circle drawn in direct control mode around the ship locked as the target with the lock target input, always tinted with the enemy color. Unlike the soft target ring of [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DirectControlSoftTargetCircle]], this target persists until unlocked, destroyed, or out of sight.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DirectControlHardTargetSound: Sound played in direct control mode when the player toggles the target lock, both when locking onto the closest ship as the hard target and when releasing an existing hard target. The locked target is marked with [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DirectControlHardTargetCircle]].
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CancelPartSelectionIcon: Icon of the cancel button in the ship GUI tool button row, which clears the current part selection. Temporarily replaced by [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ErrorFlashSprite]] during an error flash.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.TargetIcon: Icon of the target command button in the parts card command box, which enters the weapon targeting mode for the selected weapons.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CancelTargetIcon: Icon of the cancel targets button in the parts card command box, which clears the explicit targets of the selected weapons.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.AutoThrustOffIcon: Icon of the auto thrust toggle button in the parts card command box while auto thrust is disabled for the selected thrusters.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.AutoThrustOnIcon: Icon of the auto thrust toggle button in the parts card command box while auto thrust is enabled for the selected thrusters.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.MenuIcon: Icon of the menu button in the top left menu bar, which opens the in game menu.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.GalaxyMapIcon: Icon of the galaxy map toggle button in the top left menu bar, which shows and hides the galaxy map. While the map is open the button is overlaid with [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CloseHudButtonIcon]].
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.PauseSprite: Icon of the pause button on the game speed bar at the top of the screen, which pauses the simulation.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.UnpauseSprite: Icon of the unpause button on the game speed bar, shown in place of the pause button while the simulation is paused.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.IncreaseSpeedSprite: Icon of the increase speed button on the game speed bar, which steps the simulation speed up to the next available game speed.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DecreaseSpeedSprite: Icon of the decrease speed button on the game speed bar, which steps the simulation speed down to the next available game speed.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.PartTriggers: Button definitions for player-activated part triggers shown on the command card, one button per entry. Parts reference an entry by its TriggerID through their UI trigger rules, and each trigger also gets a bindable hotkey.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.PartTargeters: Button definitions for part targeting commands shown on the command card, each creating a target selection button and optionally a matching cancel button. Parts reference an entry by its TargeterID.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.PartToggles: Button definitions for part settings that cycle between multiple choices, shown on the command card and used while placing parts in build mode. Parts reference an entry by its ToggleID and each choice maps to a part mode number.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.PartColors: Button definitions for part color settings chosen through a color picker, shown on the command card and applied while placing parts. Parts reference an entry by its ColorID.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.PartHighlights: Fill and border colors for tile highlight overlays drawn over parts, with the fill alpha scaled by each cell's highlight intensity. At load the game adds an extra entry for every buff type that defines a RectFillColor, keyed by that buff's ID.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ErrorSound: Sound played when a player action fails, such as invalid part placement, an FTL jump that cannot be plotted, or a crew or resource transfer that cannot be completed. It does not overlap itself if errors repeat quickly.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ErrorFlashCount: Number of times the ship card's cancel button flashes to [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ErrorFlashSprite]] when an action is rejected, with each on and off phase lasting [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ErrorFlashInterval]] seconds.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ErrorFlashInterval: Duration in seconds of each half of a cancel button error flash, first while [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ErrorFlashSprite]] is shown and then while the normal icon is restored, repeated [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ErrorFlashCount]] times.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ErrorFlashSprite: Error sprite flashed onto the cancel part selection button when a command is issued at an invalid location while parts are selected. It alternates with [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CancelPartSelectionIcon]] according to [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ErrorFlashCount]] and [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ErrorFlashInterval]].
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.PlotCourseLocalSound: Sound played when the player plots a local FTL jump, after jump commands are issued to the selected ships.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.PlotCourseGalaxySound: Sound played as local feedback when the player sets a destination node on the galaxy map.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.SummonCrewSound: Sound played when the player summons all crew back to a ship, which cancels its off-ship transfer, salvage, and queued construction jobs. Summoning for several ships at once does not overlap the sound.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ShipLogoColorFactor: Multiplicative color factor applied on top of a ship icon button's own tint when rendering the ship logo. In vanilla it mainly reduces the logo's alpha.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ShipLogoRedprintsColor: Color used to draw the destroyed part redprint ghost on ship icon buttons that show redprints, multiplied by the widget's current color scale.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ShipLogoUnselectedColorFactor: Color scale applied to a ship's button in the HUD ships list while that ship is not selected, while selected ships render at plain white. The vanilla value fades unselected logos to half opacity.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.FtlPointColor: Indicator color for discovered FTL point landmarks that are not hostile to the local player, overriding the default allegiance-based indicator color.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.FtlJumpCursor: Mouse cursor shown while plotting an FTL jump whenever the hovered destination is valid and the jump's fuel cost can be afforded. An invalid destination or unaffordable jump shows the generic invalid cursor instead.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.FtlJumpLine: Line drawn from each jumping ship to the hovered FTL destination while plotting a jump. It is shown fully opaque when the destination is valid and at half opacity while pointing at the raw mouse position.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.FtlJumpLinePulseSpeed: Scroll speed of the [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.FtlJumpLine]] texture, advanced with game time and scaled by the camera zoom so the pulses appear to travel along the line toward the destination.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.MPPlayerColors: Pool of colors assigned to multiplayer players. The color order is shuffled once per game from the match's random seed and each player receives the color at their player index, wrapping around when there are more players than colors.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ShipHiddenSound: Sound played when a selected ship becomes hidden to outside observers, such as when it enters a nebula that blocks sight.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ShipRevealedSound: Sound played when a selected ship that was hidden to outside observers becomes visible again, such as when it leaves a sight-blocking nebula.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.LaunchGameSound: Sound played once when a game session starts, at the moment the game is pushed onto the application state stack.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.TimePauseSound: Sound played when the game becomes paused. It only plays if the pause state actually changes.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.TimeContinueSound: Sound played when the game resumes from being paused. It only plays if the pause state actually changes.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.OpenBuildRepairMenuSound: Plays when the build and repair toolbox opens.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.OpenPaintMenuSound: Plays when the paint toolbox opens.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.OpenCrewMenuSound: Plays when the crew toolbox opens.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.OpenResourcesMenuSound: Plays when the resources toolbox opens.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.OpenCommsMenuSound: Plays when the comms toolbox opens.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CloseCommsMenuSound: Plays when the comms toolbox closes.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CloseNonCommsMenuSound: Plays when the build, paint, crew, or resources toolbox closes. The comms toolbox uses [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CloseCommsMenuSound]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CopySound: Plays when the player copies the selected parts in build mode or the selected decals in paint mode.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CutSound: Plays when the player cuts selected parts or decals, or grabs a part or decal off the ship with the grab tool.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.PasteSound: Plays when the player enters paste mode with copied parts in build mode or copied decals in paint mode. Not played when paste mode is entered as part of a cut.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.StartSalvageSelectSound: Plays when the salvage command button is selected and salvage target selection begins.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.StopSalvageSelectSound: Plays when the salvage command button is clicked while already selected, ending salvage target selection.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.DeletePartInBinSound: Plays when the player drops a grabbed part into the trashcan button or deletes the selected parts or decals with the delete button.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ShowShipInteriorSound: Plays when the interior toggle button switches to showing ship interiors.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.HideShipInteriorSound: Plays when the interior toggle button switches back to showing ship roofs.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.StartTradeSound: Plays when the player confirms a trade in the comms trade tab and the resource transfer starts successfully. On failure [[Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ErrorSound]] plays instead.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.ShowGalaxyMapSound: Plays when the galaxy map toggle button is selected and the galaxy map opens.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.HideGalaxyMapSound: Plays when the galaxy map toggle button is deselected and the galaxy map closes.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.AllCrewInsideSound: Plays when the last crew member returns inside a selected player ship so that no crew remain outside. Suppressed while an enemy ship is nearby.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.FirstCrewLeftSound: Plays when the first crew member leaves a selected player ship while all other crew are still inside. Suppressed while an enemy ship is nearby.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.CrewDistanceWarningSound: Plays when a crew member of the local player first strays far enough from its home ship to get a distance warning indicator. Only sounds for the first crew member currently warned, not for each additional one.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.BlueprintUnlockedSound: Plays when the player buys a technology in the comms techs tab or unlocks a technology in Build Battle mode.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.GenericPutdownSound: Plays when a Creative Mode tool places something in the simulation, such as spawning a ship, editing nebula cells, or placing a planet, crew, or point-of-interest doodad.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.AmbientTradeLoopSound: Loops continuously while a comms window with a missions tab is open, providing ambient sound for the station. Stops when the window closes or the missions tab is no longer available.
Cosmoteer.Game.GameGuiRules.SeenShipPingDuration: Duration in seconds of simulation time that a ship is remembered as already seen after its indicator disappears. If the ship is spotted again within this window its indicator ping does not trigger the automatic game slowdown.

Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Brushes.BlockTileBrus.Size: The size of the brush rectangle.

Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.EditShipToolIcon: Icon shown on the build-mode toggle button in the ship HUD.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.EditorGroups: Catalog of the build-menu category groups (name, icon, sort order) that parts and doors are filed into, keyed by group ID. Must also contain the groups `__favorites0` and up that back the favorite tabs counted by [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.FavoriteGroups]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ColorReplaceShader: Shader that redraws a part icon as a flat silhouette in [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.StackedPartGroupGlowColor]], forming the base for the glow behind stacked-part group icons in the build toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.StackedPartGroupGlowShader: Directional blur shader run in repeated horizontal and vertical passes to spread the silhouette from [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ColorReplaceShader]] into a glow around stacked-part group icons.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.StackedPartGroupGlowColor: Tint of the glow drawn behind stacked-part group icons in the build toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.StackedPartGroupGlowIterations: Number of blur passes for the stacked-part group glow, expressed as a fraction of the icon's larger pixel dimension. More passes widen and smooth the glow.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.StackedPartGroupGlowDisplacement: Offset in pixels that each blur pass of the stacked-part group glow samples at, controlling how far the glow spreads per pass.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.StackedPartGroupIconScale: Fraction from 0 to 1 of the group icon's render area that the part icon itself fills when centered. The remaining margin leaves room for the glow.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.StatsGui: Rules for the ship-stats panel in the build toolbox, supplying the stat-bar sprites, segment sprite, color gradients and default label of each stat bar.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MirrorLine: Rules for the draggable mirror centerline overlay, supplying its line color, handle sprite and handle size. Shared by the build, crew, paint and resources toolboxes.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ErrorFlashCount: Number of on/off flash cycles played when signalling an invalid action, such as an illegal part or door placement or insufficient money or resources. Each half cycle lasts [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ErrorFlashInterval]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ErrorFlashInterval: Duration in seconds of each half of an error flash cycle. Pairs with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ErrorFlashCount]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintUnderlayColor: Tint of the translucent full-screen underlay shown behind the build menu while editing blueprints.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.AutoDoorValidColor: Tint of automatically placed door previews that would be validly added while dragging parts. Invalid door locations use [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.AutoDoorInvalidColor]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.AutoDoorInvalidColor: Tint of automatically placed door previews at invalid locations while the placement as a whole is still valid.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.AutoDoorInvalidCursorColor: Tint applied to all auto-door previews when the placement under the cursor is itself invalid.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.LegalDoorLocationColor1: First endpoint of the pulsing highlight drawn over every legal door location while the door tool is active. The highlight fades between this and [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.LegalDoorLocationColor2]] with a period of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.LegalDoorLocationInterval]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.LegalDoorLocationColor2: Second endpoint color of the legal door location pulse. In the vanilla data it differs from [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.LegalDoorLocationColor1]] only in alpha, making the pulse a fade.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.LegalDoorLocationInterval: Period in seconds of one pulse of the legal door location highlight.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintsOffSprite: Icon shown on the blueprint-mode toggle button in the build toolbox while blueprint editing is off. Swapped for [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintsOnSprite]] when the toggle is on.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintsOnSprite: Icon shown on the blueprint-mode toggle button in the build toolbox while blueprint editing is on. Counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintsOffSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RemovePartSprite: Icon for the remove-part tool button in the build toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.GrabPartSprite: Icon for the grab-part tool button in the build toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.SelectPartsSprite: Icon for the select-parts tool button in the build toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RepairToolSprite: Icon for the repair tool button in the build toolbox. Also layered onto the main build button in the ship HUD to signal repair mode.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ConstructionInProgressSprite: Overlay sprite layered onto the main build button in the ship HUD while construction is underway.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MirrorOffSprite: Icon shown on the mirror-mode toggle button in the build toolbox while mirroring is off. Swapped for [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MirrorOnSprite]] when mirroring is enabled.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MirrorOnSprite: Icon shown on the mirror-mode toggle button in the build toolbox while mirroring is enabled. Counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MirrorOffSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RemovePartNineSlice: Frame drawn over each blueprint part or door the remove tool will delete when clicked. Drawn together with the X from [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RemovePartXLine]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RemovePartXLine: Line style of the X drawn diagonally across a part slated for removal. Its thickness scales with the square root of the camera zoom.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RemovePartXLineRectDeflate: Distance in tiles by which the part's rectangle is shrunk on every side before the removal X from [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RemovePartXLine]] is drawn, insetting the X from the part edges.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.UnRemovePartNineSlice: Frame drawn over already-removed parts or doors that the remove tool would restore instead of delete.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.GrabPartNineSlice: Frame drawn over the blueprint part or door under the cursor that the grab tool will pick up, including its mirrored counterparts.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.GrabPartCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the grab-part tool is active. Also used by the select-parts tool while it is grabbing a selection.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RemovePartCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the remove-part tool is active.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.SelectPartsCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the select-parts tool is active and not grabbing a selection.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RepairCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the repair tool is active.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.SelectedPartNineSlice: Frame drawn over each currently selected part in the select-parts tool.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.SelectedMirroredPartNineSlice: Frame drawn over the mirrored counterparts of selected parts that are not themselves directly selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.TentativeSelectedPartNineSlice: Frame drawn over parts inside the current drag-selection rectangle that are not yet committed to the selection.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.TentativeSelectedMirroredPartNineSlice: Frame drawn over the mirrored counterparts of tentatively selected parts that are not otherwise in the tentative selection.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RepairPartNineSlice: Frame drawn over the destroyed or damaged part or door under the cursor that the repair tool will act on.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.PartSelectionOutlineSprite: Border of the drag-selection rectangle in the select-parts tool, drawn as four edge segments around [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.PartSelectionFillSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.PartSelectionFillSprite: Fill of the drag-selection rectangle interior in the select-parts tool. Drawn under the [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.PartSelectionOutlineSprite]] border.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ProhibitedZoneSprite: Sprite tiled over each ship cell where a part cannot be placed, shown while hovering a placement and when flashing an invalid part's prohibited zone.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.DeletedTileSprite: Marker drawn over a ship cell whose physical part has been deleted from the blueprint, shown only when viewing pending blueprint changes. Also textures the deleted-door markers sized by [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.DeletedHorizontalDoorSpriteSize]] and [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.DeletedVerticalDoorSpriteSize]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.DeletedHorizontalDoorSpriteSize: Width and height in tiles of the deleted marker drawn over a removed horizontal door location. Uses the [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.DeletedTileSprite]] texture.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.DeletedVerticalDoorSpriteSize: Width and height in tiles of the deleted marker drawn over a removed vertical door location, the transpose of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.DeletedHorizontalDoorSpriteSize]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ConstructionBlockedSprite: Icon drawn centered on each queued part whose construction is currently blocked.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RotateLeftSprite: Icon for the rotate-left button on the context toolbar shown while placing parts.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RotateRightSprite: Icon for the rotate-right button on the context toolbar shown while placing parts.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.FlipHorizontalSprite: Icon for the flip-horizontal button on the context toolbar shown while placing parts.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.FlipVerticalSprite: Icon for the flip-vertical button on the context toolbar shown while placing parts.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.TrashcanSprite: Icon for the delete button shown while a part is held with the grab tool, which discards the grabbed part.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CopyPartsSprite: Icon for the copy button on the select-parts toolbar.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CutPartsSprite: Icon for the cut button on the select-parts toolbar.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.PastePartsSprite: Icon for the paste button on the select-parts toolbar.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.DeletePartsSprite: Icon for the delete button on the select-parts toolbar.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CancelPastePartsSprite: Icon for the cancel button on the select-parts toolbar, which clears the current selection.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BuildSound: Fallback sound played when placing parts in live construction and none of the category-specific putdown sounds such as [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.SmallModulePutdownSound]] applies.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintModeOnSound: Sound played when entering blueprint editing mode.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MakeItSoSound: Sound played when committing construction with the confirm button. In crew-and-resources construction mode [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintMakeItSoSound]] plays instead.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintAddSound: Fallback sound for adding blueprint parts when no category-specific blueprint putdown sound applies. Also plays when loading, importing or recentering blueprints, and when queueing repairs in crew-and-resources construction mode.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintDeleteSound: Sound played when blueprint parts are removed, for example clearing all or invalid blueprints, undoing a blueprint-only change, or cancelling a queued repair.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintMakeItSoSound: Sound played in crew-and-resources construction mode when committing construction or repair-all, standing in for [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MakeItSoSound]] and [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RepairAllSound]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintDiscedModificationsPopupSound: Sound played when the confirmation dialog about discarding uncommitted ship modifications opens on toggling blueprint mode.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintDiscedModificationsConfirmedSou: Sound played when uncommitted blueprint changes are actually discarded, resetting the blueprints to match the physical ship.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RepairSound: Sound played when the repair tool applies an instant repair. In crew-and-resources construction mode [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintAddSound]] plays instead.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RepairAllSound: Sound played when clicking the repair-all button. In crew-and-resources construction mode [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.BlueprintMakeItSoSound]] plays instead.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CancelConstructionSound: Sound played when pressing the cancel input in build mode to reset the current tool or clear a paste or selection.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CancelAllSound: Sound played when clicking the cancel-all button, which cancels every queued construction, deconstruction and repair on the ship.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ItemFlipFirstSound: One of the two sounds alternated on each flip of a held or pasted item, starting with this one. Alternates with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ItemFlipSecondSound]] so consecutive flips produce a two-note back and forth.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ItemFlipSecondSound: The alternate flip sound, interleaved with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.ItemFlipFirstSound]] on consecutive flips.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RotateLeftSound: Sound played when rotating a held or pasted item counterclockwise.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.RotateRightSound: Sound played when rotating a held or pasted item clockwise.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MediumModuleMaxArea: Largest tile area (width times height) at which a placed part still counts as a medium module when choosing a putdown sound. Larger parts use [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.LargeModulePutdownSound]]. Pairs with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.SmallModuleMaxArea]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.SmallModuleMaxArea: Largest tile area at which a placed part counts as a small module when choosing a putdown sound. Parts above it fall through to the [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MediumModuleMaxArea]] threshold.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.SmallModulePutdownSound: Sound played when placing parts that classify as small modules, with tile area at most [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.SmallModuleMaxArea]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MediumModulePutdownSound: Sound played when placing parts that classify as medium modules, with tile area between [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.SmallModuleMaxArea]] and [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MediumModuleMaxArea]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.LargeModulePutdownSound: Sound played when placing parts with tile area above [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MediumModuleMaxArea]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.StructurePutdownSound: Sound played when placing parts in the `structure` type category.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.FloorModulePutdownSound: Sound played when placing parts in the `corridor` type category.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.DoorModulePutdownSound: Sound played when a placement adds only doors and no parts.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.SmallModuleBlueprintPutdownSound: Blueprint-mode counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.SmallModulePutdownSound]], played when small modules are added to a blueprint instead of built directly.
