Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MediumModuleBlueprintPutdownSound: Blueprint-mode counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.MediumModulePutdownSound]], played when medium modules are added to a blueprint.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.LargeModuleBlueprintPutdownSound: Blueprint-mode counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.LargeModulePutdownSound]], played when large modules are added to a blueprint.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.StructureBlueprintPutdownSound: Blueprint-mode counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.StructurePutdownSound]], played when structure parts are added to a blueprint.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.FloorModuleBlueprintPutdownSound: Blueprint-mode counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.FloorModulePutdownSound]], played when corridor parts are added to a blueprint.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.DoorModuleBlueprintPutdownSound: Blueprint-mode counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.DoorModulePutdownSound]], played when only doors are added to a blueprint.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.FtlOverlayAlpha: Opacity from 0 to 1 of every colored cell in the FTL jump-efficiency overlay drawn over the ship.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.FtlOverlayHueRange: Hue range in degrees that the FTL jump-efficiency overlay maps each cell's efficiency onto, with zero efficiency at the start of the range and full efficiency at the end. The vanilla `[0, 120]` runs from red to green. Pairs with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.FtlOverlayAlpha]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathLine: Line style of the crew-access path drawn from a placed part to its nearest crew source while the path stays shorter than [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathLineWarningDist]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathWarningLine: Line style of a crew-access path whose length reaches [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathLineWarningDist]] but not [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathLineStrongWarningDist]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathStrongWarningLine: Line style of a crew-access path whose length reaches [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathLineStrongWarningDist]], the strongest visual warning that crew are far away.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathLineWarningDist: Path length in tiles at or above which the crew-access path switches from [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathLine]] to [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathWarningLine]] and a warning is shown at the cursor.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathLineStrongWarningDist: Path length in tiles at or above which the crew-access path escalates to [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathStrongWarningLine]] and the strong-warning cursor text.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathLineUSpeed: Scroll speed of the crew-path line texture along its length, in texture U units per second, animating the line toward its destination.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CrewPathOverlapSeparation: Offset in tiles between overlapping crew-path lines. Multiple paths are fanned out by this vector, centered on the original route, so they do not draw on top of each other.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.FavoriteGroups: Number of user-favorite tabs in the build toolbox. Each tab N maps to the editor group ID `__favoritesN` counting from 0, which must exist in [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.EditorGroups]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.FavoriteSprite: Badge overlaid on a part button in the build toolbox to mark it as favorited.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.BuildGuiRules.CenterOfMassSprite: Icon drawn at the ship's center of mass in the build overlay. Scales with camera zoom and rotates with the ship.

Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.Stats.IconTextS.StatMode: The mode to use when collecting stat values. See StatCollectionMode values for semantics.

Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.Stats.StatBarMa.TooltipKey: The localization string key providing the marker format in tooltips.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.Stats.StatBarMa.Stat: The stat that the value is derived from.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.Stats.StatBarMa.StatColor: The color to use for the marker and

Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Build.Stats.StatBarRu.AdditionalStatMarkers: Additional values to display in the bar tooltip, and optionally as a RecommendedStat style marker.

Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SelectCrewCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the crew selection tool is active. Replaced by [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.AssignToSquadCursor]] when hovering a crew quarters the selected crew can be assigned to.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.AssignToSquadCursor: Mouse cursor shown when hovering a crew quarters that the currently selected crew have a path to, indicating that clicking will assign them to that quarters.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SetCrewRoleCursor: Mouse cursor shown while a role is selected in the crew toolbox and clicking a crew quarters will assign that role to it.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetToolCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the squad target tool is active. Replaced by [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetToolSelectionCursor]] once one or more crew quarters are selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetToolSelectionCursor: Mouse cursor shown in the squad target tool while crew quarters are selected, indicating that clicking a valid part will add or remove it as a supply target of those quarters.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.ToolIcon: Icon of the button in the ship toolbar that opens the crew management toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.RosterIcon: Icon of the crew toolbox button that opens the roster window listing the ship's crew.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SelectCrewIcon: Icon of the crew selection tool button in the crew toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetToolOffIcon: Icon shown on the squad target tool's toggle button while the tool is inactive. Swapped for [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetToolOnIcon]] when the tool is selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetToolOnIcon: Icon shown on the squad target tool's toggle button while the tool is active.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.ClearSquadTargetsIcon: Icon of the context button that clears all supply targets of the selected crew quarters in the squad target tool. Drawn with the disabled icon shader while none of the selected quarters have targets.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TransferCrewIcon: Icon of the crew entry in the crew and resource transfer window.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.CrewSelectionOutlineSprite: Sprite used to draw the four edges of the drag-selection rectangle when selecting crew or squads. Its color is multiplied by the current player color.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.CrewSelectionFillSprite: Sprite that fills the inside of the drag-selection rectangle. Its color is multiplied by the current player color.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.CrewSelectSound: Sound played when crew are selected, either by drag-selecting or with the select-all hotkey.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.AssignToSquadSound: Sound played when the selected crew are assigned to a crew quarters.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.ChangeRoleColorSound: Sound played when a role color change is committed in the role editor, and when a color change is undone or redone.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SetCrewRoleSound: Sound played when clicking a crew quarters assigns the selected role to it, and when a role assignment is undone or redone.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SquadSelectSound: Sound played when crew quarters are selected with the squad target tool.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SquadDeselectSound: Sound played when crew quarters are deselected in the squad target tool.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.AddSquadTargetSound: Sound played when a supply target is added to the selected crew quarters.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.RemoveSquadTargetSound: Sound played when a supply target is removed from the selected crew quarters. Also used as the fallback sound for other undo and redo steps in the crew toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TentativeCrewCircle: Circle drawn around crew inside the drag-selection rectangle that would be selected on release. Tinted with the current player color.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SelectedCrewCircle: Circle drawn around each currently selected crew member. Tinted with the current player color.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SquadCrewCircle: Circle drawn around the crew assigned to the quarters under the cursor while assigning a role, when the quarters does not already have that role.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SquadCrewDimCircle: Dimmed circle used in place of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SquadCrewCircle]] when the hovered quarters already has the role being assigned.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TentativeCrewLine: Line drawn from a tentatively selected crew member to their home quarters when crew homes are shown.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SelectedCrewLine: Line drawn from a selected crew member to their home quarters when crew homes are shown.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SquadCrewLine: Line drawn from each assigned crew member to the quarters under the cursor while assigning a role, when the quarters does not already have that role.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SquadCrewDimLine: Dimmed line used in place of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SquadCrewLine]] when the hovered quarters already has the role being assigned.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TentativeSquadNineSlice: Border drawn around the home quarters of tentatively selected crew, and around the quarters under the cursor that the selected crew could be assigned to.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SelectedSquadNineSlice: Border drawn around the home quarters of the selected crew when crew homes are shown.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TentativeCrewPathLine: Line traced along the walking path of tentatively selected crew when crew paths are shown.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SelectedCrewPathLine: Line traced along the walking path of selected crew when crew paths are shown.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.CrewPathLineUSpeed: Speed at which the texture of crew path lines scrolls along the line, in texture units per second. Produces the marching animation on [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TentativeCrewPathLine]] and [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SelectedCrewPathLine]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.RoleSprite: Generic role icon used for the role buttons in the crew toolbox and for the color preview in the role editor. Tinted with the role's color.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.RoleCursorSprite: Sprite drawn next to the mouse cursor while assigning a role to crew quarters. Tinted with the role's color.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.RoleDragLine: Vertical line drawn between role buttons to show the insertion position while dragging a role to reorder it.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.PriorityDisableSprite: Icon shown on the disable option (priority 0) of a job's priority row in the role editor.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.MirrorLine: Appearance settings for the draggable mirror centerline shown over the ship when mirror mode is enabled in the crew toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.MirrorOffSprite: Icon shown on the mirror mode toggle button while mirroring is disabled. Swapped for [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.MirrorOnSprite]] when enabled.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.MirrorOnSprite: Icon shown on the mirror mode toggle button while mirroring is enabled.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.CrewHighlightDimColor: Color multiplied over the ship view to dim it while the crew toolbox highlights crew and their quarters. Highlighted crew and parts are excluded from the dimming.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.CrewHighlightSprite: Sprite drawn over each assigned crew member to exclude them from the dimming overlay while the crew selection tool is active. When a role is hovered in the toolbox, only that role's crew are highlighted.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.PartHighlightNineSlice: Rectangle drawn over each crew quarters to exclude it from the dimming overlay. When a role is hovered in the toolbox, only that role's quarters are highlighted.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.PartHighlightTweenTime: Seconds it takes a part's highlight in the squad target tool's dimming overlay to fade in or out.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SelectedPartNineSlice: Border drawn around each selected crew quarters in the squad target tool.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SelectedMirroredPartNineSlice: Border drawn around the mirrored counterparts of the selected crew quarters when mirror mode is enabled.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TentativePartNineSlice: Border drawn around crew quarters inside the drag-selection rectangle that would be selected on release in the squad target tool.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TentativeMirroredPartNineSlice: Border drawn around the mirrored counterparts of tentatively selected crew quarters when mirror mode is enabled.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TentativeTargetNineSlice: Border drawn around the targetable part under the cursor, and its mirrors, while crew quarters are selected in the squad target tool.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SetCrewRoleNineSlice: Border drawn around the crew quarters under the cursor while assigning a role, when the quarters does not already have that role.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.AlreadySetCrewRoleNineSlice: Border used in place of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SetCrewRoleNineSlice]] when the hovered quarters already has the role being assigned.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.RandomizeCrewIcon: Icon of the button in the crew editor that randomizes the crew member's name and appearance.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.FavoriteSprite: Icon shown on crew members marked as favorites in the roster window and the crew editor.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.FavoriteUnderlaySprite: Sprite drawn in the world beneath each favorite crew member while the crew selection tool is active.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetLineDim: Dimmed style for the supply target lines of quarters that are not selected while other quarters are selected in the squad target tool. Only drawn when showing unselected crew assignments is enabled.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetLineFull: Full-strength style for the supply target lines of quarters that are neither selected nor under the cursor while no quarters are selected. Only drawn when showing unselected crew assignments is enabled.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetLineSelected: Style for the supply target lines of the selected or hovered quarters in the squad target tool. Also used for the lines of quarters whose role is hovered in the toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TentativeTargetLine: Line drawn from each selected quarters to the targetable part under the cursor before it is added as a supply target.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetLineUSpeed: Speed at which the texture of supply target lines scrolls along the line, in texture units per second. Produces the marching animation.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.CrewOverlayIcon: Crew icon drawn over each crew quarters and targetable part in the squad target tool. Tinted with the source or target overlay colors depending on the part's state.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.CrewOverlayIconBackgroundSprite: Backing sprite drawn behind [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.CrewOverlayIcon]]. Its size also determines the drawn size of the icon overlay.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SourceOverlayDimColor: Tint of the crew overlay icon on crew quarters that are neither selected nor hovered while other quarters are selected in the squad target tool.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SourceOverlayPulsingColorRange: Color range the crew overlay icon on crew quarters pulses between while no quarters are selected in the squad target tool.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SourceOverlayPulseInterval: Seconds for one full pulse cycle between the colors of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SourceOverlayPulsingColorRange]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.SourceOverlaySelectedColor: Tint of the crew overlay icon on crew quarters that are selected, under the cursor, or belong to the role hovered in the toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetOverlayDimColor: Tint of the crew overlay icon on targetable parts while no crew quarters are selected in the squad target tool.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetOverlayPulsingColorRange: Color range the crew overlay icon pulses between on parts that the selected quarters could target but do not target yet.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetOverlayPulseInterval: Seconds for one full pulse cycle between the colors of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetOverlayPulsingColorRange]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Crew.CrewGuiRules.TargetOverlaySelectedColor: Tint of the crew overlay icon on parts that are already targeted by the selected or highlighted quarters.

Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.PartNetworkGuiRules.DefaultRouteLine: The line rendered to visualise Part Network routes.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.PartNetworkGuiRules.DefaultRouteHubSprite: The sprite rendered at the central joint between multiple route lines.

Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.PartNetworkOverlayIco.RouteLineOffset: The offset of the route line end position along the port's direction.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.PartNetworkOverlayIco.EdgeOffset: The offset of the icon along the port's direction
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.PartNetworkOverlayIco.DrawLineToEdgeWhenConnected: Whether an additional line to the actual, un-offset edge should be drawn when the port is connected.

Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ToolIcon: Icon shown on the ship HUD mode button that opens the resource management tools.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.GrabToolIcon: Icon shown on the resources toolbox button that activates the grab tool for picking up and moving resources between storage tiles.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ClearToolIcon: Icon shown on the resources toolbox button that activates the tool for clearing a storage tile's assigned resource type.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplyChainToolOffIcon: Icon shown on the supply-chain toolbox button while the supply-chain tool is inactive. Swapped for [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplyChainToolOnIcon]] when the tool is selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplyChainToolOnIcon: Icon shown on the supply-chain toolbox button while the supply-chain tool is active. Replaces [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplyChainToolOffIcon]] when the tool is selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.MirrorLine: Appearance of the mirror centerlines and their drag handles that define the symmetry axes for mirrored resource placement. Shown while the mirror toggle ([[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.MirrorOnSprite]]) is enabled.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.MirrorOffSprite: Icon shown on the mirror toggle button while mirrored resource placement is off. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.MirrorOnSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.MirrorOnSprite: Icon shown on the mirror toggle button while mirrored resource placement is on. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.MirrorOffSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.StorageHighlightDimColor: Semi-transparent color painted over the whole ship except storage tiles and highlighted resource nuggets while the resources tool or supply-chain tool is active, darkening everything outside the areas of interest.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.StorageHighlightNineSlice: Border drawn around each storage grid that has no consumption or supply toggle state, tinted each frame by [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.StorageHighlightColorLerpRange]]. Grids with a toggle state use the consumption or supply nine-slices instead.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.StorageHighlightConsumptionOnNineSlice: Border drawn around a storage grid whose part currently has resource consumption enabled while the resources tool is active. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.StorageHighlightConsumptionOffNineSlice]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.StorageHighlightConsumptionOffNineSlice: Border drawn around a storage grid whose part currently has resource consumption disabled. The off counterpart to [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.StorageHighlightConsumptionOnNineSlice]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.StorageHighlightSupplyOnNineSlice: Border drawn around a storage grid that has automatic supply enabled and no consumption state. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.StorageHighlightSupplyOffNineSlice]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.StorageHighlightSupplyOffNineSlice: Border drawn around a storage grid that has automatic supply disabled and no consumption state. The off counterpart to [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.StorageHighlightSupplyOnNineSlice]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.StorageHighlightColorLerpRange: Two endpoint colors the storage grid borders ping-pong between, producing a subtle pulse. One full cycle takes [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.StorageHighlightColorLerpInterval]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.StorageHighlightColorLerpInterval: Seconds for one full ping-pong cycle of the storage grid border animation between the endpoints of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.StorageHighlightColorLerpRange]]. Each grid gets a random phase offset so the boxes do not pulse in unison.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TypedStorageGridInset: Distance in tiles that a typed storage part's highlight rectangle is pulled inward from the outer edges of its tiles, so the resources overlay box sits inside the part footprint. Flex storages use [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.FlexStorageGridInset]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.FlexStorageGridInset: Distance in tiles that a flex storage part's highlight rectangle is pulled inward from the outer edges of its tiles. The flex counterpart to [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.TypedStorageGridInset]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.NuggetHighlightCircle: Ring drawn around each loose resource nugget floating near the ship while the resources tool is active, excluding the nugget from the [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.StorageHighlightDimColor]] dimming.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileCountTextColor: Color of the per-tile resource count label drawn over storage tiles while the resource tools are active.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileCountTextFontSize: Font size in pixels of the per-tile resource count label drawn over storage tiles.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileCountTextHAlignment: Horizontal alignment of the per-tile resource count label relative to its anchor point, which is placed by [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.TileCountTextOffset]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileCountTextVAlignment: Vertical alignment of the per-tile resource count label relative to its anchor point, which is placed by [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.TileCountTextOffset]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileCountTextOffset: Offset in tiles from a storage tile's center to the anchor point of its resource count label. Rotated to follow the camera so the label stays fixed relative to the tile.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileCountIncreaseFmt: Format string for the tile count label when resources are being added to a non-empty tile, where `{0}` is the current count and `{1}` is the amount being added. Supports inline XML color tags.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileCountDecreaseFmt: Format string for the tile count label when resources are being removed, where `{0}` is the current count and `{1}` is the amount being removed. Supports inline XML color tags.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileCountZeroIncreaseFmt: Format string for the tile count label when resources are being added to an empty tile, where `{0}` is the amount being added. Supports inline XML color tags.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileTypeOverlayUnselectedEmptyColor: Color multiplying a storage tile's resource-type overlay icon in its dim default state, when the tile does not belong to the currently hovered or selected resource type.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileTypeOverlaySelectedPresentColor: Color multiplying the overlay icon of a tile assigned to the currently hovered or selected resource type that already holds the correct resource.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileTypeOverlaySelectedEmptyColor: Color multiplying the overlay icon of an empty tile assigned to the currently hovered or selected resource type.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileTypeOverlayIncorrectColor: Color multiplying a tile's overlay icon when the resource actually stored in the tile does not match the tile's assigned resource type.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TileTypeOverlayCursorColor: Color multiplying the resource-type overlay icon drawn beside the cursor while the set-resource-type tool is active with a resource type selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ResourceTransferLine: Line drawn from a resource transfer job's destination tile to its source for every scheduled transfer. Tinted by the four ResourceTransferLine colors depending on the source kind and whether the transfer's resource type is currently highlighted.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ResourceTransferLineUnselectedSourceColo: Color of a [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ResourceTransferLine]] whose source is an on-ship storage and whose resource type is not the currently highlighted one. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ResourceTransferLineSelectedSourceColor]] for the highlighted case.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ResourceTransferLineSelectedSourceColor: Color of a [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ResourceTransferLine]] whose source is an on-ship storage and whose resource type matches the currently highlighted one. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ResourceTransferLineUnselectedSourceColor]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ResourceTransferLineUnselectedCrewColor: Color of a [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ResourceTransferLine]] representing a crew-carried transfer or a floating nugget pickup whose resource type is not the currently highlighted one. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ResourceTransferLineSelectedCrewColor]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ResourceTransferLineSelectedCrewColor: Color of a [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ResourceTransferLine]] representing a crew-carried transfer or nugget pickup whose resource type matches the currently highlighted one. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ResourceTransferLineUnselectedCrewColor]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SetResourceTypeCursor: Mouse cursor shown by the set-resource-type tool while a resource type is selected to paint onto flex storage tiles. Replaced by [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ClearResourceTypeCursor]] when no type is selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SetResourceTypeTileHoverSprite: Sprite drawn over each flex storage tile that the set-resource-type tool would assign a type to. Replaced by [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ClearResourceTypeTileHoverSprite]] when clearing instead.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ClearResourceTypeCursor: Mouse cursor shown by the set-resource-type tool when no resource type is selected, so clicking clears tile assignments. Replaces [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SetResourceTypeCursor]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ClearResourceTypeTileHoverSprite: Sprite drawn over each flex storage tile whose resource type the set-resource-type tool would clear. Replaces [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SetResourceTypeTileHoverSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SetResourceTypeSound: Sound played when the set-resource-type tool assigns a resource type to one or more flex storage tiles. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ClearResourceTypeSound]] for clearing.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ClearResourceTypeSound: Sound played when the set-resource-type tool clears the resource type of one or more flex storage tiles. The counterpart to [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SetResourceTypeSound]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.MoveResourcesPickUpCursor: Mouse cursor shown by the move-resources tool before a source tile is grabbed. Replaced by [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.MoveResourcesPutDownCursor]] once resources are held.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.MoveResourcesPutDownCursor: Mouse cursor shown by the move-resources tool while carrying resources to a destination tile. Replaces [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.MoveResourcesPickUpCursor]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.MoveResourcesTileHoverPickUpSprite: Sprite drawn over each valid source tile the move-resources tool can pick up from. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.MoveResourcesTileHoverPutDownSprite]] for destination tiles.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.MoveResourcesTileHoverPutDownSprite: Sprite drawn over the held source tile and each valid destination tile while the move-resources tool carries resources. Replaces [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.MoveResourcesTileHoverPickUpSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.MoveResourcesCursorLine: Line drawn from the tile resources are carried from to the hovered destination tile or cursor while the move-resources tool drags resources. Its texture scrolls over time at [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.MoveResourcesCursorLineSpeed]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.MoveResourcesCursorLineSpeed: Speed in line-length units per second at which the texture of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.MoveResourcesCursorLine]] scrolls along the line. Larger values scroll faster.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.PickUpResourcesSound: Sound played when the move-resources tool picks resources up from a source tile. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.PutDownResourcesSound]] for dropping them off.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.PutDownResourcesSound: Sound played when carried resources are dropped onto a destination tile. Also used by the crew and resource transfer window and the creative-mode place-resource action.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.EjectResourcesSprite: Image on the move-resources tool's eject button, which dumps the selected storage tile's resources out through a connected airlock.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ConsumptionToggleButton: Appearance of the per-part button that toggles a consumer part's automatic resource delivery on and off. Its selected state shows the delivery-on image and its normal state the delivery-off image. See also [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplyToggleButton]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplyToggleButton: Appearance of the per-part button that toggles a storage part's automatic resource supply on and off. The supplier counterpart to [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ConsumptionToggleButton]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.PartHighlightNineSlice: Mask drawn around each relevant part while the supply-chain tool is active, cutting those parts out of the screen-wide dimming from [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.StorageHighlightDimColor]] so they stay bright. Its per-part opacity fades over [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.PartHighlightTweenTime]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.PartHighlightTweenTime: Seconds over which a part's [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.PartHighlightNineSlice]] opacity fades fully in or out as parts enter and leave the highlighted set, controlling how quickly the dimming overlay reveals or re-covers them.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SelectedPartNineSlice: Outline drawn around each part the player has selected as a supplier in the supply-chain tool. Its mirror-symmetric counterparts use [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SelectedMirroredPartNineSlice]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SelectedMirroredPartNineSlice: Outline drawn around the mirror-symmetric counterparts of selected supplier parts, showing which parts build-mirroring will also affect. Rendered dimmer than [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SelectedPartNineSlice]] in the vanilla data.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TentativePartNineSlice: Outline previewing parts that a pending drag-selection would add as suppliers, shown before the selection is confirmed. Their mirror counterparts use [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.TentativeMirroredPartNineSlice]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TentativeMirroredPartNineSlice: Outline previewing the mirror-symmetric counterparts of parts a pending drag-selection would add as suppliers. Rendered dimmer than [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.TentativePartNineSlice]] in the vanilla data.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TentativeTargetNineSlice: Outline drawn around the part under the cursor that would become a supply target if clicked. Colored to contrast with the supplier outlines [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.TentativePartNineSlice]] and [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SelectedPartNineSlice]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ProducerOverlayDimColor: Color multiplied onto a producer part's resource icon when the part is shown but not actively highlighted in the supply-chain tool. Contrasted with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ProducerOverlayPulsingColorRange]] and [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ProducerOverlaySelectedColor]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ProducerOverlayPulsingColorRange: Two colors a producer part's resource icon ping-pongs between through HSV space when highlighted in the supply-chain tool, cycling over [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ProducerOverlayPulseInterval]] seconds.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ProducerOverlayPulseInterval: Seconds for one full pulse cycle of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ProducerOverlayPulsingColorRange]]. For producers handling multiple resource types it is also the seconds each type icon is shown before cross-fading to the next.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ProducerOverlaySelectedColor: Color multiplied onto a producer part's resource icon when that producer is the selected part in the supply-chain tool. Distinct from the dimmed [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ProducerOverlayDimColor]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ConsumerOverlayDimColor: Color multiplied onto a consumer part's resource icon when the part is shown but not actively highlighted in the supply-chain tool. The consumer counterpart to [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ProducerOverlayDimColor]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ConsumerOverlayPulsingColorRange: Two colors a consumer part's resource icon ping-pongs between through HSV space when highlighted in the supply-chain tool, cycling over [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ConsumerOverlayPulseInterval]] seconds.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ConsumerOverlayPulseInterval: Seconds for one full pulse cycle of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ConsumerOverlayPulsingColorRange]]. For consumers handling multiple resource types it is also the seconds each type icon is shown before cross-fading to the next.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ConsumerOverlaySelectedColor: Color multiplied onto a consumer part's resource icon when that consumer is the selected part in the supply-chain tool. Distinct from the dimmed [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.ConsumerOverlayDimColor]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.OverlayBackgroundSprite: Backdrop drawn on each producer and consumer part beneath its resource-type icon in the supply-chain tool, darkening the part so the icon reads clearly.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.GenericTypeOverlay: Fallback icon drawn on a producer or consumer part in the supply-chain tool when the part handles the generic stackable resource rather than a specific resource type with its own overlay sprite.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ClearSupplyTargetsIcon: Icon for the context button that removes every supply target assigned to the currently selected supplier parts. Shown with the disabled shader when those parts have no targets.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplierSelectionOutlineSprite: Sprite drawing the border of the drag-selection rectangle in the supply-chain tool, tinted by the player color. Its interior is filled by [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplierSelectionFillSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplierSelectionFillSprite: Sprite filling the interior of the drag-selection rectangle in the supply-chain tool, tinted by the player color. Drawn behind the border from [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplierSelectionOutlineSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplyLineDim: Line drawn for an existing supplier-to-target link whose producer is not part of the current selection while other parts are selected. Rendered faint to de-emphasize it against [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplyLineSelected]] links.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplyLineFull: Line drawn at full opacity for an existing supplier-to-target link when no supplier is selected. The default appearance, contrasted with the faded [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplyLineDim]] used while a selection is active.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplyLineSelected: Line drawn for a supplier-to-target link belonging to a selected or hovered supplier. Colored to stand out above [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplyLineDim]] and [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplyLineFull]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.TentativeSupplyLine: Line previewing the supply link that would be created from each selected supplier to the target part under the cursor, shown before the click confirms it. Distinct from the committed [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplyLineSelected]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplyLineUSpeed: Scroll rate of the supply lines' arrow texture along each line, making the arrows appear to flow from producer toward target. Higher values scroll the flow animation faster.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplyChainToolCursor: Cursor shown while the supply-chain tool is active and no suppliers are selected. Replaced by [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplyChainToolSelectionCursor]] once suppliers are selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplyChainToolSelectionCursor: Cursor shown while supplier parts are selected in the supply-chain tool, signaling that clicking a part assigns or removes it as a supply target. Takes the place of [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplyChainToolCursor]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplierSelectSound: Sound played when parts are added as selected suppliers in the supply-chain tool. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplierDeselectSound]] for removal.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.SupplierDeselectSound: Sound played when selected supplier parts are removed from the selection in the supply-chain tool. The counterpart to [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.SupplierSelectSound]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.AddSupplyTargetSound: Sound played when the player assigns one or more parts as supply targets for the selected suppliers. Paired with [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.RemoveSupplyTargetSound]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.RemoveSupplyTargetSound: Sound played when the player removes supply targets from the selected suppliers. The counterpart to [[Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGuiRules.AddSupplyTargetSound]].
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.Resources.ResourceGui.ResourceTransferProgressAnimationSprite: Sprite drawn over a resource transfer widget while a transfer is in progress. Its shader animates in the direction resources are flowing based on the sign of the pending amount.

Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.ToggledImageButton.StateNormalSelectedNineSlice: The settings for the nine-slice shown when this widget is in its "Normal" state and is selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.ToggledImageButton.StateHighlightedSelectedNineSlice: The settings for the nine-slice shown when this widget is in its "Highlighted" state and is selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.ToggledImageButton.StatePressedSelectedNineSlice: The settings for the nine-slice shown when this widget is in its "Pressed" or "HotkeyPressed" states and is selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.ToggledImageButton.StateDisabledSelectedNineSlice: The settings for the nine-slice shown when this widget is in its "Disabled" state and is selected.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.ToggledImageButton.ImageRectController: The settings for how and where the image is shown.
Cosmoteer.Game.Gui.ToggledImageButton.ImageSprite: The settings for the image shown on this button.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.GeneratorStag.Name: The human-readable name of this GeneratorStage.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.GeneratorStag.IsEnabled: Whether this particular stage is enabled and will generate parts.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.ShipGenerator.Stages: A modifiable list of generator stages that are used to produce the random ships.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Astero.Parts: The type of parts to place.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Astero.ReplaceableParts: The list of part types that may be replaced by this stage.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Astero.MinPartsFraction: The minimum number of parts to add.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Astero.MaxPartsFraction: The maximum number of parts to add.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Astero.MinPartsPerDeposit: The minimum number of parts to add per deposit area.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Astero.MaxPartsPerDeposit: The maximum number of parts to add per deposit area.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Astero.Parts: The type of parts to place.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Astero.MinParts: The minimum number of parts to add.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Astero.MaxParts: The maximum number of parts to add.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Astero.WedgeParts: The type of parts to place.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Astero.ReplaceableParts: The list of part types that may be replaced by this stage.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.BasePl.ReplaceableParts: The list of part types that may be replaced by this stage.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.BasePl.ContiguousPathSearchLength: The maximum length to check to make sure contiguous paths won't be disconnected.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Conver.Conversions: The mapping from original part type to converted part type.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Conver.ChanceAtCenter: The chance that the conversion will happen at the center of the ship.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Conver.ChanceAtEdge: The chance that the conversion will happen at the edge of the ship.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Conver.ChanceExponent: The exponent applied to the chance after the base value is calculated from the part's distance to the center.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Enlarg.ReplaceableParts: The list of part types that may be replaced by this stage.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Enlarg.LargerParts: The type of parts to place.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Enlarg.RandomizeReplaceOrder: Whether the parts should be replaced in random order.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.FillGa.Part: The type of part to fill the gaps with.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.FillGa.FillMode: The logic used to determine which cells should be filled.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.FillGa.ScanRange: The distance in each direction to scan when determining whether an empty cell should be filled.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.FillGa.Iterations: The number of iterations of the algorithm to run.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.FillWi.Part: The type of part to place.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.FillWi.Iterations: The number of iterations of the algorithm to run.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.FillWi.FillOrder: The order in which the cells are filled.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.GridSt.Part: The type of part to add.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.GridSt.MinColumns: The minimum number of columns to generate.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.GridSt.MaxColumns: The maximum number of columns to generate.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.GridSt.MinRows: The minimum number of rows to generate.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.GridSt.MaxRows: The maximum number of rows to generate.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.GridSt.MinWidth: The minimum width of each cell.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.GridSt.MaxWidth: The maximum width of each cell.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.GridSt.MinHeight: The minimum height of each cell.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.GridSt.MaxHeight: The maximum height of each cell.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.GridSt.UniformCellSize: Whether or not each cell is of the same size.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Modula.MinModules: The minimum number of modules with which the create the ship.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Modula.MaxModules: The maximum number of modules with which to create the ship.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Modula.Modules: The modules from which the ship may be built.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceD.Door: The type of part to place.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceD.MinTravelDistance: If the distance between two sides of the wall are greater than this number, then a door will be added to the wall.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.SearchRange: The maximum distance from a potential new part location that an existing part can be and still count as being "near" the potential location.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.PlaceNear: The parts near which the new parts should be placed.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.Avoid: Avoids putting additional parts near parts of these types.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.Part: The type of part to place.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.MinParts: The minimum number of parts to place.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.MaxParts: The maximum number of parts to place.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.PlacementStrategy: The strategy to use when picking an optimal location for the part.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.UseWeightedRotationFlips: Whether to pick a desired rotation based on weight.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.RotationFlipWeights: The weights by which to pick the desired rotation/flip, if UseWeightedRotationFlips is true.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.MinScore: The minimum total score of parts to place.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.MaxScore: The maximum total score of parts to place.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.PlacementStrategy: The strategy to use when picking an optimal location for the part.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.UseWeightedRotationFlips: Whether to pick a desired rotation based on weight.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.RotationFlipWeights: The weights by which to pick the desired rotation/flip, if UseWeightedRotationFlips is true.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.Part: The type of part to place.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceP.Iterations: The number of iterations of the algorithm to run.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceW.UseWeightedRotationFlips: Whether to pick a desired rotation based on weight.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.PlaceW.RotationFlipWeights: The weights by which to pick the desired rotation/flip, if UseWeightedRotationFlips is true.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Struct.MinRadius: The minimum radius of the circle.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Struct.MaxRadius: The maximum radius of the circle.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Struct.MinWidth: The minimum width of the rectangle.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Struct.MaxWidth: The maximum width of the rectangle.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Struct.MinHeight: The minimum height of the rectangle.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Struct.MaxHeight: The maximum height of the rectangle.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Struct.RandomWeight: The random weight that this module will be selected.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Struct.AllowedFromDirections: The directions from which this module may be added, or null to allow from any direction.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Struct.Part: The type of part to add.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Surrou.PartToAdd: The type of part to place.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Surrou.PartToSurround: The type of part to surround.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Ships.Stages.Surrou.Layers: The number of layers to surround the parts with.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Simulation.MultiSpa.Mode: How the spawned objects will be searched for.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Simulation.MultiSpa.Tag: The tag of the spawned objects to search for.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Simulation.MultiSpa.Max: The maximum number of spawned objects to find.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Simulation.MultiSpa.LimitToBatch: Whether only the spawned objects belonging to the same batch as the first spawned object will be found.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Simulation.MultiSpa.LimitToFleet: Whether only the spawned objects belonging to the same fleet as the first spawned object will be found.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Simulation.MultiSpa.Distance: The maximum distance that the spawned objects can be from the search location.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Simulation.MultiSpa.OnlyFactions: If not null, only objects belonging to these factions will be found.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Simulation.MultiSpa.OnlyOpposingFactions: Whether only objects with opposing factions should be returned.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Simulation.SpawnedO.Mode: How the spawned object will be searched for.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Simulation.SpawnedO.Tag: The tag of the spawned object to search for.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Simulation.SpawnedO.OnlyFactions: If not null, only objects belonging to these factions will be found.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Simulation.SpawnedO.OnlyOpposingFactions: Whether only objects with opposing factions should be returned.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.HeightMapG.Name: The name of the generated heightmap.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.HeightMapG.Enabled: Whether the heightmap should be generated.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.HeightMapG.BaseValue: The base value of the heightmap before the layers are applied.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.HeightMapG.Layers: The layers that are used to generate the heightmap.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.HeightMapL.OpacityInRange: A range of values of the existing heightmap data in which this layer's opacity will ramp from 0 to 1.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.HeightMapL.OpacityOutRange: A range of values of the existing heightmap data in which this layer's opacity will ramp from 1 to 0.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.HeightMapL.Name: The name of this layer. Only used in the generator editor interface.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.HeightMapL.BlendMode: Specifies how this layer will be applied to the previous layer's results.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.HeightMapL.HeightScale: The factor applied to the values of this layer.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.TextureGen.SampleMode: The sample mode of the texture.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.TextureGen.BaseColor: The base color of the texture before the layers are applied.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.TextureGen.Layers: The layers that are used to generate the texture.

Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.TextureLay.Name: The name of this layer. Only used in the generator editor interface.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.TextureLay.BlendMode: Specifies how this layer will be applied to the previous layer's results.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.TextureLay.ColorScale: The per-channel color scaling applied to this layer's color before it is blended onto the previous layer's results.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.TextureLay.ReadClippingMask: If true, the effects of this layer will be clipped to the alpha channel of the previous layer that has this set to false.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.TextureLay.WriteClippingMask: If true, this layer's alpha values will be written to the clipping mask.
Cosmoteer.Generators.Textures.TextureLay.WriteTexture: If true, this layer will write to the texture.

Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.MainMenuBackgrounds: The backdrops available behind the title screen, each with its own background style, light direction, and planet. The title screen shows one at a time and steps through this array when the player cycles backgrounds or when the cycle-background setting advances it on each game launch.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.CreditsBackground: The backdrop shown behind the credits screen. Only its background style is used, without a planet or logo ship.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LogoShip: The ship that flies in the title screen background, arriving and leaving with FTL jump animations. Vanilla points this at the ship carrying the game logo.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.MasterVolumeAdjustSound: Plays as feedback when the master volume slider in the audio settings changes, so the player hears the new level.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.EffectsVolumeAdjustSound: Plays through the effects audio group as feedback when the effects volume slider in the audio settings changes.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.InterfaceVolumeAdjustSound: Plays through the interface audio group as feedback when the interface volume slider in the audio settings changes.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.CountdownBeepSound: Plays on each tick of the game-start countdown in the multiplayer setup screen and Build Battle.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.ChatMessageSound: Plays when a message arrives in the multiplayer chat box.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LogoShipFlyInSound: Plays when the [[Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LogoShip]] jumps into the title screen background.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LogoShipFlyOutSound: Plays when the [[Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LogoShip]] jumps out of the title screen background.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.MultiplayerJoinedSound: Plays when another player joins the multiplayer game setup lobby.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.MultiplayerLeftSound: Plays when a player leaves the multiplayer lobby or a game in progress.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LogoShipFlyOutDelay: Seconds between [[Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LogoShipFlyOutSound]] starting and the [[Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LogoShip]] actually jumping out. Vanilla uses 0.3.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.ShipColorSprite: The ship image shown on the color chooser buttons in the accessibility settings tab, where the player overrides the player, enemy, ally, and neutral ship colors.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.PlayerInGameSprite: Icon shown in the multiplayer player list next to players who are currently in a game.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.ContinueGameIcon: Icon on the title screen Continue button that loads the most recent save.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.NewGameIcon: Icon on the title screen New Game button.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LoadGameIcon: Icon on the title screen Load Game button.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.MultiplayerIcon: Icon on the title screen Multiplayer button.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.ExitIcon: Icon on the title screen Exit button. Also reused for the unstash button in Build Battle.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.NewsIcon: Icon on the title screen News button.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.SettingsIcon: Icon on the Settings button of the title screen and multiplayer lobby screens.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.ModsIcon: Icon on the Mods button of the title screen and multiplayer lobby screens.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.ShipLibraryIcon: Icon for the Ship Library entry in the title screen menu. Also reused in the PvP scoreboard and Build Battle gui.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.CodexIcon: Icon for the Codex entry in the title screen menu.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.RoadmapIcon: Icon for the Roadmap entry in the title screen menu.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.CommunityIcon: Icon for the Community submenu in the title screen menu.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.FeedbackIcon: Icon on the title screen Feedback button.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.CreditsIcon: Icon on the title screen Credits button.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.DiscordIcon: Icon for the Discord entry in the title screen Community submenu.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.SteamIcon: Icon for the Steam entry in the title screen Community submenu.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.RedditIcon: Icon for the Reddit entry in the title screen Community submenu.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.BackIcon: Icon on the back button of the multiplayer lobby and game setup screens.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.OnlineIcon: Icon on the Online tab of the multiplayer lobby browser, which lists Steam lobbies.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LanIcon: Icon on the LAN tab of the multiplayer lobby browser.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.DirectConnectIcon: Icon on the direct connect button of the multiplayer lobby browser.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.SearchIcon: Icon displayed inside text search fields throughout the gui.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LockIcon: Icon marking password-protected games in the multiplayer game list.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.HostIcon: Icon marking the hosting player in the game setup screen and PvP scoreboard.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.AiIcon: Icon marking AI-controlled players in the game setup screen and PvP scoreboard.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.ObserverIcon: Icon marking observers in the PvP scoreboard.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.DisconnectedIcon: Icon shown in the PvP scoreboard for players who have left or disconnected from the game.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.WaitingIcon: Icon shown in the PvP scoreboard for players the game is currently waiting on.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.RefreshIcon: Icon on the refresh button of the Steam lobby list.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.InCommsIcon: Icon shown in the PvP scoreboard next to players who are currently in the comms menu.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.Spinner: The sprite used as the animated busy indicator wherever the gui shows a loading spinner. Rotates continuously at [[Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.SpinnerSpeed]].
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.SpinnerSpeed: Degrees per second the [[Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.Spinner]] rotates. Vanilla uses 360, one full revolution per second.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LatencyLevels: Thresholds mapping a player's measured latency to a connection quality icon in the multiplayer game list and scoreboard. Levels are checked in order, and the first whose `MaxLatency` in seconds is not exceeded supplies the sprite shown.
Cosmoteer.Gui.MenuRules.LobbyNameGenerator: Generates the random code appended to Steam lobby names when hosting an online game. Vanilla produces a three character sequence of letters and digits.

Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.PulsingTextShader: Shader applied to a label's text renderer to make it pulse for attention, used on the build toolbox vital-part warning message. Vanilla points it at pulsing.shader.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.RightClickCursor: Cursor shown while hovering a widget that offers an alt-click or right-click context action, such as items in the ship library, mods dialog, resource toolboxes and crew roster. Vanilla uses cursor_right_click.png.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.InvalidCursor: Cursor shown while the pointer is over a target where the current action cannot be performed, such as an unaffordable or out-of-range FTL jump, an invalid salvage target, or an invalid crew placement. Vanilla uses cursor_invalid.png.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.GoodTextColor: Green color used for positive or success text, applied to labels and buttons like download-complete notices, victory messages and valid crew counts. Vanilla aliases [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MoneyRefundTextColor]] to it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.BadTextColor: Red color used for negative, error, or warning text, applied to failure and conflict labels, defeat messages, floating warning text, and warning indicator arrows.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MoneyTextColor: Cyan color used when displaying credit amounts the player can afford, applied to inline money text and to affordable ship-cost labels.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.InsufficientMoneyTextColor: Color used for credit amounts the player cannot afford, applied to money text and disabled purchase labels when funds are too low. Vanilla aliases it to [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.BadTextColor]].
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MoneyRefundTextColor: Color used for positive credit changes such as refunds and payouts, applied to money text prefixed with a plus sign and the money icon. Vanilla aliases it to [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.GoodTextColor]].
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.FameTextColor: Blue color used when displaying fame amounts in formatted UI text.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.InsufficientFameTextColor: Color used for fame amounts the player cannot afford, applied to fame text when the requirement is not met. Vanilla aliases it to [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.BadTextColor]].
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.FuelTextColor: Blue color used when displaying affordable fuel amounts in formatted UI text.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.InsufficientFuelTextColor: Color used for fuel amounts the player cannot afford, applied to fuel text when the available amount is short.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.LimitedTextColor: Orange color used to mark a value that is capped or limited, applied to resource transfer max labels and resource limit fields and stars.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ResizerTLBRIcon: Sprite intended as the top-left to bottom-right diagonal resize handle, the counterpart to [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ResizerTRBLIcon]]. Vanilla points it at widgets_resizer_tlbr.png, though no read site appears in the current game code.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ResizerTRBLIcon: Sprite drawn as a corner resize handle, used for the resize grip in the ship Miniview window. Vanilla points it at widgets_resizer_trbl.png.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MoverIcon: Sprite intended as a drag-to-move handle. Vanilla points it at widgets_mover.png, though no read site appears in the current game code.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DialogOpenSoundEffect: Sound effect played on the interface audio group whenever a dialog opens. Vanilla plays sounds/dialog_open.wav at volume 0.25.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DialogCloseSoundEffect: Sound effect played on the interface audio group whenever a dialog closes. Vanilla plays sounds/dialog_close.wav at volume 0.25.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DragAndDropCompleteSoundEffect: Sound effect assigned as the drop sound of drag-and-drop targets, played when a dragged item is successfully dropped such as reorganizing ships into folders.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DisabledIconShader: Shader applied to a button's image sprite to dim it while the button is disabled, used on undo, redo, build, repair, copy, cut, paste, delete, and similar toolbar icons when their action is unavailable. Vanilla points it at disabled.shader.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.PulsingIconShader: Shader applied to an icon sprite to make it pulse for attention, used on the repair-tool overlay sprite of the ship build button. Vanilla points it at pulsing.shader.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.GrayscaleIconShader: Shader applied to an icon sprite to desaturate it, used for part buttons that are locked in the demo or that the player cannot currently afford, and for grayed-out scoreboard icons. Vanilla points it at grayscale.shader.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.BlurShader: Shader used to blur the background behind a widget, producing the frosted-glass effect behind dialogs, tooltips, menus, context menus, and drop lists when fancy UI effects are enabled. Runs once per pass counted by [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.BlurPasses]].
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.BlurPasses: Number of blur iterations applied behind a widget with [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.BlurShader]]. Each iteration blurs horizontally and then vertically, so the draw count is twice this value. Vanilla sets it to 4.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.BlurFallbackMaterial: Material used to tint the area behind a widget instead of blurring it when fancy UI effects are disabled, giving a flat translucent backing behind dialogs and menus.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DefaultFont: Engine-wide default font. Every widget and text renderer that does not specify its own font uses this one. Vanilla loads the Play and Microsoft YaHei faces with fixed-width digits.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ShadowFont: Font that renders text with a soft black drop shadow, applied throughout the UI to labels and buttons that need to stay readable over busy backgrounds such as build, crew, ship name, chat, and title-screen text. Vanilla derives it from [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DefaultFont]] with a blurred black shadow pass.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.GlowFont: Font that renders text with a soft glow, applied to attention labels such as input-binding conflict warnings and the wishlist prompt. Vanilla derives it from [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DefaultFont]] with a blur pass behind the foreground.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ShadowGlowFont: Font that renders text with both a black shadow and a glow pass, applied to the build toolbox vital-part warning message. Vanilla derives it from [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DefaultFont]] with a shadow pass and a glow pass.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DefaultCursor: Engine-wide default mouse cursor, shown whenever no widget or tool overrides the pointer. Vanilla uses cursor_arrow.png.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.Widget: Base template for every GUI widget, holding position and the set of attached components. Plain widgets copy their initial settings from this template, and through the chain of other default templates it seeds the baseline for every widget class in the game.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.StaticWidget: Default template for widgets drawn with a nine-slice background that do not react to input. Static widgets copy their settings from this template, and the vanilla version establishes the grey semi-transparent box look that boxes, labels and images build on.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DynamicWidget: Default template for widgets that swap their nine-slice depending on interaction state such as normal, highlighted and pressed. Interactive widgets copy their settings from this template, and the game wires the default highlight and click shader-time animations onto it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.SimpleBox: Default template for containers that hold child widgets without arranging them. Simple boxes copy their settings from this template, which inherits the [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.StaticWidget]] box background in vanilla.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.LayoutBox: Default template for containers that automatically arrange their children according to a chosen layout mode. Layout boxes copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ScrollBox: Default template for layout boxes that show scroll bars when their children overflow the box. Scroll boxes copy their settings from this template, and vanilla wires the fade and scale animations onto its scroll bars.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ListBox: Default template for scroll boxes that track the selection state of their children and by default stretch them horizontally from top to bottom. List boxes copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.WindowBox: Default template for scroll boxes with a title bar that the user can move and resize. Window boxes copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TabBox: Default template for containers whose child pages are switched between using tab buttons. Tab boxes copy their settings from this template, with the buttons and pages coming from [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TabButton]] and [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TabPageBox]].
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TabButton: Default template for the clickable button in a tab strip that selects a page of a [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TabBox]]. Each tab button copies its settings from this template, which vanilla inherits from [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.SelectableButton]].
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TabPageBox: Default template for the scrollable page container that a [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TabBox]] shows for the selected tab. Each tab page copies its settings from this template, and the game wires the slide animation played when switching tabs onto it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.Label: Default template for widgets that display a simple text label. Labels copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.Image: Default template for widgets that display a single customizable image. Images copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.Button: Default template for clickable text buttons that react to hover and press. Buttons copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.SelectableButton: Default template for buttons that the user can select. Selectable buttons copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ToggledImageButton: Default template for selectable buttons that carry two full sets of state nine-slices for their unselected and selected looks plus a sprite. Such buttons copy their settings from this template, and vanilla builds its on and off switch buttons on it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ImageButton: Default template for clickable buttons that show an image with text on top. Image buttons copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ListItem: Default template for selectable buttons that highlight their background when selected, used for rows in list boxes. List items copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ImageListItem: Default template for selectable list items with an image and text on top. Such items copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TreeItem: Default template for selectable items in a tree view that can hold collapsible sub-items. Tree items copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ToggleCheckButton: Default template for toggle buttons that show a check sprite when selected. Check toggles copy their settings from this template, which vanilla draws as a filled or empty check box.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ToggleRadioButton: Default template for toggle buttons that show a radio sprite when selected. Radio toggles copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ToggleExpanderButton: Default template for toggle buttons that show an expander sprite for collapsing and expanding a section. Expander toggles copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DropButton: Default template for buttons that display another widget near them when clicked. Drop buttons copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DropList: Default template for list boxes opened by clicking a button and shown as a drop-down beneath it. Drop-down lists copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.HorizontalScrollBar: Default template for horizontal scroll bars whose value is changed by dragging a paddle or clicking increment and decrement buttons. Horizontal scroll bars copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.VerticalScrollBar: Default template for vertical scroll bars, the vertical counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.HorizontalScrollBar]]. Vertical scroll bars copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.HorizontalSlider: Default template for scroll bars styled as a draggable horizontal slider. Sliders copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ProgressBar: Default template for progress bars, a static widget with an overlay sprite clipped according to a value. Progress bars copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.SingleLineTextEditField: Default template for single-line editable text fields. Single-line fields copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.SingleLinePasswordTextEditField: Default template for single-line editable fields that mask the entered text. Password fields copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MultiLineTextEditField: Default template for multi-line editable text fields. Multi-line fields copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MultiLineWrappedTextEditField: Default template for multi-line editable fields that word-wrap their text. Wrapped multi-line fields copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MenuBar: Default template for bars meant to dock to the top of the window and hold menu categories. Menu bars copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MenuItem: Default template for buttons meant to appear inside a menu category. Menu items copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MenuToggleCheckItem: Default template for checkable menu items. Such items copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MenuToggleRadioItem: Default template for radio-style menu items. Such items copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MenuCategory: Default template for the list of menu items reached by clicking a header item. Menu categories copy their settings from this template, and the game wires the open animation of its menu box.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MenuSubCategory: Default template for the list of menu items reached by mousing over an item in another menu. Submenus copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MenuSeparator: Default template for the line that separates items within a menu category. Separators copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ContextMenu: Default template for lists of menu items that can be shown on demand anywhere on the screen. Context menus copy their settings from this template, and the game wires its open animation and click bindings.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ToolTipLabel: Default template for labels extended to display tool tips. Tooltip labels copy their settings from this template, and the game sets its display delay to half a second.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ToolTipBox: Default template for the scrollable wrapper around a tooltip label that allows unfocused scrolling with a key-bind and the scroll wheel. Tooltip boxes copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ZeroButtonDialog: Default template for dialogs that show a message with no button for the user to close them, expecting the application to close them. Such dialogs copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.OneButtonDialog: Default template for dialogs that show a message and prompt the user to press a single button. Such dialogs copy their settings from this template, and the game labels its button Okay bound to the confirm key.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TwoButtonDialog: Default template for dialogs that show a message and prompt a choice between two options. Such dialogs copy their settings from this template, and the game labels the options Okay and Cancel.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ThreeButtonDialog: Default template for dialogs that show a message and prompt a choice among three options. Such dialogs copy their settings from this template, and the game labels the options Yes, No and Cancel.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.OneButtonTextEntryDialog: Default template for dialogs that show a message and prompt the user to edit text and press a single button. Such dialogs copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TwoButtonTextEntryDialog: Default template for dialogs that show a message and prompt the user to edit text and choose between two options. Such dialogs copy their settings from this template, and the game labels the options Okay and Cancel.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TwoButtonPasswordEntryDialog: Default template for two-option text entry dialogs whose field masks the entered text. Password entry dialogs copy their settings from this template.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.HollowWidget: Styles a hollow recessed frame drawn from the alternate box sprite region so a container shows an inset outline instead of a filled panel. Dialogs and toolboxes copy it onto their list and layout boxes, including the saved-game list, mods lists, settings tabs, tab-strip boxes, and many build, crew, paint, and resources toolbox panels.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MaskedSimpleBox: Adds an alpha nine-slice child mask to the plain [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.SimpleBox]] so child content is clipped to rounded corners. The minimap, miniview, and the decal size picker popout build from it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MaskedLayoutBox: Adds the rounded alpha child mask to a docked [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.LayoutBox]]. The new game config, career setup, and clear objects dialogs build boxes from it, and the vote window, comms toolbox, and the POI and planet doodad config windows copy its child mask onto themselves.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MaskedScrollBox: Adds the rounded alpha child mask to a [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ScrollBox]] so scrolled content is clipped to the panel corners. Vanilla data uses it as the menu dropdown box and as the base for the tooltip box.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MaskedListBox: Adds the rounded alpha child mask to a [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ListBox]]. The load and save game dialog's saved-game list and the paint toolbox layers list build from it, and vanilla data uses it as the drop-list list box.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MaskedTabBox: Adds the rounded alpha child mask to a [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TabBox]]. Only the styling definition exists and no code or other data consumers were found.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.BoxInnerShadow: Styles a non-interactive overlay that fills its parent and draws an inner shadow nine-slice, leaving input to pass through. The minimap and miniview add it as a child so their framed viewports get a recessed shadow along the inside edge.
