Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.SquareButton: Styles a compact square button using the un-rounded box sprite. Toolbox action buttons copy it, including build and paint rotate, flip, cut, copy, paste, delete and invert, plus the game speed steppers, eject and trashcan buttons, and formation buttons.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.FlatButton: Styles a flat borderless button whose frame only appears on hover or press. It backs menu and toggle buttons and is copied onto the game menu button, ship command button, workshop preview, forfeit and new-ship buttons, and similar inline controls.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.OkayButton: Styles a standard button with green confirm text. Dialogs create their okay, submit, start, join, next and close buttons from it across the load and save, settings, feedback, news, mods, hire crew, and ship info dialogs among others.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.GoodButton: Styles a green affirmative button with a green-tinted frame and green text. Construct, ready, start, activate, do-trade and transfer buttons build from it, along with the title screen wishlist button.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.BadButton: Styles a red cautionary button with a red-tinted frame and red text. Cancel, decline and deactivate buttons build from it, including cancel-all and cancel-ready in the build toolbox, the multiplayer cancel countdown, mod deactivate, FTL cancel jump, and mission decline.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.TitleBarButton: Styles a flat title-bar button that stays borderless normally and shows its frame only when highlighted or pressed. Vanilla data uses it as the base for [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.UndoButton]] and [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.RedoButton]].
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.VerticalSeparator: Styles a one-pixel-wide vertical divider line drawn from a thin sprite strip. Only the styling definition exists and no code or other data consumers were found.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MainMenuButton: Styles a large flat menu button using the glow font at size 20 with extra side padding. The multiplayer game setup screen builds its match results button from it, and it is the base for [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MainMenuIconButton]].
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MainMenuIconButton: Extends [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MainMenuButton]] with left-aligned text and a 48 pixel icon on the left. The title screen builds most of its main menu entries from it, and the lobby and game setup screens use it for their back, settings, mods, and direct connect buttons.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.SelectedMainMenuButton: Extends [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MainMenuIconButton]] so the selected highlight frame is always shown. The lobby screen applies it to the online or LAN entry that matches the current screen to mark it as active.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MainMenuCategory: Combines [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MainMenuIconButton]] styling with dropdown menu behavior. The title screen builds its community and extras dropdown category button from it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.MainMenuItem: Styles a taller 36 pixel menu entry with size 20 text and an icon. The title screen dropdown menus create their items from it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.CodexItem: Styles a codex list entry as a [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ListItem]] with panel open and close sounds on selection. The codex dialog and its page window items build their entries from it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ToolButton: Styles a selectable tool button that combines flat button and image list item behavior with an all-around selected frame. Build, paint, resources, and crew toolboxes create their tool icons from it, along with the ship command selectable and the galaxy map FTL jump button.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.IconItem: Extends [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ToolButton]] with a filled normal-state frame so icons always sit in a visible cell. The texture picker, paint scheme icons, and the creative doodads toolbox build their entries from it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.PopoutButton: Styles a button whose nine-slice has a downward popout tab and an extended right border. The part color picker, part popout toggle, and decal size picker buttons build from it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.FlatPopoutButton: Styles the flat variant of the popout tab button combining flat button and image list item behavior. The build and paint toolbox brush buttons and the ship gui popout button build from it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.SwitchButton: Styles a tall toggle switch with a raised frame and a sliding indicator strip along the bottom border. The part switched build toggle button uses it for the plain switch style.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.OffAlertSwitchButton: Extends [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.SwitchButton]] with an alerting sprite for the off state. The part switched build toggle button uses it for the off-alert switch style.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.OnAlertSwitchButton: Extends [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.SwitchButton]] with a mirrored alerting sprite for the on state. The part switched build toggle button uses it for the on-alert switch style.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.FlatSwitchButton: Styles the flat version of the sliding toggle switch. Build, resources, paint, and crew toolboxes build blueprint, mirror, supply, and target toggles from it, the ship gui builds its command switch, and the part switched toggle button uses it for the plain switch style.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.FlatOffAlertSwitchButton: Extends [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.FlatSwitchButton]] with an alerting off-state sprite. The part switched toggle button uses it for the off-alert switch style.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.FlatOnAlertSwitchButton: Extends [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.FlatSwitchButton]] with a mirrored alerting on-state sprite. The part switched toggle button uses it for the on-alert switch style.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.LeftTabButton: Restyles the tab selector button for tab columns stacked down the left edge rather than across the top. Pairs with [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.VerticalTabPageBox]] to build the left-hand tab strip of the settings dialog.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.VerticalTabPageBox: Styles the scrolling page body shown for each tab in a vertically stacked tab layout, and its tab-switch animation slides pages vertically instead of horizontally. The settings dialog pairs it with [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.LeftTabButton]].
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.HorizontalExpanderButton: Styles an expander toggle whose selected state shows a sideways chevron, driving the collapsible custom-formations row in the fleet formations panel.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.VerticalExpanderButton: Styles an expander toggle with up and down chevrons and a centered icon for vertically collapsing sections. Only the styling definition exists and no game code currently instantiates it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.CategoryBox: Styles a borderless container for collapsible category groups such as the settings dialog sections, the multiplayer mode rules box, and the creative doodads toolbox. Receives an expand animation that scales the box open from zero height.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.FixedWindowBox: Provides the window frame used by the game's non-movable toolbox and popup windows including the build, resources, paint, crew, and comms toolboxes plus the FTL jump and multiplayer vote windows. Shares the standard look of [[Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.WindowBox]].
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ProgressBarWhite: Styles a progress bar with a white fill, used for the countdown or capture bar shown by the PvP elimination game mode.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ThinProgressBarGood: Styles a slim horizontal bar with a green fill, used for the positive reputation progress bar in the career reputation tab.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ThinProgressBarBad: Styles a slim horizontal bar with a red fill, used for the negative reputation progress bar in the career reputation tab.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ThinProgressBarFame: Styles a slim horizontal bar with a fame-tinted fill, used for the fame progress bar in the career reputation tab.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ProgressDotEmpty: Styles the unfilled milestone marker dot placed along the career reputation progress bars.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ProgressDotGood: Styles the green milestone marker dot for reached positive milestones on the career reputation bars.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ProgressDotBad: Styles the red milestone marker dot for negative milestones on the career reputation bars.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ProgressDotFame: Styles the fame-colored milestone marker dot for the fame track in the career reputation tab.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.StatBar: Styles the progress bar behind each ship statistic readout, used by the ship stat bars in the build toolbox stats panel such as command points.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.IdealStatBar: Styles the thin green marker overlaid on a ship stat bar to indicate the ideal or target value, drawn by the build toolbox ship stat bars.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.HamburgerMenuDown: Styles a three-line hamburger menu category whose dropdown opens downward, used for the options menus in the load/save, mods, ship library, roster, and role edit dialogs among others.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.HamburgerMenuUp: Styles a hamburger menu category whose dropdown opens upward, used for the options menus anchored near the bottom of the build, paint, resources, and crew toolboxes.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.HamburgerButtonDown: Styles a hamburger menu that adds a full button background and opens downward, used for the option menus in the creative battle helper, PvP and career config screens, and the ship info dialog.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.HamburgerButtonUp: Styles a button-backed hamburger menu opening upward. Only the styling definition exists and no game code currently instantiates it.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.HamburgerFlatButtonDown: Styles a hamburger menu with a flat button background opening downward, used for the option menus in the multiplayer player list, game setup screen, and color picker.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.HamburgerFlatButtonUp: Styles a hamburger menu with a flat button background opening upward, used for the menus on ship and part cards, the crew and comms trade toolboxes, and the crew and resource transfer window.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.UndoButton: Styles the undo icon button on toolbar title bars, used by the build, paint, resources, and crew toolboxes.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.RedoButton: Styles the redo icon button on toolbar title bars, used by the build, paint, resources, and crew toolboxes.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ArrowUpLeft: Styles the up-left diagonal arrow cell in the eight-way ship direction chooser.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ArrowUp: Styles the upward arrow cell in the eight-way ship direction chooser.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ArrowUpRight: Styles the up-right diagonal arrow cell in the eight-way ship direction chooser.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ArrowRight: Styles the rightward arrow cell in the eight-way ship direction chooser.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ArrowDownRight: Styles the down-right diagonal arrow cell in the eight-way ship direction chooser.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ArrowDown: Styles the downward arrow cell in the eight-way ship direction chooser.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ArrowDownLeft: Styles the down-left diagonal arrow cell in the eight-way ship direction chooser.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.ArrowLeft: Styles the leftward arrow cell in the eight-way ship direction chooser.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.PopoutConnector: Styles the small connector graphic that visually links a popped-out panel back to its originating button, used by the build and paint color pickers, the formations box, the decal size picker, and part popout toggle buttons.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DragCandidateNineSlice: Styles the outline drawn around a valid drop target while a drag is in progress, shown on ship library folder items during drag and drop.
Cosmoteer.Gui.WidgetRules.DragHoverNineSlice: Styles the stronger highlight drawn on the drop target directly under the cursor, shown on ship library folder items when a dragged item hovers over them.

Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.CombatDifficultyR.StatusFactorsVsEnemies: The chance and value factors for status types when applied to enemies.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.CombatDifficultyR.StatusFactorsFromEnemies: The chance and value factors for status types when applied by enemies.

Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.LogIcon: The icon on the career HUD button that opens the log window and on that window's mission log tab.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.ReputationIcon: The icon on the reputation tab of the career log window. Also marks the fame bar inside that tab.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.TrackedIcon: The icon marking missions that are currently tracked. Shown next to tracked missions in the HUD list and on the untrack buttons in the mission log.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.UntrackedIcon: The icon on the track buttons of missions that are not currently tracked in the mission log.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.NewMissionSound: Played together with the on-screen New Mission banner when the player receives a mission.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedSound: Played together with the on-screen Mission Complete banner when a mission is finished.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionFailedSound: Played together with the on-screen Mission Failed banner when a mission fails.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.FameLevelUpSound: Played together with the on-screen banner when the player's fame reaches a new level.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.GoToMissionSound: Played when clicking a mission's location link in the missions panel or an objective, which jumps the view to the mission target.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.ReputationLevelUpSound: Played together with the on-screen banner when reputation with a faction rises to a higher level.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.ReputationLevelDownSound: Played together with the on-screen banner when reputation with a faction drops to a lower level.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.NewMissionColorAnim: Color keyframes for the New Mission headline shown at the top center of the screen. Each entry pairs a time in seconds from the start of the animation with the color the label tweens to, and the banner is removed after the last keyframe.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.NewMissionNameColorAnim: Color keyframes for the mission name line shown below the New Mission headline. Uses the same seconds-and-color keyframe format as [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.NewMissionColorAnim]].
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedColorAnimIn: Color keyframes that fade in the Mission Complete headline. The matching fade-out is [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.MissionCompletedColorAnimOut]].
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedNameColorAnimIn: Color keyframes that fade in the mission name line below the Mission Complete headline.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedFirstRewardAnimDelayIn: Seconds after the Mission Complete headline appears before the first reward line starts animating in.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedAdditionalRewardAnimDela: Seconds between each further reward line starting to animate in after the first.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedRewardColorAnimIn: Color keyframes that fade in each reward line of the Mission Complete banner.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedRewardTickDuration: Seconds a reward line takes to count its displayed value up from zero to the full reward amount.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedDelayAfterLastReward: Seconds the completed banner holds after the last reward line has appeared before the fade-out animations begin.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedColorAnimOut: Color keyframes that fade out the Mission Complete headline once [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.MissionCompletedDelayAfterLastReward]] has elapsed.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedNameColorAnimOut: Color keyframes that fade out the mission name line of the Mission Complete banner.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedFirstRewardAnimDelayOut: Seconds into the fade-out phase before the first reward line starts fading out.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedAdditionalRewardAnimDela: Seconds between each further reward line starting to fade out after the first.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionCompletedRewardColorAnimOut: Color keyframes that fade out each reward line of the Mission Complete banner.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionFailedColorAnim: Color keyframes for the Mission Failed headline, covering both fade-in and fade-out in one sequence. Uses the same seconds-and-color keyframe format as [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.NewMissionColorAnim]].
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MissionFailedNameColorAnim: Color keyframes for the mission name line shown below the Mission Failed headline.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.FameLevelUpColorAnim: Color keyframes for the Fame Level Up headline shown when the player's fame reaches a new level.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.FameLevelUpNameColorAnim: Color keyframes for the new fame title line shown below the Fame Level Up headline.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.ReputationLevelUpColorAnim: Color keyframes for the Reputation Level Up headline shown when reputation with a faction rises to a higher level.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.FameLevelUpCrewColorAnim: Color keyframes for the crew gained line of the fame banner. That line only appears when the new fame level raises the player's crew capacity.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.FameLevelUpCrewAnimDelayIn: Seconds after the Fame Level Up banner appears before the crew gained line starts animating.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.ReputationLevelUpNameColorAnim: Color keyframes for the faction and title line shown below the Reputation Level Up headline.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.ReputationLevelDownColorAnim: Color keyframes for the Reputation Level Down headline shown when reputation with a faction drops to a lower level.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.ReputationLevelDownNameColorAnim: Color keyframes for the faction and title line shown below the Reputation Level Down headline.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.MaxTrackedMissions: The most missions the player can have tracked at the same time. Trying to track more shows a dialog, and the value also caps the Show Nearby Missions choices in the gameplay settings.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.TrackedMissionLandmarkIcon: The icon drawn over the HUD indicator of a ship or stasis landmark that is the target of a tracked mission. [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.UntrackedMissionLandmarkIcon]] is used instead when the mission is not tracked.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.TrackedMissionLandmarkIconOffset: Offset in GUI pixels from the center of the target's HUD indicator to where [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.TrackedMissionLandmarkIcon]] is drawn. Negative Y moves the icon up.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.TrackedMissionPOIIcon: The icon drawn over the HUD indicator of a point of interest that is the target of a tracked mission. [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.UntrackedMissionPOIIcon]] is used instead when the mission is not tracked.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.TrackedMissionPOIIconOffset: Offset in GUI pixels from the center of the point of interest's HUD indicator to where [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.TrackedMissionPOIIcon]] is drawn. Negative Y moves the icon up.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.TrackedMissionNodeIcon: The icon drawn on a galaxy map node that a tracked mission points to. [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.UntrackedMissionNodeIcon]] is used instead when the mission is not tracked.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.TrackedMissionNodeIconOffset: Offset from the map node's center to where [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.TrackedMissionNodeIcon]] is drawn, in galaxy map coordinates. The offset is multiplied by the same scale as the icon itself.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.UntrackedMissionLandmarkIcon: The icon drawn over the HUD indicator of a ship or stasis landmark that is the target of an untracked mission. Tracked missions use [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.TrackedMissionLandmarkIcon]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.UntrackedMissionLandmarkIconOffset: Offset in GUI pixels from the center of the target's HUD indicator to where [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.UntrackedMissionLandmarkIcon]] is drawn. Negative Y moves the icon up.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.UntrackedMissionPOIIcon: The icon drawn over the HUD indicator of a point of interest that is the target of an untracked mission. Tracked missions use [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.TrackedMissionPOIIcon]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.UntrackedMissionPOIIconOffset: Offset in GUI pixels from the center of the point of interest's HUD indicator to where [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.UntrackedMissionPOIIcon]] is drawn. Negative Y moves the icon up.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.UntrackedMissionNodeIcon: The icon drawn on a galaxy map node that an untracked mission points to. Tracked missions use [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.TrackedMissionNodeIcon]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.Missions.UntrackedMissionNodeIconOffset: Offset from the map node's center to where [[Cosmoteer.Modes.Career.Missions.MissionsGuiRules.UntrackedMissionNodeIcon]] is drawn, in galaxy map coordinates. The offset is multiplied by the same scale as the icon itself.

Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.ID: The unique identifier other rules use to refer to this resource, for example in part costs or [[Cosmoteer.Data.Rules.FtlFuelResource]]. Loading fails if two resources share the same ID.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.NameKey: Localization key of the resource's display name, shown in the resources toolbox, tooltips and transfer windows.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.PluralNameKey: Localization key of the name used when referring to more than one unit of the resource. Falls back to [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.NameKey]] when omitted.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.DescriptionKey: Localization key of the descriptive text shown in the resource's tooltips, for example on toolbox buttons and nuggets.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.BuyPrice: The money cost to buy one unit of the resource. Also serves as the per-unit value when totaling ship costs and refunds, and as the sell refund when [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.SellPrice]] is not set.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.SellPrice: The money refunded when selling one unit of the resource. Defaults to [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.BuyPrice]] when omitted.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.Icon: The sprite representing the resource in UI lists, buttons and transfer windows. It is also registered as a text image named `resource.<ID>` so the resource can be shown inline in text.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.IconButtonVerticalOffset: Vertical offset, in pixels, applied to the position of [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.Icon]] inside the resource's button in the resources toolbox.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.Overlay: The sprite drawn over storage tiles assigned to this resource in the resource management overlay, and at the cursor while assigning a tile's resource type.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.QuantityDisplayDivisor: Divides quantities and capacities of the resource before they are displayed in the UI. Vanilla batteries use `1000` so that internal charge units display as whole batteries.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.QuantityDisplayFormat: The .NET numeric format string used to render displayed quantities after dividing by [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.QuantityDisplayDivisor]]. The default `n0` shows whole numbers with digit grouping.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.MaxPerNugget: The quantity of the resource in a full nugget, and the most a single crew member can carry at once. Also spans the quantity range that [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.NuggetSprites]] is mapped over.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.CarrySpeed: Multiplies a crew member's move speed while carrying this resource. Values below 1 slow the carrier down.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.MaxStackSize: How many units a single storage stack can hold. Any value above 0 makes the resource stackable and storable in resource grids, which also requires [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.FlexGridPriorityKey]] to be set.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.ReturnToStorage: Whether leftover carried amounts are returned to the ship's storage instead of simply discarded, and whether stored amounts count toward the ship's resource refund. Resources with this enabled must define [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.NuggetSprites]].
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.LoseExcessOnDelivery: Destroys whatever part of a crew delivery the destination could not accept, instead of leaving the excess in the crew member's hands.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.FlexGridPriorityKey: Identifier under which crew role priorities for fetching this resource into flex resource storage are stored. Required for stackable resources (see [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.MaxStackSize]]).
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.FlexGridDefaultPriority: The default crew job priority for fetching this resource into flex resource storage, used when a crew role has no explicit priority set under [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.FlexGridPriorityKey]].
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.DropRates: The `0..1` chances that this resource drops as nuggets when its containing part or storage is destroyed or salvaged or its carrier is killed, plus the fraction of the build cost charged when repairing. Each rate has a `Player…` variant that overrides it for player-built parts.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.StackSprites: The sprites drawn on a storage tile holding this resource. The sprite advances to the next entry for every [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.ResourcesPerStackSprite]] units in the stack.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.ResourcesPerStackSprite: How many units of the resource each successive entry of [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.StackSprites]] represents. With the default of 1 the stack sprite changes with every unit added.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.StackReceiveMediaEffects: Effects played when this resource is delivered into a stack, for example into storage or a construction or repair site.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.StackPickupMediaEffects: Effects played when a crew member picks this resource up from a stack.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.NuggetPickupMediaEffects: Effects played when a nugget of this resource is collected in space.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.DeathHitEffects: Hit effects performed when a storage stack of this resource is destroyed. Their strength scales with how full the stack was relative to [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.MaxStackSize]].
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.DeathMediaEffects: Effects played when a storage stack of this resource is destroyed. Their strength scales with how full the stack was, like [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.DeathHitEffects]].
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.HitResponse: Defines the response to the resource's containing part being hit.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.Rarity: The rarity category of the resource's nuggets, which must match an entry in [[Cosmoteer.Resources.NuggetRules.Rarities]]. It controls how the nuggets are rendered as dots on the map and in fog of war. Defaults to `Common`.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.NuggetSprite: A single sprite used for this resource's nuggets and for the resource while carried by crew. Shorthand for [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.NuggetSprites]] with one entry.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.NuggetSprites: The sprites used for this resource's nuggets and for the resource while carried by crew. The sprite is picked by the quantity relative to [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.MaxPerNugget]], so later entries represent larger amounts. Required when [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.ReturnToStorage]] is true.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.NestedNuggetSprites: Nested form of [[Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules.NuggetSprites]]. The outer list splits the `1..MaxPerNugget` quantity range into bands and each inner list subdivides its band further.

Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules/HitRes.HitEffects: The hit effects to perform, if any.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules/HitRes.MediaEffects: The media effects to play, if any.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules/HitRes.Chance: The default base chance to respond to a hit.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules/HitRes.ChancePerNDamage: The chance added per NDamage in a hit.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules/HitRes.NDamage: The quantity of damage in a hit that adds a response chance of ChancePerNDamage.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules/HitRes.RequireDirectHit: Whether triggering a response requires a specified HitArea that includes the resource's location.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules/HitRes.RemoveOnResponse: Whether the responding resources should be removed on a successful response.

Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules/HitRes.Chance: When set, overrides the default Chance value.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules/HitRes.ChancePerNDamage: When set, overrides the default ChancePerNDamage value.
Cosmoteer.Resources.ResourceRules/HitRes.NDamage: When set, overrides the default NDamage value.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Blueprints.BlueprintNetw.Signal: ID of the signal to send.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Blueprints.BlueprintNetw.SignalThroughNode: ID of the blueprint-available component to spread the network signal through.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Blueprints.BlueprintNetw.ValidSignals: The collection of signal IDs that can activate the value overlay.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Blueprints.BlueprintNetw.AllowUpstreamSignals: Whether signals upstream from the SignalThroughNode subnetwork are valid.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Blueprints.BlueprintNetw.AllowDownstreamSignals: Whether signals downstream from (visible to) the SignalThroughNode subnetwork are valid.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Blueprints.BlueprintNetw.SignalThroughNode: ID of the blueprint-available component to receive the network signal from.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Blueprints.BlueprintNetw.DrawOnHover: Whether this value overlay should be drawn regardless of signal when the part is hovered in build mode.

Cosmoteer.Ships.BuffModifier.BuffType: The ID of the BuffType that the modifier uses.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.BasePartNetwork.BlueprintOperationalToggle: The ID of the blueprint component to use for the blueprint port's operational checks, if any.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.BasePartNetwork.BlueprintConnectionMode: The mode used to determine the validity of a connection between two blueprint ports.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.BasePartNetwork.RouteEndpoint: The IPartNetworkRegistrable component ID that inbound routes should connect to. If null, inbound routes will not be created.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.BasePartNetwork.RouteSource: The IPartNetworkRegistrable component ID that outbound routes should connect from. If null, outbound routes will not be created.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.BasePartNetwork.BlueprintRouteEndpoint: The IPartNetworkRegistrable component ID that inbound blueprint routes should connect to. If null, inbound blueprint routes will not be created.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.BasePartNetwork.BlueprintRouteSource: The IPartNetworkRegistrable component ID that outbound blueprint routes should connect from. If null, outbound blueprint routes will not be created.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.BasePartNetwork.Types: The port types that the port fulfills, and, if CompatibleTypes is unset, the port types that the port accepts.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.BasePartNetwork.CompatibleTypes: The port types that the port will accept connections with.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.BasePartNetwork.IncompatibleTypes: The port types that the port will refuse connections with, even if they are otherwise compatible.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkFilt.Categories: The network categories that components must match one or more of to be considered valid. If this is null, all categories will be allowed.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkFilt.AllowUncategorized: Whether components with no network categories will be included.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkPort.OverlayIcon: The ID of the custom overlay icon drawn to represent the port.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkPort.ConnectedEdgeIcon: The ID of the additional icon to draw on the port's edge when connected, if any.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkPort.RouteLine: The ID of the custom overlay route line drawn for the port's routes.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkPort.DrawRouteHub: Whether this port wants its component to draw a 'route hub' sprite. A hub will be drawn if this is true for any connected ports.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkPort.RouteDrawMode: The mode determining when route lines should be drawn for the port.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkPort.IconDrawMode: The mode determining when the icon should be drawn for the port
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkPort.HighlightPartOnPrimaryConnection: Whether the port's part will be highlighted when connected to a port on a part being placed.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkPort.PartHighlightColor: The color of the highlight drawn when HighlightPartOnPrimaryConnection is true.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkPort.SpreadHighlightThroughNetwork: The ID of a network node provider component that the highlight should spread from to all parts in its network, if any.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkPort.Direction: The orthogonal direction that the port connects in.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkReso.ResourceStorage: The backing resource storage that the store will use to provide and receive resources.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkReso.ReservedResourceLevel: The amount of resources in the ResourceStorage that are unavailable to the store. Represents a percentage of max capacity if ResourceLevelsAreFractions is true.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkReso.MaxResourceLevel: The resource level in the ResourceStorage that the store is able to deposit up to. Represents a percentage of max capacity if ResourceLevelsAreFractions is true.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkReso.ResourceLevelsAreFractions: Whether the resource level values are a fraction of the ResourceStorage's capacity, or an absolute value.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkReso.AllowProviding: Whether the store can fulfill requests for its resource from the part-network.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkReso.AllowReceiving: Whether the store can receive resources pushed through the part-network.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkReso.Categories: The network categories of the storage.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkRout.Start: The ID of the route's origin component.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkRout.End: The ID of the route's destination component.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkRout.Cost: The path cost of traversing the route.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkTogg.ToggleSource: The ID of the IPartComponentToggle that this exposes to the network.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkTogg.Categories: The network categories of the toggle.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkValu.Mode: The combination mode for the retrieved values.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkValu.ValueSource: The ID of the IPartComponentValue that this exposes to the network.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Networks.PartNetworkValu.Categories: The network categories of the value.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Buffs.BasePartBuff.BlueprintBuffMultiplier: The value reference for the buff value multiplier for the BlueprintPartBuffProvider.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Buffs.PartFloodBuf.FloodFillLimitRegion: The region around the initial source provider that this provider can flood-fill to. Infinite if null.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Buffs.PartFloodBuf.ChainLengthLimit: The maximum number of parts aside from the source that can be chained through to provide a buff, including the receiver.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Colliders.BaseColl.StatusResistances: The collider's base resistances to status types, interpolated by the current DamageFraction.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.Crew:
The number of crew that operate this part. The component itself acts as a toggle that reads on
while at least this many crew have arrived, which is how `IsOperational`-style
[[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartMultiToggleRules]] chains gate a weapon on its operators.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.DefaultPriority:
The base priority with which crew work this job, used whenever the player has not set a custom
priority for it in the crew management UI. Higher values win crew over lower ones.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.OverridePriorityPart:
Stores and looks up the player-set crew priority under another part's identity instead of this
part's own. Keeps priorities saved in old ships working after a part is renamed or split.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.OverridePriorityKey:
Stores and looks up the player-set crew priority under another component's id instead of this
component's own. Several crew components can share one key so they appear as a single priority
entry, and vanilla uses it to keep old saves working after a component was renamed.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.OverridePriorityName:
Stores and looks up the player-set crew priority under an arbitrary label instead of a component
id. The free-form spelling of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.OverridePriorityKey]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.PrerequisitesBeforeCrewing:
Toggles that must all be on for the part to request operators at all. While any is off for longer
than [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.PrerequisitesUnmetAbandonDelay]], the job asks for
zero crew and the current operators walk away. Vanilla weapons list their power and scorched
toggles here, so an unpowered turret does not hold crew.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.HighPriorityPrerequisites:
Toggles that must all be on for the operators to hold their station against other work. While any
is off for longer than [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.PrerequisitesUnmetAbandonDelay]],
the job's effective priority drops by 10000, so any other job (a delivery run, another part) pulls
the operators away, and they only return when nothing else needs them. The vanilla missile
launcher points this at a `ToggleProxy` whose target each missile mode supplies, so crew stand
down when the selected mode has no ammo. A proxy with no connected target reads as off, so every
toggled component set must declare the proxied component.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.CrewDestinations:
The points, in part rules coordinates, an operator walks to and stands at. Each assigned crew
picks one destination, so there should be at least [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.Crew]]
entries.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.CrewLocations:
Ids of `CrewLocation` components in this part. An arrived operator binds one and takes its
location and rotation as their standing pose, which is how gunners face their weapon. Unbound
operators just stand at their [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.CrewDestinations]] point.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.SwitchLocationInterval:
How long an arrived operator stays at one destination before picking a new one, in seconds. When
set, crew periodically rotate between the part's destinations instead of standing still.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.AbandonOnStatusSeverity: The minimum JobInterruptionSeverity for abandonment of this job, if any.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.PrerequisitesUnmetAbandonDelay:
The grace period, in seconds, before an unmet prerequisite takes effect (default 1.5). A toggle
flickering off for less than this neither dismisses the operators
([[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.PrerequisitesBeforeCrewing]]) nor drops their priority
([[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Crew.PartCrewRules.HighPriorityPrerequisites]]).

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Graphics.PartIndic.HidesIndicators: The indices of other indicators in the same PartIndicatorSpritesRules that this indicator hides.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Graphics.PartShiel.MimicFadeInStartTime: Whether the media effect fade start time should be set to the start time of Shield's effects.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ComponentTog.ID: The ID of the IPartComponentToggle to access.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ComponentTog.ToggleID: The ID of the toggle within the component, or the component's default toggle if null.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ComponentTri.ID: The ID of the IPartComponentTrigger to access.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ComponentTri.TriggerID: The ID of the trigger within the component, or the component's default trigger if null.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ComponentVal.ID: The ID of the IPartComponentValue to access.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ComponentVal.ValueID: The ID of the value within the component, or the component's default value if null.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartTileLine.OcclusionCheckInterval: The time interval between each line occlusion check and subsequent application of effects.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartTileLine.OccludedByShields: Whether other ships' shield arcs occlude the line.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartTileLine.OcclusionHitEffects: The hit effects applied to other ships intersecting a line.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartTileLine.OcclusionContinuousMediaEffects: The media effects played when other ships intersect a line.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartTileLine.EffectScaleFactor: The component providing the value that the occluded score is multiplied by to calculate the effect scale.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartTileLine.MediaIntensity: The value passed to media effects as intensity. Defaults to the effect scale if null.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartTileLine.FractionalTextValueBasis: If not null, the line score as a fraction of this value is passed as a secondary text value.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartTileLine.FractionalTextValueScaleFactor: The value that the secondary text value is scaled by.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartTileScor.Region: The region that will be included in the calculations.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartTogglePr.ProxyRules:
The proxy target: which part and component to mirror. Written flat on the proxy itself
(`ComponentID = …`, optionally `PartLocation` or `PartCriteria`), see
[[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ProxyRules]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartTogglePr.IsOnWhenNotConnected:
The toggle state this proxy reports while no target component is connected (default `false`).
Disconnected covers a `PartLocation` cell with no matching part, and equally a `ComponentID`
that no active component carries. The vanilla missile launcher relies on that: its
`MissilesPrereqProxy` targets a `MissilesPrereq` component that each `ToggledComponents`
missile mode must supply, so a mode that forgets it reads permanently off and the launcher's
crew stand down.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartToggleTr.AllowOperationalBypass: Whether the component can trigger without being operational, as long as it has no operational toggle, or the toggle is already true.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartToggleVa.BlueprintToggle: The toggle to use in place of Toggle for blueprints, if any.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartToggledC.Toggle:
The toggle that controls whether this set's `Components` exist on the part. They are added the
moment the toggle turns on and removed when it turns off, so the part behaves as if they were
never written while inactive. Invert the reading with
[[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartToggledComponentsRules.Invert]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartToggledC.Invert:
Inverts [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartToggledComponentsRules.Toggle]], so the components
exist while that toggle is off.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartToggledC.IncludeInBlueprints:
Whether the sub-components are included when rendering the ship's blueprints: never (`False`),
always (`True`), or following the toggle's current state (`Dynamic`, the vanilla missile
launcher's choice so only the selected mode's visuals appear).

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.AccumulatingToggle: The IPartComponentToggle that determines the operational state of the accumulator.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.BaseValue: The value the accumulator starts at and moves towards when toggle off.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.AccumulationRate: The rate at which the value accumulates when toggled on.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.DissipationRate: The rate at which the value moves towards BaseValue when toggled off.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.NonOperationalBehaviour: The accumulator's behaviour while it is non-operational.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.MinValue: The lowest value that the accumulator can reach.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.MaxValue: The highest value that the accumulator can reach.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.Trigger: The IPartComponentTrigger that triggers this accumulation instance.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.Value: The value to use for accumulating, dissipating, or setting on being triggered.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.Operation: Whether this trigger acts as accumulation, dissipation, or directly sets the value.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.AllowWhenOn: Whether this trigger activates when the accumulator is on.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.AllowWhenOff: Whether this trigger activates when the accumulator is off.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.AllowWhenOperational: Whether this trigger activates when the accumulator is operational.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.PartValueAcc.AllowWhenNonOperational: Whether this trigger activates when the accumulator is non-operational.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ProxyRules.PartLocation:
The cell, in this part's rules coordinates, whose occupying part is proxied. Rotation and flip
of the placed part are applied, and the proxy reconnects automatically as parts are built or
destroyed on that cell. When omitted, the proxy targets a component of its own part instead.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ProxyRules.ProxyToggle:
A toggle of this part that gates the proxy connection. While it is off the proxy disconnects
entirely, as if the target did not exist. Invert the reading with
[[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ProxyRules.InvertProxyToggle]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ProxyRules.InvertProxyToggle:
Inverts [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ProxyRules.ProxyToggle]], so the proxy connects while
that toggle is off.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ProxyRules.ProxyableComponents:
The candidate targets, checked in order against the proxied part. The first entry whose
`PartCriteria` matches (or that has none) wins, and its `ComponentID` names the component to
mirror. The flat `ComponentID = …` spelling on the proxy is the one-entry shorthand for this
list.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ProxyRules/P.PartCriteria:
A filter the candidate part must match (part id, categories, ownership relative to this part)
before this entry's [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ProxyRules+ProxyableComponent.ComponentID]]
is proxied. An entry without criteria matches any part.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.ProxyRules/P.ComponentID:
The id of the component to mirror in the targeted part. The connection is live: if the
component does not exist yet (a `ToggledComponents` mode that is switched off, a part still
under construction), the proxy waits and connects the moment a component with this id
appears, and disconnects again when it is removed.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.UIToggleMode.TargetUIToggle: The ID of the PartUIToggle to override.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.UIToggleMode.TargetMode: The mode to set the TargetUIToggle to when overridden.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.UIToggleMode.OverrideTrigger: The ID of the component that will trigger the override.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Logic.UIToggleMode.ResetTrigger: The ID of the component that will end the override, returning the toggle to its previous value.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartComponentRules.BlueprintSortOrder: Specifies the ordering of DrawBlueprintData@) relative to other components on the part. Declaration order is used to break ties.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.NameKey: The part's name, displayed when it is selected or hovered over.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.IconNameKey: The part name as it shows up in the ship editor's menus.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.JobsNameKey: The part's display name in the crew jobs UI (the role edit window). When omitted, [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.NameKey]] is shown instead. Vanilla crew quarters use it to show a generic job name like "General".
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ID: Game-wide unique ID of the part. Must start with the author's name.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.OtherIDs: Alternate part identifiers this part also answers to.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.FlipWhenLoadingIDs: Part IDs that trigger flipping when the ship is loaded.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.DontStackInEditorGroups: Editor tabs in which this part's sub-parts (see [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.EditorParentParts]]) are not collapsed into one stacked palette button. In the listed groups the part gets a plain button of its own. Vanilla armor uses this to appear un-stacked in the Structure tab.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.EditorParentParts: The IDs of parts that will contain this part as a 'sub-part' within the ship editor.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.DefaultEditorHotkey: The default key binding that selects this part for placement in the ship editor. Listing more than one key makes a key combination, e.g. `[PlatformCmdCtrl, B]`, while most parts use a single key like `[Z]`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.EditorReplacementPartID: The part automatically placed into each vacated cell when this part is deleted or built over in the ship editor. Inherited as `corridor` from `base_part.rules`. Setting it to `""` leaves the cells empty, which vanilla armor wedges do.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.DescriptionKey: Descriptive text shown in the part's tooltip, below the name and cost line.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Resources: Resource name and cost pairs defining what the part costs to build.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.IsBuildable: If false, the part cannot be built.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.FractionalCostToRepair: How much of the build cost, at most, is spent to repair the part to full health.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Size: The width and height of the part, in tiles.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.PhysicalRect: The sub-rectangle of the part's [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Size]] footprint that is physically solid, as `[x, y, width, height]` in tiles from the part's un-rotated top-left. Only these cells collide, carry mass and hull walls, and connect to neighboring parts. The remaining cells are non-solid overhang, which vanilla weapons and thrusters use for barrels and nozzles, e.g. the medium cannon's `[0, 1, 2, 1]`. Must lie within the part's size rect.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.SaveRect: Shifts the part's origin in saved ship files: the location written to and read from saves is offset by this rect's top-left instead of using the part's own top-left. Keeps previously saved ships loading correctly when a part's [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Size]] or [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.PhysicalRect]] changes. Vanilla weapons and thrusters typically set `SaveRect = &PhysicalRect`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.AllowedContiguity: The edges of the part that count as structural connections to neighboring parts when the game determines ship contiguity. A connection exists only if both parts allow the shared edge, and directions are in the part's un-rotated orientation. Wedge parts restrict this so their empty diagonal side does not connect, e.g. vanilla `armor_wedge` uses `[Right, Bottom]`. Any value other than `Sides` also makes the part incompatible with the editor's brush tools unless [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ForceBrushCompatibility]] says otherwise.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.GenerateRectCollider: Whether the part gets an automatic rectangular collider spanning its [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.RectColliderRectType]] rect, which also provides its physics cells and mass. Set to false for parts with a custom `PolygonCollider` component, as vanilla wedge and triangle parts do.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.RectColliderRectType: Which rect the automatic collider spans. `Normal` covers the full [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Size]] footprint, `Physical` only the [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.PhysicalRect]]. Ignored when [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.GenerateRectCollider]] is false.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.BoundingRectType: Which rect contributes the part's corner points to the ship's overall bounding circle. `Normal` uses the full [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Size]] footprint, `Physical` uses the [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.PhysicalRect]]. Vanilla thrusters use `Physical` so their non-solid nozzle overhang does not inflate the ship's bounds.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.PartNetworkOverlayMidpoint: The point where the blueprint resource-network overlay's route lines converge on this part, in tiles from the part's top-left corner. When omitted, the center of the part's rect is used.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.CellOccupancyFactor: The value by which to multiply the calculated collider occupancy value of cells within the part.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.SelectionPriority: Determines selection ordering when parts overlap.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.SelectionTypeID: Part ID used to group parts together for selection.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.SelectionTypeByRotation: If true, double-clicking the part in-game only selects parts of the same type facing the same direction.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.SelectionTypeRotations: Rotation offsets treated as equivalent when double-click selecting parts of the same type with [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.SelectionTypeByRotation]]. Two parts match if any pair of listed offsets brings their rotations to the same value modulo 4. Vanilla's 2-way small thruster uses `[0, 3]` so both orientations of the same placement select together.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.IgnoreRotationForMirroredSelection: If true, mirror selection in the ship editor matches the mirrored counterpart by part type and occupied cells only, ignoring rotation and flip. Set on rotationally symmetric parts like armor, corridors and storages, whose mirrored copy may be stored with a different rotation.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Density: How much one tile of the part weighs, in tons.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.MaxHealth: How much damage the part can endure before being destroyed.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.HealthType: The part's health category. See the HealthTypes reference.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ConstructionWork: Total crew-seconds of work needed to build the part. When omitted, it is derived from health as the ship's `DefaultConstructionWorkPerHealth` times [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.MaxHealth]], which in vanilla works out to one second per corridor tile.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ConstructionSwapDelay: Seconds to wait after construction reaches 100% before the under-construction placeholder is swapped for the finished part. Vanilla Terran parts use `0.2`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.WorkPerRepairedHealth: Crew-seconds of work needed to restore one point of health when repairing the part. When omitted, the ship's `DefaultWorkPerRepairedHealth` is used.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.SalvageProgressMediaEffects: Continuous visual and sound effects played at the part while it is being salvaged, with intensity rising as salvage progresses.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.SalvageProgressMediaEffectsTimeout: Seconds after the last salvage progress before [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.SalvageProgressMediaEffects]] stop playing.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ConstructionProgressMediaEffects: Continuous visual and sound effects played at the part while it is being constructed, with intensity rising as construction progresses.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ConstructionProgressMediaEffectsTimeout: Seconds after the last construction progress before [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ConstructionProgressMediaEffects]] stop playing.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ConstructionFinishedMediaEffects: One-shot effects played when construction of the part completes, just before the [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ConstructionSwapDelay]] swap to the finished part.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ExplosiveDamageAbsorption: The fraction (`0..1`) of an explosion's remaining damage this part can absorb, soaking the blast and shielding parts behind it. The range interpolates from the first value at full health to the second when nearly destroyed. Inherited as `50%` from `base_part.rules`, and vanilla armor uses `100%`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.DamageResistances: Per damage type, the fraction of incoming damage removed before it is applied: the part takes `damage * (1 - resistance)`, so `50%` halves the damage and negative values amplify it. Each range interpolates from the first value at full health to the second when nearly destroyed, and unlisted damage types have zero resistance. Vanilla armor uses `explosive = 50%` and `thermal = -25%`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.StatusResistances: The part's base resistances to status types, interpolated by the current DamageFraction.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.UnderlyingPart: Part that underlies this part and spawns in its place when it is destroyed, one instance covering the whole footprint. The underlying part's resources and cost are deducted from this part's, so it only pays the difference. Also readable as `CreatePartWhenDestroyed`. See [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.UnderlyingPartPerTile]] for the per-tile variant and [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.CreateUnderlyingWhenDestroyed]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.UnderlyingPartPerTile: Per-tile variant of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.UnderlyingPart]]: one copy of the referenced part per physical tile spawns when this part is destroyed, and its cost per tile is deducted from this part's cost. Also readable as `CreatePartPerTileWhenDestroyed`. Inherited as `structure` from `base_part.rules`, so destroyed vanilla parts leave structure behind.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.CreateUnderlyingWhenDestroyed: If false, the underlying part (see [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.UnderlyingPart]] and [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.UnderlyingPartPerTile]]) only appears when this part is removed by salvage, not when it is destroyed in combat.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.TempConstructionPartPerTile: Part temporarily placed on each physical tile as scaffolding while this part is under construction, removed when the finished part is swapped in. Vanilla structure wedges use a full `structure` tile per cell.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.CreatePartPerTileWhenGrabbed: Part placed on each physical tile when this part is grabbed (picked up or moved) in the ship editor. Inherited as `corridor` from `base_part.rules`, and vanilla armor sets `""` so grabbing it leaves nothing behind.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.DestroyedPartHealthFraction: The fraction of its max health the spawned underlying part starts with when this part is destroyed in combat, with salvage progress pulling the value toward full. Inherited as `50%` from `base_part.rules`. See [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.UnderlyingPart]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.CrewKillChanceWhenDestroyed: Chance (`0..1`) that each crew member inside the part is killed when the part is destroyed. Survivors are ejected into space at [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.CrewThrowSpeedWhenDestroyed]]. Inherited as `25%` from `base_part.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.CrewThrowSpeedWhenDestroyed: Speed added in a random direction to surviving crew ejected into space when the part is destroyed. Inherited as `10` from `base_part.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.InitialPenetrationResistance: How much of a penetrating projectile's remaining penetration budget is consumed when it first breaches the part through a wall. The range interpolates from the first value at full health to the second when nearly destroyed. See [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ContinuingPenetrationResistance]] for travel after the breach. Vanilla armor uses `7` versus the base part's `1`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ContinuingPenetrationResistance: How much of a penetrating projectile's remaining penetration budget is consumed while it continues through the part after the initial breach. The range interpolates from the first value at full health to the second when nearly destroyed. Usually lower than [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.InitialPenetrationResistance]], e.g. `.5` versus `1` in `base_part.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.CrewSpeedFactor: The speed at which crew move within the part.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.CrewCongestedSpeedFactor: The speed at which crew move within the part while passing other crew.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.CrewMinimumSpeedFactor: The minimum values to multiply crew speed by within the part.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.IsCrewSalvageable: Whether crew can salvage the part by walking up to it. If false, the part can only be salvaged with salvage weapons.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.HasNonContiguousPathing: Set to true if some tiles of the part cannot be walked across by crew in a given direction.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.BlockedTravelCells: Cells of the part, counted from its un-rotated top-left, that crew can never stand on or walk through. Usually paired with [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.HasNonContiguousPathing]] so the remaining cells form separate walkable islands. Vanilla large weapons use it to keep crew out of the mechanism.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.BlockedTravelCellDirections: Per-cell directional travel blocks. Each `Key` is a cell of the part (un-rotated, from the top-left) and each `Value` lists the directions crew may not move while in that cell, so unlike [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.BlockedTravelCells]] the other directions stay open. Directions rotate and flip with the part.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ForceManhattanPathInPart: If true, crew heading to a specific spot inside the part must path all the way to that exact cell instead of stopping at the first reachable cell of the part. Vanilla pairs it with [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.BlockedTravelCells]] on large weapons to funnel crew along a fixed interior route.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ProhibitRects: Rectangular keep-out zones the part projects, each a category plus an `[x, y, width, height]` rect in tiles from the part's un-rotated top-left, typically extending outside its footprint. Parts listing the category in their [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ProhibitedBy]] cannot be placed or built inside the zone. The zones rotate and flip with the part, and the [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Prohibits]] shorthands generate entries here.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ProhibitedBy: The prohibit categories this part respects: it cannot be placed or built where another part projects a keep-out zone (see [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ProhibitRects]]) of a listed category. This is a hard placement block, not a warning. Inherited as `[default]` from `base_part.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ContiguityOriginPriority: When a ship breaks into disconnected pieces, the piece containing the highest-priority part is kept as the surviving origin ship, with ties broken by largest total area. Vanilla control rooms set 1 to 3 by size so the piece holding the best control room stays the ship.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.IsExternal: If false, the part must have either no doors to anything else or only doors with legal placement.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.IsRotateable: Whether the part can be rotated during placement.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.IsFlippable: Whether the part can be flipped during placement.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.IsWalled: If false, the part can only have doors to parts that are themselves not walled.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.IsSelfDestructible: Whether the ship's self-destruct command destroys this part. Vanilla armor sets it to false, so plating survives a self-destruct.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.IsLowPriorityDeconstruction: If true, crew only deconstruct this part when no other construction, repair or normal deconstruction job needs doing. Vanilla airlocks set it so they are removed last.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ForceBrushCompatibility: Overrides whether the part can be drawn with the editor's line, rectangle and fill brush tools. When omitted, a part qualifies automatically if it is 1x1, has [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.AllowedContiguity]] `Sides`, and its [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.AllowedDoorLocations]] is unset or empty.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.AllowedDoorLocations: Cells just outside the part, in un-rotated coordinates from its top-left, that doors to this part may connect to. For example a 1x1 airlock lists `[0, 1]`, the cell directly below it. Omitting the field allows doors on every side-adjacent cell, while an empty list `[]` forbids doors entirely, as vanilla armor and structure do.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.MaxDoors: The maximum number of doors that may border the part, enforced both when placing doors manually and by the automatic door generator. The default means no limit.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.GeneratorRequiresDoor: If true, the procedural ship generator only places the part where at least one door can be created for it, so generated crew-operated parts are never sealed off. Inherited as true from `base_part.rules`, with armor and structure turning it off.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.AutoDoorMaxPathLength: Radius, in walkable interior cells, within which the automatic door generator will not add another door near an existing one on this part. Inherited as `6` from `base_part.rules`. `0` lets auto-doors be placed as densely as possible.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.NoAutoDoors: Excludes the part from automatic door creation, both as a source and as a target, and also suppresses the door-access warning. Doors can still be placed manually.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.DoorAccessWarningLevel: How the ship editor warns when crew have no walkable path from this part to the ship exterior. `Strong` shows the full no-access warning, `Weak` a softer variant, and `None` skips the check. Purely a warning, it never blocks building.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ExternalWalls: The sides of the part that count as solid hull. A wall sprite renders against each listed side from the neighboring cell whenever that cell is empty space or an open side of another part (see [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.InternalWalls]]). Directions are in the part's un-rotated orientation and rotate and flip with it. Inherited as `[All]` from `base_part.rules`, so a part that says nothing is walled on every side.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.BlueprintExternalWalls: The blueprint-view counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ExternalWalls]]. When omitted, the blueprint uses `ExternalWalls`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ExternalWallsByCell: Per-cell override of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ExternalWalls]] for multi-cell parts. Each `Key` is a cell of the part (`[0, 0]` is the top-left cell) and each `Value` lists the sides of that cell that count as solid hull, with `[]` meaning no walls there. Cells without an entry keep the whole-part `ExternalWalls` value.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.BlueprintExternalWallsByCell: The blueprint-view counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ExternalWallsByCell]]. When omitted, the blueprint uses `ExternalWallsByCell`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.InternalWalls: The sides of the part's cells that are visually open, so a wall may render inside this part's own cell along them. A wall appears on a listed side only when the neighbor on that side declares the shared edge solid via its [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ExternalWalls]]. Inherited as `[None]` from `base_part.rules` (fully enclosed room). Angled armor lists its open wedge sides here, and open-platform structure uses `[All]`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.BlueprintInternalWalls: The blueprint-view counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.InternalWalls]]. When omitted, the blueprint uses `InternalWalls`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.InternalWallsByCell: Per-cell override of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.InternalWalls]] for multi-cell parts, in the same `Key`/`Value` form as [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ExternalWallsByCell]]. Cells without an entry keep the whole-part `InternalWalls` value.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.BlueprintInternalWallsByCell: The blueprint-view counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.InternalWallsByCell]]. When omitted, the blueprint uses `InternalWallsByCell`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.VirtualInternalCells: Pairs of `ExternalCell`/`InternalCell` (in un-rotated cells relative to the part) declaring that the two cells are joined as interior, which suppresses the exterior hull wall between them. Vanilla wedge armor and structure use it so sloped parts merge visually with their diagonal neighbor. Not applied while the part is under construction.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.TypeCategories: Part-category identifiers used to classify the part.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.RequiresCategories: Part categories of which the ship must contain at least one part, otherwise this part is flagged invalid in the ship editor and blueprint. Provider parts list the dependent categories in [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.AffectsValidityOfCategories]] so the flag updates promptly.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.AffectsValidityOfCategories: Part categories whose members get their validity re-checked whenever this part is added or removed. Set on parts that other parts depend on through [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.RequiresCategories]], so their invalid markers update immediately.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.SignificanceToggle: ID of the IPartComponentToggle that determines whether this part should be considered for determining ship 'significance'.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.EditorIcon: The icon that represents the part on build-palette buttons and in other UI such as the parts card and the jobs window. This is a UI image only, not the in-world sprite.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.JobsIcon: Sprite shown for the part's job assignments.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.FlipHRotate: The rotation mapping applied when the part is flipped horizontally.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.FlipVRotate: The rotation mapping applied when the part is flipped vertically.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.AIValueFactor: How much the AI wants to destroy this part on ships it considers enemies.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ReceivableBuffs: The buff types this part can receive from buff-providing parts, with received values clamped by [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.MinBuffValues]] and [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.MaxBuffValues]]. Vanilla thrusters list `Engine` and related buffs, and an empty list `[]` disables buff reception.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.MinBuffValues: Per-buff minimum values this part clamps received buffs to.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.MaxBuffValues: Per-buff maximum values this part clamps received buffs to.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Stats: Stat lines shown in the part's stats tooltip. Each entry maps a stat ID to the number (or numbers) substituted into that stat's localized format string, so units and meaning come from the stat definition, not from this field. Lines render in the order the stats are declared game-wide, and [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.StatsByCategory]] can group further lines under headings.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Features: Feature tags listed under the "Features" header of the part's stats tooltip. Each ID resolves to a localized label from the game-wide part-feature list, falling back to the raw ID string.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.StatsByCategory: Groups of stat lines rendered under their own named heading in the part's stats tooltip, after the top-level [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Stats]]. Each entry holds a `NameKey`, a `Stats` map and optional `Features`, which vanilla uses for example to show one block per firing mode of the cannon deck.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.StatsNotesKey: Footnote text appended at the very bottom of the part's stats tooltip, after all [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Stats]] and [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.StatsByCategory]] lines.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.SecondaryToolTip: An extra tooltip panel with its own title, description, stats and features, shown next to the main part tooltip when the player has secondary tooltips enabled in the settings. Vanilla's cannon deck uses it for a second stats block.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.FractionalNonPlayerRefund: The fraction of the part's cost refunded when a non-player ship sells or refunds it in Career mode. The player always gets the full cost back. Inherited as 25% from `base_part.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.EditorGroups: The names of the ship-editor tabs the part appears in.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.EditorGroup: The name of the ship-editor tab the part appears in.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ExplosiveDamageResistance: Shorthand that stores the value as the `Explosive` entry of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.DamageResistances]] at load time. It has no effect of its own beyond that, and an explicit `DamageResistances` entry for `Explosive` takes precedence.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Prohibits: The prohibit categories used by the [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ProhibitLeft]], [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ProhibitRight]], [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ProhibitAbove]] and [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ProhibitBelow]] shorthands, which add one keep-out rect per listed category. Must be set whenever any of those fields is used. Inherited as `[default]` from `base_part.rules`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ProhibitLeft: Shorthand that adds a keep-out zone extending this many tiles beyond the part's left edge (un-rotated), spanning its full height, for every category in [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Prohibits]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ProhibitRight: Shorthand that adds a keep-out zone extending this many tiles beyond the part's right edge (un-rotated), spanning its full height, for every category in [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Prohibits]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ProhibitAbove: Shorthand that adds a keep-out zone extending this many tiles beyond the part's top edge (un-rotated), spanning its full width, for every category in [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Prohibits]]. Vanilla cannons use it for muzzle clearance, e.g. `ProhibitAbove = 2` on the large cannon.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.ProhibitBelow: Shorthand that adds a keep-out zone extending this many tiles beyond the part's bottom edge (un-rotated), spanning its full width, for every category in [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.PartRules.Prohibits]]. Vanilla thrusters use it for exhaust clearance, e.g. `ProhibitBelow = 3` on the small thruster.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Resources.BaseReso.ToggleOnResources:
The storage doubles as a toggle, and this is the fill level that switches it on: it reads on
once the stored amount reaches this many resources, and off again only when the amount falls
to [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Resources.BaseResourceStorageRules.ToggleOffResources]] or below.
The gap between the two is a hysteresis band that stops the toggle from flickering while
resources stream in and out. Vanilla missile modes set it to one shot's worth of parts, so the
toggle means "has enough to fire".
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Resources.BaseReso.ToggleOffResources:
The fill level that switches the storage's toggle off again: it reads off once the stored
amount falls to this many resources or below, after having reached
[[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Resources.BaseResourceStorageRules.ToggleOnResources]]. Usually set
one below the on threshold.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Resources.Resource.TriggerThreshold: The resource threshold at which the storage triggers and consumes the threshold amount from its stores. If set to 0, the storage will trigger as soon as it has any resources, consuming all of them.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Resources.Resource.QuantityScale: The amount to scale the proxied storage's quantities by. 1 resource in the proxied storage is worth QuantityScale resources in the proxy. Any resource operations on the proxy are divided by the scale and rounded away from 0.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Resources.Resource.IgnoreResourcesForInitToMax: Whether the current resource level is ignored when initializing the storage to 'max'.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.UI.BasePartProgres.ShowProgressFor: The IPartComponentValue component to use as the current bar value.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.UI.BasePartProgres.Invert: Whether to invert the displayed progress value.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.UI.PartSegmentedPr.SegmentCount: The number of equally sized segments the bar will be split into.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.UI.PartSegmentedPr.PerSegmentColor: Whether the progress bar colors will be calculated per-segment, or for the whole bar.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.UI.PartUIToggleRul.VisibilityToggle: ID of the toggle that determines whether this UIToggle's button is shown when its part is selected. If this references another PartUIToggleRules, it additionally controls visibility in blueprint mode.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.Range: The maximum length of the beam, in tiles. Objects beyond this distance cannot be hit, except that an object the beam is already hitting stays valid out to [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.StretchRange]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.StretchRange: An extended maximum length, in tiles, that applies once the beam is hitting something, letting it hold onto a target that drifts beyond [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.Range]]. New objects must still first be hit within Range. When set, it also replaces Range as the full length used by the OverRange scaling fields. The vanilla tractor beam grabs at 300 and stretches to 600.
