Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.IdealRange: The attack distance, in tiles, the ship AI tries to hold, interpolated between its Min and Max based on the target's radius relative to [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.IdealRadius]]. Falls back to [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.Range]] when unset.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.IdealRadius: The target radius span, in tiles, mapped onto [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.IdealRange]]. Targets no larger than the Min radius use the ideal range's Min, targets at least the Max radius use its Max, with interpolation in between.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ClipRangeToTarget: Limits the beam's length to the distance to its current target, so the beam ends at the target instead of continuing on to full [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.Range]]. The vanilla flak cannon uses this so its [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.HitNothing]] burst detonates at the target's distance.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.RandomOffsetRadius: The radius, in tiles, of a circle around the beam's endpoint within which the actual endpoint is randomly scattered. Interpolated from Min to Max as the beam's length approaches full range, so the scatter grows with distance.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.Width: The collision thickness of the beam, in tiles. Half of it is also reported as the emitted radius for targeting purposes. The visual width comes from [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.BeamMediaEffects]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.SecondaryWidth: The width of the beam's secondary collision check. The closer hit of the two checks is used for effects, allowing for 'proximity' hits with a more narrow criteria.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.SecondaryFilter: The filter used for the beam's secondary collision check, if SecondaryWidth is greater than 0.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.SecondaryOverrideFilter: The filter, if any, that the primary collision result is checked against in order to abort the secondary collision check.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ShieldCollisions: The filter deciding whose shields block the beam. Defaults to enemy shields plus the shields of a targeted friendly ship, never the emitting ship's own. Shields that pass the filter stop the beam and receive [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.HitShield]]. The vanilla tractor beam sets `Shields = false` to pass through all shields.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.CrewCollisions: The filter deciding which crew floating in space the beam can hit. All crew collisions are off by default. Crew that pass the filter stop the beam and receive [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.HitCrew]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.AttenuatorCollisions: The filter deciding which beam-attenuating projectiles the beam collides with. Defaults to enemy projectiles. Only relevant when [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.AttenuationLimit]] is set.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.FriendlyShipLowCollisions: Whether the beam collides with the low part colliders of ships it cannot damage, including the emitting ship itself. Set to false to let the beam pass over low parts of friendly ships, as the vanilla resonance beam does. Also accepted under the alias `SourceShipLowCollisions`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.FriendlyShipHighCollisions: Whether the beam collides with the high part colliders of ships it cannot damage, including the emitting ship itself. Also accepted under the alias `SourceShipHighCollisions`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.NonFriendlyShipLowCollisions: Whether the beam collides with the low part colliders of ships it can damage. Set to false to make the beam pass over enemy low parts and only hit their high colliders.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.NonFriendlyShipHighCollisions: Whether the beam collides with the high part colliders of ships it can damage.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.PenetratesStructure: Whether the beam passes through bare structure and continues to whatever lies behind, still applying [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.HitStructural]] to the structure it crosses. When false the beam stops at the first structure hit, as the vanilla mining and tractor beams do.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.UseHitNormalAsWorldDirection: Uses the hit surface's normal, pointed into the surface, as the world direction for hit effects instead of the beam's actual travel direction. Hit effects then behave as if the beam struck the surface head-on.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.AntiPhasingRaycastDistance: The distance backwards that the beam raycast is extended to help detect fixtures overlapping the emitter. Collisions detected within this distance will register at the beam emission location.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ColliderOverlapTest: Whether a separate test is performed to detect overlap with a collider at the emission point.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.AttenuationLimit: The strength the beam pits against beam-attenuating projectiles such as the overclocked flak cannon's flak field. The beam stops at such a projectile, drains up to this amount from its attenuation damage pool, and applies [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.HitAttenuator]]. When unset the beam ignores attenuators entirely.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.HitAttenuator: The hit effects and media effects applied at the point where the beam strikes a beam-attenuating projectile. Only used when [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.AttenuationLimit]] is set.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.HitOperational: The hit effects and media effects applied at the point where the beam strikes an operational (non-structure) part.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.HitShield: The hit effects and media effects applied at the point where the beam strikes a shield that passes [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.ShieldCollisions]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.HitStructural: The hit effects and media effects applied at the point where the beam strikes structure. With [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.PenetratesStructure]] true these are applied to the structure the beam passes through while it continues on.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.HitCrew: The hit effects and media effects applied when the beam strikes crew in space. Requires [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.CrewCollisions]] to allow crew hits.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.HitNothing: The hit effects and media effects applied at the beam's endpoint when it hits nothing at all. The vanilla flak cannon uses this to detonate its burst in open space.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ConditionalEffectToggle: A toggle component on the same part that gates the [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules+BeamHitRules.ConditionalHitEffects]] and [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules+BeamHitRules.ConditionalMediaEffects]] of the Hit groups. While the toggle is on, those effects are applied in addition to the normal ones.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.Duration: Seconds the beam stays on after each trigger. Triggering again while emitting extends the emission back up to this duration, so a repeatedly fired beam becomes continuous. At 0, the default, the beam is instantaneous and applies its hit effects once per shot.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.HitInterval: Seconds between successive applications of the beam's hit effects while a continuous beam is emitting. Only meaningful when [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.Duration]] is greater than 0.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.KeepLengthWhenHitNothing: Keeps the beam at its previous length when it stops hitting anything, instead of instantly extending to full range.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ResourceUsageScaleOverRange: Multiplier on the emitter's resource usage, interpolated from Min to Max as the beam's current length goes from 0 to full range. Defaults to a constant 1. [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.InvertRangeScaling]] reverses the direction.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.HitEffectsScaleOverRange: Multiplier on the scale of all hit effects, interpolated from Min to Max as the beam's current length goes from 0 to full range. The vanilla ion beam uses `[1, 80%]` to deal reduced damage at long range. Defaults to a constant 1.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ValueOverRange: Multiplier on the value this emitter reports to other components (for example through a `MultiValue`), interpolated from Min to Max as the beam's current length goes from 0 to full range. Defaults to a constant 1.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.MediaEffectsScaleOverRange: Multiplier on the intensity of the beam's media effects, interpolated from Min to Max as the beam's current length goes from 0 to full range. Defaults to a constant 1.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ResourceUsageScaleOverRampUp: Multiplier on the emitter's resource usage, interpolated from Min to Max as the beam ramps up over [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.RampUpTime]]. Defaults to `[0, 1]`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.HitEffectsScaleOverRampUp: Multiplier on the scale of all hit effects, interpolated from Min to Max as the beam ramps up over [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.RampUpTime]]. Defaults to `[0, 1]`, so damage fades in during ramp-up.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ValueOverRampUp: Multiplier on the value this emitter reports to other components, interpolated from Min to Max as the beam ramps up over [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.RampUpTime]]. Defaults to `[0, 1]`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.MediaEffectsScaleOverRampUp: Multiplier on the intensity of the beam's media effects, interpolated from Min to Max as the beam ramps up over [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.RampUpTime]]. Defaults to `[0, 1]`, so the visuals fade in during ramp-up.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.InvertRangeScaling: Reverses the interpolation of the four OverRange scaling fields, so their Min applies at full beam length and their Max at zero length.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.EffectsScaleFactor: A value component on the same part whose current value is used as the beam's effect scale, feeding every modifiable with an `EffectScaleExponent` such as hit damage, spread, and media effect intensity. The vanilla ion beam prism points this at its stored ion energy so beam power tracks accumulated energy.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ValueScaleFactor: A value component on the same part whose current value multiplies the value this emitter reports to other components, together with [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.ValueOverRange]] and [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.ValueOverRampUp]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.UpdateScaleFactorsPerEmit: When true, [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.EffectsScaleFactor]] and [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.ValueScaleFactor]] are sampled only at the moment the beam emits. When false, the default, they are re-read every tick while a continuous beam is firing.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.RampUpTime: Seconds for the beam to ramp up to full strength after it starts firing. While ramping, the four OverRampUp scaling fields interpolate from their Min to their Max. At 0, the default, the beam is always at full ramp-up.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.RequireHitForRampUp: Makes ramp-up count only time spent actually hitting something. The ramp starts at the first hit and drains back down over the same time span once the beam stops hitting, as used by the vanilla tractor beam.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.InitHitLengthResetDelay: Seconds after the beam loses its hit during which its initial hit length is remembered and the emission is kept alive, letting the beam re-acquire the target. While remembered, [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.StretchRange]] continues to apply.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.BeamMediaEffects: The media effects that render the beam itself, played continuously while a continuous beam is on or as a one-shot for instantaneous beams. Their beam length parameter follows the beam's actual length plus [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.BeamMediaEffectsLengthOffset]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.BeamMediaEffectsOffset: The offset, in tiles relative to the anchoring component, at which [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.BeamMediaEffects]] are attached. The vanilla manipulator beam uses this to start the visual at its muzzle.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.BeamMediaEffectsIntensity: Multiplier on the intensity of the beam, start, and end media effects, on top of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.MediaEffectsScaleOverRange]] and [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.MediaEffectsScaleOverRampUp]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.AttachBeamMediaEffectsTo: A component on the same part that the beam media effects anchor to instead of the emitter itself. The vanilla ion beam prism attaches them to its interpolated turret so the visual follows the turret's rendered rotation.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ColorRed: The red channel of a tint applied to all of the beam's media effects. Channels left unset default to 1, and setting any of the four channels enables the custom tint. Being modifiable, it can be driven by buffs, as the vanilla resonance beam does with its thermal buffs.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ColorGreen: The green channel of the beam's media effect tint. See [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.ColorRed]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ColorBlue: The blue channel of the beam's media effect tint. See [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.ColorRed]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ColorAlpha: The alpha channel of the beam's media effect tint. See [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.ColorRed]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.BeamMediaEffectsLengthOffset: A distance, in tiles, added to the beam's actual length when setting the visual beam length. Negative values shorten the visual, as the vanilla manipulator beam does to compensate for its muzzle offset.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.Spread: A random angular deviation added to the beam's aim direction, rolled between Min and Max each time the beam updates. Angles are written with a `d` suffix for degrees, for example `[-1.5d, 1.5d]`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.PelletSpread: An extra angular deviation applied per pellet when [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.EmitterRules.Pellets]] emits more than one, rolled between Min and Max for each pellet. With [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.EvenPelletSpread]] the pellets are spaced across the range instead.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.EvenPelletSpread: Spaces the pellets evenly across the [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitterRules.PelletSpread]] range instead of rolling each pellet's angle at random.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.StartMediaEffects: One-shot media effects played when a continuous beam starts emitting, such as the vanilla tractor beam's start sound.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.EndMediaEffects: One-shot media effects played when a continuous beam stops emitting.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ConditionalHitEffects: Hit effects performed only when the ConditionalEffectToggle is true.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BeamEmitte.ConditionalMediaEffects: Media effects played only when the ConditionalEffectToggle is true.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.BulletEmit.TriggerValueIsShotOffsetTime: Whether the emitter fakes an emission time offset using the value from the FireTrigger.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.FiringArc: Total width, in degrees, of the arc the turret can rotate through, centered on its mount direction. `360d` lets it spin all the way around.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.TargetingRange: Maximum distance, in tiles, at which the turret searches for new targets. Defaults to [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeaponRules.ValidRange]] if that is set, otherwise to the targeting emitter's range multiplied by the ship's [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultTargetingRangeFactor]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.TargetingArc: Width, in degrees, of the arc within which the turret searches for new targets. Defaults to [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeaponRules.ValidArc]] if that is set, otherwise to [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeaponRules.FiringArc]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.ValidRange: Maximum distance, in tiles, at which an acquired target remains valid to keep and fire at. Defaults to the targeting emitter's current range.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.ValidArc: Width, in degrees, of the arc within which an acquired target remains valid to keep and fire at. Defaults to [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeaponRules.FiringArc]], and missile launchers widen it to `360d` beyond their launch arc.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.RotateSpeed: Degrees per second the turret rotates toward its desired aim direction.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.ReturnToCenter: With nothing to aim at, the turret rotates back to its centered rest direction. When false it stays at whatever rotation it last had, as on the manipulator beam.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.FireThresholdAngle: Maximum angular distance, in degrees, between the turret's current facing and the aim direction for it to be willing to fire. Vanilla turrets use small values like `1d`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.AllowFireWithBlockedLOS: Skips all line-of-sight checks, letting the turret target and fire even when the path to the target is obstructed. Missile launchers enable it.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.AllowFireInFogOfWar: Lets the turret keep targets that are outside its team's sight. When false a target hidden in fog of war is invalid.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.BlueprintArcSprite: Sprite for the firing-arc indicator fanned out in front of the part while it is being placed in build mode. The fan spans [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeaponRules.ValidArc]] if set, otherwise [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeaponRules.FiringArc]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.BlueprintArcRadius: Radius, in tiles, of the build-mode arc indicator drawn with [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeaponRules.BlueprintArcSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.BlueprintArcSpriteSegments: Number of quad segments the build-mode arc indicator is subdivided into. Vanilla turrets use 64.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.CoverageSectorArc: Renderer for the coverage sector shown when the weapon's coverage is displayed during commands. The sector spans [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeaponRules.ValidArc]] (or [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeaponRules.FiringArc]]) and extends to the weapon's standard range.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.TargetSearchesPerSecond: How many times per second the turret runs a search for a new target. Defaults to 2, and the flak cannon raises it to 8.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.InstantSearchesAfterTargetLost: Number of search passes run immediately when the turret loses its target, instead of waiting for the next scheduled search. The chaingun uses 5.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.TargetChecksPerSearch: How many candidate targets each search validates before giving up. The manipulator beam checks 10 per search.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.HoldTargetWhileFiring: Seconds the turret's rotation stays frozen after the weapon toggles on, so it does not swing away while firing. The timer is refreshed for as long as the weapon stays toggled on.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.HoldTargetAfterCancel: Seconds the turret holds its current rotation after losing its target instead of immediately swinging elsewhere. Laser blasters tie it to their emitter's `FireDelay`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.AllowRotationToggle: The component ID of a toggle component. While that toggle is off the turret may not rotate, either freezing in place or returning to center depending on [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeaponRules.ResetRotationIfNotAllowed]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.ResetRotationIfNotAllowed: When rotation is disallowed by [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeaponRules.AllowRotationToggle]], the turret rotates back to its centered direction instead of freezing at its current rotation.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.TargetPathThickness: Thickness, in tiles, of the line-of-sight ray used when checking whether the path to the target is blocked. Point defense sets it to match its bullet's size so shots need a wide enough clear corridor.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.AutoTargets: Ordered list of automatic targeting behaviors the turret tries, first entry first, until one yields a target. Each entry can be gated by its own [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponAutoTargetRules.Toggle]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.AutoTargetCrewSafetyDist: When auto-targeting crew in space, a candidate is skipped if any allied crew member is within this distance, in tiles, of it. Vanilla anti-crew weapons use 8.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.PreventOverlappingTargets: Prevents two turrets that both set this flag from auto-targeting the same object at once. The manipulator beam uses it.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.AimIncludesEmitterOffset: Compensates the aim direction for the emitter's offset from the turret's pivot so the shot itself lines up with the target. The chaingun disables it and simply points the turret at the target.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.TurretWeap.AllowAimAtProhibitedTarget: Lets the turret acquire and track targets that [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRules.ProhibitTargetPartsFilter]] prohibits, without treating them as something to fire at. The ion beam prism enables this.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.FireInterval: Seconds between shots. A newly built weapon starts fully unloaded and must wait one full interval before its first shot.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.FirePermissionToggle: The component ID of a toggle component that gates firing. While that toggle is off the weapon holds fire even when it wants to shoot.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.TargetingEmitter: The component ID of the emitter whose range and aim prediction drive this weapon's targeting checks. When neither this nor [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRules.Emitter]] is set, the weapon assumes infinite range and aims straight at the target's center.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.ResourceCheckEmitters: The component IDs of emitters whose resource supply gates firing. The weapon holds fire unless at least one listed emitter has the resources it needs to shoot, or every one of them when [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRules.ResourceCheckRequiresAllEmitters]] is true.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.ResourceCheckRequiresAllEmitters: Requires every emitter in [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRules.ResourceCheckEmitters]] to have its resources before the weapon fires, instead of just one of them.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.AIFirepowerRating: The weapon's contribution to its ship's total firepower score, which the AI uses to judge how threatening a ship is. Vanilla weapons point this at the part's damage-per-second stat.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.AddAIFirepowerRatingsWhenTargetedByPartC: Part categories of other weapons on the same ship whose firepower rating is added to this weapon's own rating while they are targeting this part. The ion prism lists `[ion_beam_emitter, ion_beam_prism]` so the beams feeding it count toward its rating.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.CanBeGivenExplicitTarget: Whether the player can give this weapon explicit targets. When false the weapon is skipped by all target-assignment input and never receives ship-wide targets.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.AllowShipWideExplicitTargets: Whether the weapon picks up ship-wide explicit targets given by right-clicking enemy parts with no specific weapon selected. Ignored unless [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRules.CanBeGivenExplicitTarget]] is true.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.AllowShipWideExplicitTargetsToggle: The component ID of a toggle component. When set, the weapon only accepts ship-wide explicit targets while that toggle is on, as with the flak cannon's fire-preference toggle.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.ShowExplicitTarget: Whether the weapon's explicit targets are drawn in the world with a reticle and target line.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.ShowTargetButtons: Whether the target buttons for this weapon appear in the part's UI card. Ignored unless [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRules.CanBeGivenExplicitTarget]] is true.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.AllowDirectControl: Whether the weapon can be aimed and fired in direct control mode.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.AllowDirectControlToggle: The component ID of a toggle component. When set, direct control only drives this weapon while that toggle is on.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.DefaultDirectControlBinding: The zero-based index of the direct control fire button that fires this weapon. Turreted weapons default to 0 and fixed weapons to 1, while vanilla assigns 2 to the tractor beam and 3 to the mine launcher.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.CanSalvage: Counts the weapon as a salvaging weapon, like the mining laser. A ship with an operational salvaging weapon can be ordered to salvage parts that are not crew-salvageable.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.IsDefensiveOnly: Marks the weapon as purely defensive, like point defense. It is excluded from firepower estimation and does not count when the game checks whether a crippled ship still has significant weapons.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.IncludeInFirepowerEstimation: Whether the weapon counts toward firepower estimation, which attack commands use to position the ship so its weapons bear on the target and the AI uses for its total firepower score. Also controls whether the weapon's coverage area is drawn while giving commands.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.EstimateFirepowerToggle: The component ID of a toggle component. When set, the weapon only counts toward firepower estimation while that toggle is on.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.AutoFireToggle: The component ID of the toggle that enables autonomous fire. The weapon only wants to fire on its own while that toggle is on.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.AlwaysShowCoverage: Draws the weapon's coverage area while giving commands even when the weapon is excluded from firepower estimation. Used by the mining laser and the flak cannon.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.IgnoreFriendlyShipLowLOSChecks: Lets the weapon's line-of-sight rays pass through the low colliders of ships it cannot damage, so it can shoot over low parts of friendly ships like the deck cannon does. Also accepted under the older name `IgnoreSourceShipLowLOSChecks`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.IgnoreFriendlyShipHighLOSChecks: Lets the weapon's line-of-sight rays pass through even the high colliders of ships it cannot damage, so friendly ships never block its fire. The manipulator beam enables this together with [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRules.IgnoreFriendlyShipLowLOSChecks]]. Also accepted under the older name `IgnoreSourceShipHighLOSChecks`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.IgnoreFriendlyStructureLOSChecks: Lets the weapon's line-of-sight rays pass through structure cells of ships it cannot damage, so friendly structure does not block fire.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.IgnoreJunkLOSChecks: Junk debris never blocks this weapon's line of sight, even junk whose [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.JunkBlocksWeaponLOS]] setting says it should.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.AllowEnemyStructureAutoTargeting: Lets auto-targeting pick structure cells on enemy ships. When false, enemy structure is only targeted once the ship has no operational parts left. FixedWeapon flips this default to true.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.ProhibitTargetPartsFilter: Filter for parts the weapon refuses to target. The ion beam emitter uses it to avoid force-firing on friendly ion prisms.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.ProhibitShipRelativePointTargets: Refuses explicit targets that are points pinned relative to a ship rather than actual parts.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.SuppressShipWideExplicitTargetsWhenTarge: While the weapon's current target matches this filter, it stops accepting ship-wide explicit targets, so whole-ship attack orders do not override a special assignment such as a friendly ion prism.
While the weapon is targeting a ship-relative point, it stops accepting ship-wide explicit targets.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.SuppressDirectControlWhenTargetingPartsF: While the weapon's current target matches this filter, direct control ignores the weapon so manual fire does not override the assignment.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.SuppressDirectControlWhenTargetingShipRe: While the weapon is targeting a ship-relative point, direct control ignores it.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.SuppressFirepowerEstimationWhenTargeting: Excludes the weapon from firepower estimation while its current target matches this filter, for example an ion beam aimed into a friendly prism.
Excludes the weapon from firepower estimation while it is targeting a ship-relative point. The ion prism uses this so the same prism is not counted for both its turret mode and its fixed mode.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.SuppressFirepowerEstimationWhenNotTarget: Excludes the weapon from firepower estimation unless it is targeting a ship-relative point. The counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRules.SuppressFirepowerEstimationWhenTargetingShipRelativePoints]] used by a part's chained or fixed mode.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.SaveSelfTargetPartCategories: Part categories for which a target on the weapon's own ship is saved with the ship design and restored on load. Ion beams and prisms use it to keep beam-into-prism setups intact.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.SaveShipRelativeTargets: Saves the weapon's ship-relative point targets with the ship design so they are restored on load.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.ToggleOnMode: The condition under which this weapon counts as on when other components read it as a toggle source. Defaults to `WantsAndReadyToFire`, meaning the weapon both wants to fire and is reloaded with resources and permission to shoot.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.OverrideTargetNineSlice: Replaces the standard rectangle drawn around this weapon's explicit targets in the targeting overlay.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.OverrideTargetCircle: Replaces the standard circle drawn around this weapon's explicit targets in the targeting overlay.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.OverrideTargetLine: Replaces the standard line drawn from the weapon to its explicit target, and between queued targets, in the targeting overlay.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.OverrideReticleSprite: Replaces the standard reticle sprite drawn on this weapon's explicit targets in the targeting overlay.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.Emitter: Shorthand that registers one emitter as both the [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRules.TargetingEmitter]], when that is not set explicitly, and an entry in [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRules.ResourceCheckEmitters]]. Most vanilla weapons set only this field.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRule.ResourceCheckEmitter: Shorthand that adds one more emitter to [[Cosmoteer.Ships.Parts.Weapons.WeaponRules.ResourceCheckEmitters]].

Cosmoteer.Ships.ScaledEffectScaleModifie.Basis: The value that Scale will scale the EffectScale towards.

Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipFile.FilePath: The path of the saved ship file.

Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ID: The unique identifier of this ship type, which must be in the form `author_name.ship_name`. Ship saves and game modes reference the ship type through this ID.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.OtherIDs: Additional IDs that resolve to this same ship type. Lets a renamed ship type keep loading saves and references that still use an old ID.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ShipScale: Multiplies the world-space size of the ship's tiles, scaling part sprites and physical sizes. Also caps how many crew can work the same construction, repair, or salvage job at the whole-number part of this value.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.NameKey: Localization key for the ship type's display name, shown for example when choosing a ship class for a new ship.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.Parts: Every part type that can exist on ships of this type. Each part's ID must be unique within the ship.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.Doors: The door types available on ships of this type. Each door's ID must be unique within the ship.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultDoorID: The door type used when doors are auto-created or when assigning a type to doors from a legacy ship save. Must match an entry in [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.Doors]], or be empty for ships without doors.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ExternalWalls: Sprite rules for the exterior walls drawn automatically around the outside of the ship's interior.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ExternalRoofWalls: Sprite rules for the exterior walls drawn on the ship's roof layer, using the same format as [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ExternalWalls]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MaxBorders: The rectangle of tile coordinates parts may be built within. The build tools reject parts outside it, imported PNG blueprints are checked against its size, and recentering centers the blueprint inside it. Vanilla allows -60 to 60 on both axes.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MaxAbsLinearVelocity: Hard cap on the ship's linear speed. The physics engine clamps the ship body's velocity to this magnitude. Vanilla ships use `Infinity`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MaxAbsAngularVelocity: Hard cap on the ship's rotation speed, applied to the physics body's angular velocity.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.RenderLayers: The render layers ship sprites can draw into, keyed by layer ID. Each layer's sprites are packed into texture atlases, and layers are drawn in sorted order within their render stage.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.Blueprints: Rules for rendering the ship's blueprint view, including the blueprint materials, atlas parameters, and blueprint external walls.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.Redprints: Rules for rendering redprints, the red overlay used for invalid blueprint parts, including its material and the repair indicator sprites.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.LinearDrag: The drag solver applied to the ship body's linear velocity, slowing the ship when it is not thrusting. Omitting it gives the ship no linear drag. Vanilla uses an `Exponential` solver.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AngularDrag: The drag solver applied to the ship body's angular velocity, slowing its rotation. Omitting it gives the ship no angular drag. Vanilla uses a `Viscous` solver.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.Restitution: Bounciness of the ship's collision fixtures. 0 makes collisions absorb all bounce, higher values make the ship rebound off other objects.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.RotationalInertiaFactor: Multiplier on the rotational inertia computed from the ship's mass distribution. Higher values make the ship slower to rotate.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ThrusterUpdateInterval: Seconds between recalculations of the ship's thruster activations, drawn at random from this range so ships don't all update on the same tick. Ignored while the ship's speed is inside [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ThrusterUpdateIntervalOverrideSpeedRange]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ThrusterUpdateIntervalOverrideSpeedRange: Speed range within which thruster activations are recalculated every physics tick instead of waiting for [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ThrusterUpdateInterval]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ThrusterSolverIterations: Number of iterations the thrust solver runs when translating a desired acceleration into individual thruster activation levels. More iterations track the desired acceleration more closely at more CPU cost.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MaxLinearMagicAcceleration: Maximum linear acceleration applied directly to the ship's body to make up for whatever its thrusters cannot deliver. Lets a ship move without adequate thrusters. Omitted or 0 disables the effect.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MaxAngularMagicAcceleration: Maximum angular acceleration applied directly to the ship's body to make up for whatever its thrusters cannot deliver, like [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MaxLinearMagicAcceleration]] but for rotation. Omitted or 0 disables the effect.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.UncommandedThrusterShutdownDelay: Seconds after the last movement command before the ship's thrusters shut down and stop updating.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MaxRampUpTimeForDeceleration: Seconds of thruster ramp-up the deceleration planner is willing to rely on. When predicting braking thrust, a thruster's usable activation is capped so reaching that level takes no longer than this.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.TargetReachedDistanceSizeRatio: Multiple of the ship's bounding diameter within which the autopilot considers its destination reached and switches to fine position adjustment.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.TargetResetDistanceSizeRatio: Multiple of the ship's bounding diameter beyond which the autopilot abandons fine adjustment and resumes a normal approach. Acts as the hysteresis counterpart to [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.TargetReachedDistanceSizeRatio]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AdjustRotationVelocity: Speed the ship must slow to before the autopilot starts rotating it toward the commanded final facing.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.VelocityCalculateFactor: Gain that converts the remaining distance to the destination, after subtracting the predicted stopping distance, into desired acceleration. Higher values make the autopilot approach more aggressively.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.TooCloseVelocityOverrideStrength: Multiplier on the push-away velocity used when the ship gets too close to another ship or obstacle. Vanilla's large value makes the override dominate normal movement.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AngularVelocityCalculateFactor: Gain that converts the remaining rotation offset, after subtracting the predicted stopping angle, into desired angular acceleration. The rotational counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.VelocityCalculateFactor]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.RetroVelocityCalculateFactor: Gain applied against the ship's current velocity when computing desired acceleration. Acts as the damping term that cancels residual drift.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.RetroAngularVelocityCalculateFactor: Gain applied against the ship's current angular velocity when computing desired angular acceleration. The rotational counterpart of [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.RetroVelocityCalculateFactor]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AngleSatisfiedThreshold: Range of remaining rotation offset, in degrees, used to scale forward thrust while the ship still needs to turn. At or below the minimum the ship thrusts at full power, at or above the maximum it holds thrust until the rotation catches up.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.HorizontalSatisfiedThreshold: Range of sideways velocity, measured as a fraction of the remaining forward distance, used to scale forward thrust while cruising. At or below the minimum the ship thrusts fully forward, at or above the maximum it diverts thrust into lateral correction instead.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DecelerationDistanceDtRange: Bounds, in seconds, on the timestep used by the iterative stopping-distance prediction. The actual step is the current speed times [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DecelerationDistanceDtSpeedRatio]] clamped into this range.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DecelerationDistanceDtSpeedRatio: Multiplied with the ship's current speed to pick the timestep of the stopping-distance prediction, clamped into [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DecelerationDistanceDtRange]]. Larger values give a coarser but cheaper prediction.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ForwardThrustRatioHistoryCount: Number of recent thrust updates over which the ship averages its forward thrust ratio. The average feeds arrival-time matching with other ships flying in formation.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ArrivalTimeOptimisticBuffer: Seconds subtracted from other ships' predicted arrival times when matching arrival times, so the ship assumes its companions arrive slightly sooner than predicted.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ArrivalTimeRampDown: Controls how quickly forward thrust may drop when slowing to match arrival times with other ships. Each update the allowed thrust ratio can fall by the update interval divided by this value, so larger values give a more gradual ramp down.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.OrbitModeRadiusFactor: Multiplier on a follow command's follow radius used to decide when the ship is close enough to switch into orbiting and fine adjustment around its target.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MaxPartSearchIterations: Cap on pathfinding iterations when searching outward for nearby parts or cells, for example when crew search from an airlock or when looking for resource destinations.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.SelfDestructForce: Impulse applied to the ship at every cell bordering a self-destructing part, pushing the surrounding structure away from the destroyed part.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.BaseSightRadius: Sight radius, in tiles beyond the ship's bounding radius, that every ship of this type has even without sensor parts. Sensor parts with a larger radius take precedence, and nebulas scale the result.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.BaseRadarRadius: Radar radius, in tiles beyond the ship's bounding radius, available without sensor parts. Sensor parts with a larger radius take precedence, and nebulas scale the result.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MinCommsRadius: Minimum communication radius, in tiles beyond the ship's bounding radius. The effective comms radius is the larger of this and the ship's sight radius.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.WeaponActivationEnemyCheckRadius: Distance beyond the ship's bounding radius scanned for visible enemy ships. Whether an enemy is inside drives weapon activation and gates behavior that requires safety, such as automatic crew exit.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultTargetingRangeFactor: Multiplies a weapon's emitter range to get its default auto-targeting acquisition range when the weapon does not define its own TargetingRange.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MapJumpFuelCostPerMass: Distance-independent term of the galaxy-map FTL jump fuel cost, charged per unit of ship mass. The total cost also scales with the ship's FTL jump efficiency.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MapJumpFuelCostPerMassDistance: Fuel charged per unit of ship mass per unit of galaxy-map distance jumped. Vanilla is tuned to 1 fuel per 100 mass to jump 10 light-years at 100% efficiency.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MapJumpDistanceExponent: Exponent applied to the map jump distance before it is multiplied by [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MapJumpFuelCostPerMassDistance]]. 1 makes fuel cost linear in distance.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.LocalJumpFuelCostPerMass: Distance-independent term of the in-system FTL jump fuel cost, charged per unit of ship mass. The total cost also scales with the ship's FTL jump efficiency.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.LocalJumpFuelCostPerMassDistance: Fuel charged per unit of ship mass per tile of in-system jump distance. Vanilla is tuned to 1 fuel per 100 mass to jump 25000 tiles at 100% efficiency.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.LocalJumpDistanceExponent: Exponent applied to the in-system jump distance before it is multiplied by [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.LocalJumpFuelCostPerMassDistance]]. 1 makes fuel cost linear in distance.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.LocalJumpMinDistance: Minimum distance, measured from the ship's bounding circle to the destination, required to start an in-system FTL jump. Closer destinations are rejected.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ImmuneToEnvDamage: Exempts the ship from environmental damage sources such as damaging nebulas, lightning strikes, and out-of-bounds damage in PvP arenas.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AlwaysVisibleInNebulas: Prevents nebulas from hiding this ship from other players' sight.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.IsAsteroid: Marks this ship type as an asteroid. Crew can salvage asteroids from manipulator-beam distance instead of standing adjacent, and creative mode's clear-objects tool counts them as asteroids rather than ships.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.JunkBlocksWeaponLOS: Whether junk chunks of this ship type block weapon line-of-sight checks and homing projectile target acquisition. Weapons with `IgnoreJunkLOSChecks` shoot through regardless.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.Roofs: Rules for rendering ship roofs, including fade in and out durations, roof texture and decal parameters, and the default roof colors.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.FtlEffects: The visual and sound effect played when the ship makes an FTL jump, including the flash sprite, warp distortion, light-speed streak, and the jump-in and jump-out sounds.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultCollectNuggetPriority: Default crew priority of the collect-loose-resources job. Shown in the crew priority UI under the part named by [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AssociateCollectNuggetJobWithPart]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AssociateCollectNuggetJobWithPart: The part whose crew-priority entry hosts the collect-nuggets job. The job appears under this part's priorities with [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultCollectNuggetPriority]] as its default.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultEjectNuggetPriority: Default crew priority of the eject-resources job, used when crew carry unwanted resources to an airlock and jettison them.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AssociateEjectNuggetJobWithPart: The part whose crew-priority entry hosts the eject-nuggets job. The job appears under this part's priorities with [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultEjectNuggetPriority]] as its default.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultSalvagePriority: Default crew priority of salvage jobs. Shown in the crew priority UI under the part named by [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AssociateSalvageJobWithPart]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultConstructPriority: Default crew priority of part construction jobs. Shown in the crew priority UI under the part named by [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AssociateConstructJobWithPart]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultDeconstructPriority: Default crew priority of part deconstruction jobs. Shown in the crew priority UI under the part named by [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AssociateDeconstructJobWithPart]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultRepairPriority: Default crew priority of part repair jobs. Shown in the crew priority UI under the part named by [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AssociateRepairJobWithPart]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AssociateSalvageJobWithPart: The part whose crew-priority entry hosts the salvage job. The job appears under this part's priorities with [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultSalvagePriority]] as its default.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AssociateConstructJobWithPart: The part whose crew-priority entry hosts the construct job. The job appears under this part's priorities with [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultConstructPriority]] as its default.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AssociateDeconstructJobWithPart: The part whose crew-priority entry hosts the deconstruct job. The job appears under this part's priorities with [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultDeconstructPriority]] as its default.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.AssociateRepairJobWithPart: The part whose crew-priority entry hosts the repair job. The job appears under this part's priorities with [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultRepairPriority]] as its default.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultConstructionWorkPerHealth: Crew work required to construct a part, per point of its max health, for parts that do not define their own `ConstructionWork`. Vanilla is tuned so a corridor tile takes about one second.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.DefaultWorkPerRepairedHealth: Crew work required per point of health repaired, for parts that do not define their own `WorkPerRepairedHealth`.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ConstructionResourceSearchesPerTick: How many pending construction jobs may search for source resources each simulation tick. Throttles the cost of construction searches on busy ships.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ConstructionNuggetSinkSearchesPerTick: How many searches for places to deposit leftover construction nuggets run each simulation tick, like [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.ConstructionResourceSearchesPerTick]] but for the nugget sink side.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.MaxCrewExitShipSpeed: Maximum ship speed at which crew are still allowed to automatically exit the ship, for example to work exterior jobs.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.OverrideConstructionMode: Forces this construction mode for ships of this type, overriding the construction mode the game mode would otherwise use.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.SalvagingDisabledSuppressesResourceDrops: Suppresses resource nugget drops from this ship's destroyed parts while the game mode has salvaging disabled.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.WeaponFireRevealDuration: Seconds after the ship last fires a weapon during which it counts as temporarily revealed, even if it would otherwise be hidden from other players.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.CategoryLimits: Per part-category limits on how many parts a ship may have. The build tools refuse to place parts beyond a category's `Max`. Warning messages use the nouns from [[Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.CategoryNouns]].
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.CategoryNouns: Singular and plural localization keys naming each part category, used in messages such as build-limit warnings. Categories without an entry fall back to the raw category ID.
Cosmoteer.Ships.ShipRules.VitalCategories: Part categories the ship should always contain. When a blueprint has no part in one of these categories, the build UI shows a missing-parts warning using that entry's `WarningKey`.

Cosmoteer.Ships.StatusRemapModifier.IgnoreValuesBelowRange: Whether to skip modification when the input value is below the RemapFrom range.
Cosmoteer.Ships.StatusRemapModifier.IgnoreValuesAboveRange: Whether to skip modification when the input value is above the RemapFrom range.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusCustomSha.StatusValueToEffectValueType: The status value conversion type, if any, to return as the value of Rand).
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusCustomSha.RectSizeFactor: The value that the status' rect size is multiplied by when retrieving a random point for particle spawning.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.IndicatorSprite: The sprite displayed over instances of this status.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.Layer: The layer that instances of this status are applied to.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.StatusCombineMode: How applications of this status are handled when another status of this type exists in the same location.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.ValueCombiner: Provides the final value when combining multiple status instances.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.ValueClampRange: The range that instances of this StatusType will have their values clamped to.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.TransferToUnderlyingOnPartDestroyed: Whether tile-based statuses will transfer to underlying parts when the part they're on is destroyed.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.NegativeCombineIgnoresResistance: Whether combinatory status applications with negative values will ignore value resistances.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.CrewSpeedFactor: The range value to multiply crew speed by, sampled by interpolated status value.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.JobInterruptionSeverity: The severity value used to determine whether certain jobs should be cancelled by this status, if any.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.ResourceSink: The rules defining the status' resource sink behaviour, if any.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.SoftRepair: The rules defining the status' soft-repair behaviour, if any.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.ValueModulators: The value modulators applied to the values of this status' instances.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.ValueModulationResistanceRange: The range that status resistance values are clamped to for ValueModulators.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.TickInterval: The interval between each call of this status' TickingEffects and TickingMediaEffects.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.RequireValueRangeForTick: The status value range that ticking effects will be performed for. Statuses with values outside this range will be ignored.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.ClearPlayerSourcesOnAggressionLoss: Whether the instances of this status should have their source player cleared on loss off aggression (e.g. truces).
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.ClearOnShipJunked: Whether the instances of this status should be removed (without triggering effects) on the ship being junked.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.StatusResistances: The status resistances provided to underlying parts by instances of this status type.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.DamageResistances: The damage resistances provided to underlying parts by instances of this status type.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.PenetrationResistanceModifier: The modifier applied to the penetration resistances of parts underlying instances of this status type.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.ApplicationEffects: The hit effects performed when this status is applied.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.ApplicationMediaEffects: The media effects played when this status is applied.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.TickingEffects: The hit effects performed on each tick of this status.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.TickingMediaEffects: The media effects played on each tick of this status.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.ContinuousMediaEffects: The media effects played continuously on application of this status, and stopped on status removal.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.CustomShapeEffects: The media effects played continuously using a single effect node with an ICustomEffectShape. Supports only a subset of media effects.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.RemovalEffects: The hit effects performed on removal of this status.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType.RemovalMediaEffects: The media effects played on removal of this status.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Base.DivideByPartArea: Whether to divide the resistance value by the tile-area of the part.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Base.MultiplyByStatusCount: Whether to multiply the resistance value by the instance count of this status type on the part. If this is false, this resistance is applied once, regardless of status count.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Simp.Resistance: The resistance value, sampled by the part's DamageFraction.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.Resistance: The resistance values, sampled by the part's DamageFraction.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.Resource: The requested resource type of the sink.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.AssociateJobWithPart: The PartRules to associate with the sink's delivery jobs, if any.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.JobNameKey: The key for the delivery job's priority name.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.PrioritySortOrder: The sort order for the delivery job's priority.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.DefaultPriority: The default priority for delivery jobs to this sink.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.ValueChangePerResource: The change in status value per resource delivered.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.TargetValue: The value that resources will be delivered in order to reach.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.AlwaysRequestMaxResources: Whether the status sinks will request the maximum amount of resources that crew can carry, regardless of remaining capacity.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.DeliveryMediaEffects: The continuous media effects to play while delivering resources to the sink.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.DeliveryHitEffects: The hit effects to perform on delivery of resources. EffectScale is set to the resource quantity.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.ReassignmentSearchIterations: The number of search iterations to perform when reassigning crew from removed sinks. Behaviour is disabled at 0.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.RegistrationValueRange: The range that a status' value must be within in order to be registered for repair.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusType/Stat.DeregistrationValueBuffer: How far outside the RegistrationValueRange a repair-registered status' value can be before it is deregistered.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusTypes.AtlasTextureParams: The parameters for creation of the status indicator atlases.

Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusValueRegu.Region: The region that the regulator affects.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusValueRegu.TargetValue: The value to move status values towards.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusValueRegu.ValueDelta: The amount that each status' value may be moved towards the target.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusValueRegu.ValueDeltaPool: The total amount of value that may be added or removed
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusValueRegu.ValueSourceStorage: The resource storage that the regulator will draw from.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusValueRegu.ValuePerSourceResource: The conversion rate of ValueSourceStorage resources to status value.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusValueRegu.LimitBySourceResources: Whether the regulator requires available resources in the ValueSourceStorage in order to function.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusValueRegu.ValueTargetStorage: The resource storage that the regulator will deposit into.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusValueRegu.ValuePerTargetResource: The conversion rate of status value to ValueTargetStorage resources.
Cosmoteer.Ships.Statuses.StatusValueRegu.LimitByTargetFreeSpace: Whether the regular requires free space in the ValueTargetStorage in order to function.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Ships: Whether ships and their parts can be affected at all. When false, every ship and part check fails regardless of the allegiance fields. True by default in every effect's built-in filter.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.OperationalHealth: Allows the effect to hit parts with operational health (the normal health pool of most functional parts). A part is filtered out when the flag for its health type is false. True by default. See [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.StructuralHealth]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.StructuralHealth: Allows the effect to hit parts with structural health, such as armor and structure. True by default. See [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.OperationalHealth]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.OnlyPartCategories: When set, only parts whose type declares at least one of these part categories pass the filter.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.ExcludePartCategories: When set, parts whose type declares any of these part categories are filtered out.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Shields: Whether shields can be affected. Shield hits still respect [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.IgnoreInvulnerability]] and the allegiance fields, judged by the ship the shield belongs to. True by default.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Bullets: Whether projectiles can be affected. A projectile's allegiance is judged by the ship that fired it, so a projectile fired by the effect's own source ship falls under [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Self]], not [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Friendlies]]. Projectiles whose firing ship no longer exists skip the allegiance check entirely and always pass. True by default for most effects, but `AreaImpulse` presets it to false.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.OnlyBulletCategories: When set, only projectiles declaring at least one of these categories in their `Targetable` rules pass the filter.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.ExcludeBulletCategories: When set, projectiles declaring any of these categories in their `Targetable` rules are filtered out.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.OnlyStatuses: When set, only parts matching at least one of these status filters pass the filter.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.ExcludeStatuses: Intended to filter out parts matching any of these status filters. The current game code inverts the check, so in practice it behaves exactly like [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.OnlyStatuses]] and rejects parts that do not match.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Crew: Whether crew floating in space can be affected. Crew allegiance uses their home ship while it still exists, otherwise the crew member's own player. True by default.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Nuggets: Whether loose resource nuggets can be affected. Nuggets have no allegiance, so this flag alone decides. True by default.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.IgnoreInvulnerability: When false, ships flagged invulnerable are protected from the effect. Checked for ship and shield hits. False by default for most damage effects, but `AreaImpulse` and the bullet and crew collision filters preset it to true.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Self: Whether the effect's own source ship can be affected. This also governs projectiles fired by the source ship when [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Bullets]] filtering runs, so an effect that should not touch its own projectiles needs `Self = false` even when [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Friendlies]] is false. When unset, falls back to [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Friendlies]] and then to the game mode's normal damage rules. No effect presets this, so it is unset unless written.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.TargetedSelf: Overrides [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Self]] when the source ship is also the effect's target ship. When unset, [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Self]] applies.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Friendlies: Whether allied ships (same team, including other ships of the same player) can be affected. Does not cover the effect's own source ship, which is [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Self]]. When unset, falls back to the game mode's normal damage rules. Most effects leave it unset, but `AreaImpulse` presets it to true, so it must be written as false there to spare allies.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.TargetedFriendlies: Overrides [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Friendlies]] when the hit ally is also the effect's target ship. When unset, [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Friendlies]] applies.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Enemies: Whether enemy ships can be affected. When unset, falls back to the game mode's normal damage rules, which normally allow damaging enemies. `AreaImpulse` presets it to true, and the bullet and crew collision filters preset it to false.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.TargetedEnemies: Overrides [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Enemies]] when the hit enemy is also the effect's target ship. Enemy projectiles targeting the effect's source ship also check this before [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Enemies]]. When unset, [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Enemies]] applies.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Neutrals: Whether neutral ships (neither allied nor enemy) can be affected. When unset, falls back to the game mode's normal damage rules. `AreaImpulse` presets it to true, and the bullet and crew collision filters preset it to false.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.TargetedNeutrals: Overrides [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Neutrals]] when the hit neutral ship is also the effect's target ship. When unset, [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Neutrals]] applies.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Junk: Whether junk ships (derelict debris) can be affected. When unset, falls back to the game mode's normal damage rules. `AreaImpulse` presets it to true, and the bullet and crew collision filters preset it to false.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.TargetedJunk: Overrides [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Junk]] when the junk is also the effect's target ship. When unset, [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Junk]] applies.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.EffectFilter.Deconstruction: When true, only hits caused by part deconstruction pass the filter. When false, deconstruction hits are filtered out. When unset, the distinction is ignored.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.AreaShie.ScaleChanceByShieldArea: Whether StatusChance will be scaled by the proportion of the shield's arc that is within the effect radius.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.AreaShie.ScaleValueByShieldArea: Whether StatusValue will be scaled by the proportion of the shield's arc that is within the effect radius.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.AreaShie.ScaleDurationByShieldArea: Whether StatusDuration will be scaled by the proportion of the shield's arc that is within the effect radius.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.AreaShie.UseMaxValuesForHitShield: Whether the maximum chance, duration, and value will be used for the directly hit shield, regardless of radius.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.AreaStat.Radius: The radius within which targets will be affected.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.AreaStat.ExclusionRadius: The inner radius within which cells will be ignored.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.AreaStat.AllowOverlappingApplications: Whether the effect is allowed to apply a status to the same part more than once in a single hit. Only applicable to part-layer statuses.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.AreaStat.DivideValueByPartArea: Whether the applied value for each tile is divided by the tile area of the part. Only applicable to part-layer statuses.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.AreaStat.AffectHitShipOnly: Whether only the hit ship should be affected.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.BaseStat.StatusChance: The chance for the effect to apply a status.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.BaseStat.InitialStatusDuration: The duration provided to newly applied status instances only.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.BaseStat.StatusValue: The value given to applied statuses.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.BaseStat.ValueType: How the status value is interpreted
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.BaseStat.Filter: The filter a hit must pass to apply a status.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.DamageEf.HitAreaMode: The mode of calculating the 'direct hit area' of this effect. Used to determine reactions to the hit.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.HitPartSourceRectType: The type of rect from the hit part to use as the set of source cells. If null, only the hit location cell is used.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.MaxCost: The maximum path cost to a cell for it to be affected.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.HitDirectionCostFactor: The factor applied to cell cost based on the path direction's alignment with the initial hit direction. For example, a factor of 0.25 with a hit direction of (1,0) would result in 25% of the cost when pathing from cell (5,0) to (6,0), but 100% of the cost pathing from cell (5,0) to (5,1).
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.MaxDistance: The maximum grid-distance to a cell for it to be affected.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.MinDistance: The minimum grid-distance to a cell for it to be affected.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.UseDistanceForFalloff: Whether cell distance instead of cost is used to calculate falloff.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.FalloffPadding: The value added to the upper limit when calculating the effect of falloff.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.ChanceFalloff: The exponent for StatusChance falloff over distance.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.ValueFalloff: The exponent for StatusValue falloff over distance.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.DurationFalloff: The exponent for StatusDuration falloff over distance.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.StatusValuePool: The value pool to split across the affected area, weighted according to falloff.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.AllowOverlappingApplications: Whether the effect is allowed to apply a status to the same part more than once in a single hit. Only applicable to part-layer statuses.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.DivideValueByPartArea: Whether the applied value for each tile is divided by the tile area of the part. Only applicable to part-layer statuses.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.DefaultTileCost: The pathing cost of non-empty tiles not otherwise included in PartIDTileCosts, PartCategoryTileCosts.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.EmptyTileCost: The pathing cost of empty tiles.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.PartIDTileCosts: The pathing costs of tiles by part ID.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.PartCategoryTileCosts: The pathing costs of tiles by part TypeCategories, not otherwise included in PartIDTileCosts. A part's categories are evaluated in order.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.BlockedByWalls: Whether pathing between tiles is blocked by walls.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.GridStat.AllowDiagonals: Whether pathing across diagonals is allowed.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.KillCrew.KillChance: The chance to kill a hit crew member.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.KillCrew.AffectEntireParts: Whether entire parts are affected on hit, or just the hit cell.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.KillCrew.ImmunityResourcesIsBlacklist: Whether ImmunityResources should be treated as a blacklist rather than a whitelist.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.KillCrew.ImmunityResources: The list of resources that provide immunity from this effect when carried by a crew member. If ImmunityResourcesIsBlacklist is true, this behaviour is inverted.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.KillCrew.ImmuneWhileEmptyHanded: Whether a crew member is immune to this effect while not carrying any resources.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.KillCrew.Filter: The filter used to determine hit validity.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.Repeatin.HitEffects: The hit effects to perform every repetition.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.Repeatin.MediaEffects: The media effects to play every repetition.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.Repeatin.Interval: The period between one repetition and the next.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.Repeatin.InitialDelay: The delay before starting the first repetition.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.Repeatin.RequireHitPart: Whether to end the repeating effect if the hit part is null or destroyed.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.Repeatin.HitEffectsScaleOverIterations: The multiplier of effectScale over the duration of the repeat. Minimum on the first iteration to maximum on the last.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.Repeatin.HitEffectsScaleOverIterationsExponent: The exponent to which the normalised iteration progress value is raised, which is used to sample HitEffectsScaleOverIterations.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.StatusAp.ApplyToAllHitPartTiles: Whether the effect should apply to each tile of the hit part, or just the tile matching the hit location. Only applicable to tile-layer statuses.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.StatusAp.DivideValueByPartArea: Whether the status value will be divided by the tile-area of the hit part.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.StatusAp.DivideValueByPartHealth: Whether the status value will be divided by the max health of the hit part.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.HitEffects.StatusAp.DivideValueByPartHealthFactor: Whether the status value will be divided by the part's current health percentage.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.EmitImmediately: Whether the emitter should be created with 1 accumulated particle emission.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.LightNormalIn: The data that stores the particle locations.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.DiffuseShader: The shader used when rendering to the diffuse target.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.NormalsShader: The shader used when rendering to the normals target.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.EnableLightningShaderAnimation: Enables animating the vertex color along the entire bolt for use by the lightning shader.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.BlendLightningShaderAnimation: If lightning shader animation is enabled, enables blending between it and the original vertex color over the lifetime of the bolt.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.UseSameSeedForEntireBolt: When not enabled (the default behavior), changes the noise seed per bolt segment so each segments motion is more random.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.IgnoreBoltEndingOverride: If lightning shader animation is enabled, disables the default behavior of overriding the animation for the bolts ending segment with values to make the bolts ending fade smoothly.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.IgnoreBoltStartOverride: If lightning shader animation is enabled, disables the default behavior of overriding the animation for the bolts starting segment with values to make the bolts start fade smoothly.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.LightningShaderAnimationSegments: If lightning shader animation is enabled, controls the number of animated slices (bright sections) the bolt should have.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.TimeShiftPerSegment: Shifts the perlin noise sample forward or backwards in time incrementally for each bolt segment.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.AnimationIntensity: If lightning shader animation and blending are enabled, controls the intensity of the lightning shader animation.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.SegmentOverlap: Shifts the endpoint of each segment forward by this amount.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.SourceLocationIn: The data that stores the particle source locations.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.DestLocationIn: The data that stores the particle destination locations.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.ScaleIn: The data, if any, that stores the particle scale factors.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.ColorIn: The data, if any, that stores the particle colors.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.UVRectIn: The data, if any, that stores the particle UVs.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.TimeIn: The data that stores the particle time.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.SeedIn: The data that stores the particle seed.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.DistPerSegment: The approximate distance per segment which will determine how many segments to use.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.XOffsetPerlin: The perlin noise to add to the lightning's point X offsets.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.YOffsetPerlin: The perlin noise to add to the lightning's point Y offsets.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.BulgeExponent: The amount that the lightning bolt will "bulge" near its center.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.Color2In: The data that stores the 2nd set of particle colors.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.VelocityInOut: The data that stores the converted location.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.VelocityIn: The data that stores the particle's velocity.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.RotationInOut: The data that stores the particle's rotation.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.IndexOut: The data that will be set to the particle's index.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.BarrierIDIn: The index of the particle barrier.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.LocationIn: The data that stores the location of the particle.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.ColorOut: The data that stores the particle color.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.DataXIn: The data used for X in the output Vector2.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.DataYIn: The data used for Y in the output Vector2.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.ColorOut: The data that stores the particle color.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.LocationOut: The data that stores the end point.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.LocationInOut: The data that stores the particle's location.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.VelocityIn: The data that stores the particle's velocity.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.Mode: The particle location compensation mode to use.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.Strength: The strength of the FPS compensation.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.HitNormalOut: The data that will be set to the hit normal.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.VelocityInOut: The data that stores the converted location.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.LocationIn: The data that stores the particle's location.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.LightNormalOut: The data that stores the particle's light normal.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.LocationIn: The data that stores the location of the particle.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.BarrierLocationIn: The location of the particle barrier.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.BarrierNormalIn: The normal of the particle barrier.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.InvertNormal: Whether to invert the barrier normal.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.LocationOut: The data that stores the particle's location.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.ValueOut: The value associated with the particle's location.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.LocationOut: The data that stores the particle's location.
The data that stores the particle's location.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.RectType: Which type of part rect to use.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.Inflate: The amount by which the part's rect is inflated.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.RestrictToPartColliders: Whether the particle locations should be restricted to the part's collision geometry.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.SubtractPartCenter: Whether the particle locations should be ship or rules relative.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.RotateByDirection: Whether the part rect should be rotated by the part direction in ship space.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.LocationOut: The data that stores the particle's location.
