Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.RotationIn: The data that stores the particle's rotation.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.NormalOut: The data that stores a normal representation of the input rotation.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.DataInOut: The data that will be offset based upon the index.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.IndexIn: The index that will be used to offset the data.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.Offset: The offset that will be multiplied with the particle's index.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.Modulus: The value by which the index will be modulo'ed before being multiplied with Offset. Use 0 to disable.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.SafetyMargin: The margin that is used to restrict the rotational slice to avoid similar end rotations.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.NormalIn: The data that stores a normal representation of the input rotation.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.RotationOut: The data that stores the particle's rotation.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.ScaleOut: The data that stores the particle's scale.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.ScaleFactor: The factor by which the zoom will be scaled.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.ZoomExponent: The exponent that will be applied to the zoom.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.ScaleOut: The data that stores the particle's scale.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.ScaleFactor: The factor by which the intensity will be scaled.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.IntensityExponent: The exponent that will be applied to the intensity.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.BarrierIDOut: The index of the particle barrier.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.FlipXYIn: The data that stores the particle's sprite index.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.UvRectInOut: The data that stores the particle's UV rect coordinates.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.VelocityOut: The data that stores the particle's velocity.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.VelocityFactor: The factor by which the intensity will be scaled.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.IntensityExponent: The exponent that will be applied to the intensity.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.LocationOut: The data that stores the particle's location.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.RectType: Which type of part rect to use.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.Inflate: The amount by which the part's rect is inflated.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.Partic.RestrictToPartColliders: Whether the particle locations should be restricted to the part's collision geometry.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.TileQu.Inflate: The value to inflate the quad's rect by.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.TileQu.Z: The depth coordinate of the quad.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.TileQu.ColorOverIntensity: The color range to sample by the intensity.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.TileQu.ColorOverFade: The color range to sample by the fade value.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.MediaEffects.TileQu.DisableQuadRotation: Whether to keep the quad rotationally aligned with the world-axes instead of the ship.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.Overlays.SplitCappe.SplitRelativeThickness: Whether the line thickness and cap sizes are halved when rendering only one half of the horizontally sliced sprites.

Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipTextColorFactor: Multiplied into the color of ship name text shown in the world, such as ship indicator labels and mouseover names, after the allegiance or indicator color is chosen. Vanilla uses it to make ship text slightly transparent.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectionOutlineSprite: The border of the rectangle drawn while dragging a box selection around ships. Its color is tinted by the local player's color. When the camera focus mode is active, [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.FocusOutlineSprite]] is drawn instead.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectionFillSprite: The translucent interior of the drag selection rectangle, tinted by the local player's color. Drawn together with [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectionOutlineSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.FocusOutlineSprite: The border of the drag selection rectangle while the camera focus cursor mode is active, replacing [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectionOutlineSprite]]. Unlike the normal selection rectangle it is not tinted by the player color.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.FocusFillSprite: The interior of the drag selection rectangle while the camera focus cursor mode is active, replacing [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectionFillSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeShipCircle: The circle drawn around a ship under the mouse cursor that is not yet selected, previewing what a click would select. Tinted by the ship's allegiance color. Ships concealed inside a nebula use [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.HiddenTentativeShipCircle]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.HiddenTentativeShipCircle: Replaces [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeShipCircle]] for hovered ships that are hidden inside a nebula. Vanilla gives it a different dash pattern to signal the contact is concealed.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeShipCrewCircle: The circle drawn around each crew member who is assigned to a hovered ship but currently outside it in space. Crew whose distance warning is active use [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipCrewWarningCircle]] instead. Selected ships use [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCrewCircle]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeScheduledNuggetCollectSprite: Icon drawn over each nugget that a ship under the mouse cursor has already been ordered to collect, previewing that ship's scheduled pickups before you select it. The equivalent icon for ships you have selected is [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedScheduledNuggetCollectSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeNuggetCollectSprite: Icon drawn over a nugget that the selected ships are able to collect, shown when hovering the nugget and over each nugget inside the drag selection rectangle in salvage collection mode. Swapped for [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeNuggetCantCollectSprite]] when collection is not possible.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeNuggetCantCollectSprite: Icon drawn over a hovered nugget when none of the selected ships can collect it, for example because they lack storage capacity or are too far away. The collectable counterpart is [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeNuggetCollectSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.NuggetCancelCollectSprite: Icon drawn over a nugget whose scheduled collection would be cancelled, shown when hovering a nugget with a pending pickup in cancel mode and over nuggets inside the cancel drag rectangle in salvage collection mode.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeShipCrewLine: The line connecting a hovered ship to each of its crew members who are out in space, drawn from the ship's bounding circle to the crew member. Shown only when the show space crew lines setting is enabled, except that crew with a distance warning always get [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipCrewWarningLine]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipVelocityLine: The arrow line drawn from a selected or hovered ship's selection circle in its direction of travel, indicating current linear velocity. Its length comes from the velocity times [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipVelocityLineScale]] and it fades out at very low speeds. Also drawn for all on-screen ships when the velocity indicator display setting is set to all.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipVelocityLineScale: Multiplies the ship's linear velocity to get the world-space length of [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipVelocityLine]]. Because velocity is in tiles per second, the value is effectively the number of seconds of travel the line's length represents.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipAngularVelocityCircle: The arc drawn just outside a ship's selection circle to indicate its current rotation speed and direction. The arc's angular width is the angular velocity times [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipAngularVelocityScale]] and it fades out at very low rotation speeds. An arrowhead from [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipAngularVelocityArrow]] is drawn at its far end.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipAngularVelocityArrow: The arrowhead sprite drawn at the leading end of [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipAngularVelocityCircle]], pointing in the direction of rotation.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipAngularVelocityScale: Multiplies the ship's angular velocity in radians per second to get the angular width of the [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipAngularVelocityCircle]] arc. The value is effectively the number of seconds of rotation the arc represents.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCircle: The circle drawn around each ship the player currently has selected, tinted by the ship's allegiance color. When first selected its color tweens from [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCircleTweenFromColor]] over [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCircleTweenTime]]. Ships concealed inside a nebula use [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.HiddenSelectedShipCircle]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.HiddenSelectedShipCircle: Replaces [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCircle]] for selected ships that are hidden inside a nebula. Vanilla gives it a different dash pattern.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCircleTweenTime: Seconds over which a newly selected ship's selection circle color fades from [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCircleTweenFromColor]] to its normal color, producing a brief flash on selection.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCircleTweenFromColor: Multiplier applied to the selection circle's color at the moment a ship is selected, which then fades back to the normal color over [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCircleTweenTime]]. Vanilla uses an over-bright alpha so the circle flashes when selection starts.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCrewCircle: The circle drawn around each crew member who is assigned to a selected ship but currently outside it in space. Crew with an active distance warning use [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipCrewWarningCircle]] instead. Hovered ships use [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeShipCrewCircle]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCrewLine: The line connecting a selected ship to each of its crew members who are out in space. Shown only when the show space crew lines setting is enabled, except that crew with a distance warning always get [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipCrewWarningLine]] instead.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedScheduledNuggetCollectSprite: Icon drawn over each nugget that the currently selected ships are scheduled to collect. It animates in per [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledNuggetCollectTweenDuration]] and [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledNuggetCollectTweenFromScale]] after the collect order is issued.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledNuggetCollectTweenDuration: Seconds over which the scheduled collect icon and its resource pickup line fade and scale in after a collect order is issued. Applies to both [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedScheduledNuggetCollectSprite]] and [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeScheduledNuggetCollectSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledNuggetCollectTweenFromScale: Starting scale multiplier of the scheduled collect icon when a collect order is issued. The icon grows or shrinks from this value to 1 over [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledNuggetCollectTweenDuration]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipCrewWarningCircle: Replaces [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCrewCircle]] or [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeShipCrewCircle]] around an off-ship crew member whose distance warning is active, meaning they are straying too far from their ship.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipCrewWarningLine: The line drawn from a ship to an off-ship crew member whose distance warning is active. Unlike [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedShipCrewLine]] it is drawn regardless of the show space crew lines setting.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.FocusShipCircle: The circle drawn around ships under the mouse cursor while the camera focus cursor mode is active, previewing which ship a click would focus the camera on. Not drawn when the camera is already focused on or inside that ship. Drawn at 1.5 times the ship's bounding radius.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeAttackCommandCircle: Circle drawn around a hovered enemy ship to preview that a right click would issue an attack command, tinted with the player's enemy color. Also drawn around each target while dragging an attack command.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeFollowCommandCircle: Circle drawn around a hovered friendly or neutral ship to preview that a right click would issue a follow command, tinted with the player's own color. Also drawn around target ships while dragging a move command with collision avoidance active on them.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeFtlCommandCircle: Circle drawn around an FTL gate under the mouse cursor when the selected ships have FTL drives, previewing a jump command through the gate. It replaces the default attack or follow context circle.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeSalvageCommandCircle: Circle drawn around a hovered derelict or enemy ship that the selected ships can salvage, previewing a salvage command. Its color is tinted by the target's allegiance.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.CancelSalvageCommandCircle: Circle drawn around a hovered ship that a selected ship currently has a salvage command against, previewing that clicking would cancel that command.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.FocusModeCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the camera focus input mode is held, where clicking a ship focuses the camera on it. Also used by mission GUI elements that focus the camera on a mission target.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.PartModeCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the part selection input mode is active, where clicks select individual parts inside ships instead of whole ships.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.NuggetCollectCursor: Mouse cursor shown while hovering a nugget that the selected ships can collect. Swapped for [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.NuggetCantCollectCursor]] when collection is not possible.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.NuggetCantCollectCursor: Mouse cursor shown while hovering a nugget that none of the selected ships can collect, matching the [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeNuggetCantCollectSprite]] overlay.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.CancelSalvageCollectCursor: Mouse cursor shown when a click would cancel salvage or collection, while hovering a ship, part, or nugget with a pending salvage or pickup and while holding the cancel modifier in salvage collection mode.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SalvageCollectionSelectionOutlineSprite: The four edges of the drag-select rectangle while box-selecting parts and nuggets to salvage in salvage collection mode.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SalvageCollectionSelectionFillSprite: The interior of the drag-select rectangle while box-selecting parts and nuggets to salvage in salvage collection mode.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.CancelSalvageCollectionSelectionOutlineS: The four edges of the drag-select rectangle when the cancel-salvage input is held in salvage collection mode, replacing [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SalvageCollectionSelectionOutlineSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.CancelSalvageCollectionSelectionFillSpri: The interior of the drag-select rectangle when the cancel-salvage input is held in salvage collection mode, replacing [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SalvageCollectionSelectionFillSprite]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.NuggetLocationOverlaySprite: Icon drawn over every visible nugget while the salvage collection tool is active, marking where collectable resources are. Nuggets of resource types banned on all selected ships are skipped.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.BlueprintTransitionDuration: Seconds for blueprint, redprint, and prohibited-zone overlays to fade fully in or out when their visibility toggles.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.BackgroundTransitionDuration: Seconds for the space background dim to fade in when a toolbox opens and out when it closes. The dim fades toward [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.BackgroundDimOpacity]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.BackgroundDimOpacity: Opacity, as a 0..1 fraction, to which the space background is dimmed while a build, paint, crew, or resources toolbox is open. The dim fades in and out over [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.BackgroundTransitionDuration]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.GridTransitionDuration: Seconds for the build grid drawn over the selected ship to fade fully in or out when it is shown or hidden.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipGridLiveModeColor: Line color of the build grid drawn over the selected ship's maximum build area when viewing the live ship. Blends toward [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipGridBlueprintModeColor]] as the blueprint view fades in.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipGridBlueprintModeColor: Line color of the build grid over the selected ship while the full blueprint view is shown. The grid blends from [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipGridLiveModeColor]] as the blueprint fades in.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipDarkenColor: Color factor that unselected ships fade toward when the background is dimmed by an open toolbox, and that ships fade toward as the blueprint view fades in. White means no darkening.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SelectedPartNineSlice: Nine-slice box drawn around each selected part, tinted with the player or neutral faction color. Fades out as the build grid fades in.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativePartNineSlice: Nine-slice box drawn around parts inside the current selection rectangle that are not yet selected, tinted with the player or neutral faction color.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.FocusPartNineSlice: Nine-slice box drawn around parts under the cursor while in focus mode with the camera focused inside the ship.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.HealthBarBlockSprite: Sprite tiled horizontally across a part's health bar, one repetition per [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.HealthBarHPPerBlock]] hit points, so the bar reads as discrete blocks. Tinted from green toward red as the part takes damage.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.HealthBarHPPerBlock: Hit points represented by one block of [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.HealthBarBlockSprite]] in the health bar shown above a part. Vanilla uses 1000, so a 4000 HP part shows four blocks.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledSalvageNineSlice: Nine-slice box drawn around each part that a selected ship's crew has been ordered to salvage. Appears with a shrink animation controlled by [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledSalvageNineSliceShrinkDuration]] and fades out as salvaging progresses per [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledSalvageProgressFadeRange]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeSalvageNineSlice: Nine-slice box drawn around parts that would be salvaged, shown when hovering a salvageable part, around parts inside the salvage drag-select rectangle, and around the existing salvage targets of ships under the cursor.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.CancelSalvageNineSlice: Nine-slice box drawn around parts whose salvage order would be canceled, shown when hovering a part being salvaged with the cancel input held and around such parts inside the cancel drag-select rectangle.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledSalvageNineSliceShrinkDuration: Seconds after a salvage order is issued during which [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledSalvageNineSlice]] fades in while shrinking from [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledSalvageNineSliceShrinkFromInflate]] down to fit the part exactly.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledSalvageNineSliceShrinkFromInfla: Distance in tiles by which [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledSalvageNineSlice]] starts inflated beyond the part's rectangle when a salvage order is issued, shrinking to 0 over [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledSalvageNineSliceShrinkDuration]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledSalvageReticleSprite: Icon drawn centered on each part that a selected ship's crew has been ordered to salvage, alongside [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledSalvageNineSlice]].
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeScheduledSalvageReticleSprite: Icon drawn centered on the salvage targets of ships under the cursor, alongside [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.TentativeSalvageNineSlice]], previewing which parts those ships are set to salvage.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ScheduledSalvageProgressFadeRange: Range of the part's salvage effect intensity, a 0..1 value, over which the scheduled-salvage box and reticle fade out as crew salvage the part. With the vanilla `[0, 0.5]` the overlay is fully faded once intensity reaches 0.5.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SalvageCursor: Mouse cursor shown while the salvage collection tool is active and when hovering a ship or part that can be salvaged. Replaced by [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.CancelSalvageCollectCursor]] while the cancel modifier is held.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipSelectSound: Plays when the player selects one or more ships, whether by clicking, drag-selecting, recalling a control group, or clicking a ship logo in the FTL jump window.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipFocusSound: Plays when the camera locks focus onto a ship or group of ships, such as after a double-click. Distinct from [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.ShipSelectSound]], which covers plain selection.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.PartSelectSound: Plays when one or more parts are added to the current part selection, including selection via part control groups and the parts card.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.PartDeselectSound: Plays when parts are removed from the current part selection or the part selection is cleared.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.FtlReadySound: Plays in the FTL jump window at the moment every selected ship with a jump command finishes charging and becomes ready to jump. Fires only on the transition from not ready to ready while the jump button is active.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.NuggetCollectSound: Plays when the player successfully orders crew to collect one or more resource nuggets, including multi-nugget collection via double-click.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.NuggetCollectCancelSound: Plays when the player cancels pending nugget collection jobs, either by clicking a nugget already being collected or by drag-selecting nuggets in cancel mode. In a cancel drag that also cancels part salvage, [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SalvageCancelSound]] plays instead.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SalvageCancelSound: Plays when the player cancels scheduled salvage on one or more parts, by clicking a part already marked for salvage or by drag-selecting in cancel mode.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SalvageMineSound: Plays when the player issues salvage orders on ship parts, whether by clicking a part or by drag-selecting an area in salvage mode.
Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.GatherFloatingResourcesSound: Plays when a drag-selection in salvage mode issues collection orders for floating resource nuggets and no parts were marked for salvage in the same drag. If parts were also marked, [[Cosmoteer.Simulation.SimGuiRules.SalvageMineSound]] plays instead.

Cosmoteer.Source.Simulation.MediaEffects.LocationOut: The data that stores the particle's location.
Cosmoteer.Source.Simulation.MediaEffects.CenterType: Which type of part rect's center to use.

Cosmoteer.Source.Simulation.MediaEffects.ScaleOut: The data that stores the particle's scale.
Cosmoteer.Source.Simulation.MediaEffects.ScaleFactor: The factor by which the intensity will be scaled.
Cosmoteer.Source.Simulation.MediaEffects.Inflate: The amount by which the part's rect is inflated.
Cosmoteer.Source.Simulation.MediaEffects.RectType: Which type of part rect to use.
Cosmoteer.Source.Simulation.MediaEffects.UseMinDimension: Whether the smaller of the two part dimensions should be used instead of the larger.

Cosmoteer.ValueCombination.NormalizedAdd.ValueRange: The range to use for lerp and inverse-lerp operations.
Cosmoteer.ValueCombination.NormalizedAdd.InverseValueExponent: The exponent applied to (1-normalizedBaseValue) before multiplication with the normalizedOtherValue.
Cosmoteer.ValueCombination.NormalizedAdd.ReducePositiveValues: Whether positive input values should be reduced by the inverse value.
Cosmoteer.ValueCombination.NormalizedAdd.ReduceNegativeValues: Whether negative input values should be reduced by the inverse value.

Halfling.Audio.FilterParams.FilterType: Specifies whether and how frequencies of audio will be filtered.
Halfling.Audio.FilterParams.Frequency: Specifies the cutoff frequency used by the filter.
Halfling.Audio.FilterParams.QFactor: Specifies the Q factor used by the filter. This value controls how rapidly the audio will be reduced. It is not used by the LowPass or HighPass filters.

Halfling.Audio.ISoundEffect.Sounds: A collection Sound objects from which a random Sound will be selected to play.
Halfling.Audio.ISoundEffect.VolumeRange: The range of volumes in which the actual volume of the sound effect will be chosen at random.
Halfling.Audio.ISoundEffect.SpeedRange: The range of speeds in which the actual speed of the sound effect will be chosen at random.
Halfling.Audio.ISoundEffect.StartRange: The range of possible start times in which the actual start time will be chosen at random.
Halfling.Audio.ISoundEffect.Filter: The filter, if any, to apply to the sound.

Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise1D.Seed: A value used to hash the input coordinates. A PerlinNoise1D with the same seed value (and other properties) will always produce the same output for a given input coordinate.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise1D.GridSize: The size in units of the underlying Perlin noise grid.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise1D.Repeat: If not zero, the outputted values will repeat every this number of coordinate steps.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise1D.Octaves: The number of iterations used to generate the final noise value.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise1D.Persistence: The factor by which the strength each further octave iteration will be multiplied.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise1D.OutputExponent: The power to which output values are raised before they are scaled, shifted, and clamped.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise1D.OutputScale: The factor by which output values are multiplied after they are exponentiated and before they are shifted and clamped.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise1D.OutputShift: The amount added to the output values after they are exponentiated and scaled and before they are clamped.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise1D.ClampOutput: The range to which output values will be clamped after they are exponentiated, scaled, and shifted.

Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise2D.Seed: A value used to hash the input coordinates. A PerlinNoise2D with the same seed value (and other properties) will always produce the same output for a given input coordinate.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise2D.GridSize: The size in units of the underlying Perlin noise grid.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise2D.Repeat: If not zero, the outputted values will repeat every this number of coordinate steps for the corresponding dimension.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise2D.Octaves: The number of iterations used to generate the final noise value.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise2D.Persistence: The factor by which the strength each further octave iteration will be multiplied.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise2D.OutputExponent: The power to which output values are raised before they are scaled, shifted, and clamped.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise2D.OutputScale: The factor by which output values are multiplied after they are exponentiated and before they are shifted and clamped.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise2D.OutputShift: The amount added to the output values after they are exponentiated and scaled and before they are clamped.
Halfling.Generation.PerlinNoise2D.ClampOutput: The range to which output values will be clamped after they are exponentiated, scaled, and shifted.

Halfling.Geometry.Vector2.X: The X (1st) component of this Vector2.
Halfling.Geometry.Vector2.Y: The Y (2nd) component of this Vector2.

Halfling.Geometry.Vector3.X: The X (1st) component of this Vector3.
Halfling.Geometry.Vector3.Y: The Y (2nd) component of this Vector3.
Halfling.Geometry.Vector3.Z: The Z (3rd) component of this Vector3.

Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.Shader: The Shader used to render the circle. This must be a special shader specifically created for rendering circles.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.ShaderConstants: A collection of constant values for the Shader. This collection may be null if there is no Shader or there are no constants set.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.Texture: The Texture used to render the circle.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.Color: The shader color used to render the circle.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.TargetBlendMode: The TargetBlendMode used to render the circle.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.VertexColor: The vertex colors of both the inner and outer edges of this circle. If getting this property and the inner and outer vertex colors are now equal, an exception will be thrown. Modifying this property will force the underlying circle quads to be recomputed.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.InnerVertexColor: Vertex color of the inner edge of the circle. Modifying this property will force the underlying circle quads to be recomputed.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.UVRect: The UV coordinates on Texture from which the circle is rendered. The outer edge will equal UVRect.Top and the inner edge will equal UVRect.Bottom. The circle will loop from UVRect.Left to UVRect.Right. Modifying this property will force the underlying circle quads to be recomputed.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.InnerRadius: The distance from the center of the circle to inside edge.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.Thickness: The thickness of the circle as measured from its inner radius to its outer radius.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.QuadCount: The number of quads that will be used to form the circle. The higher this value, the smoother the circle will appear. Modifying this property will force the underlying circle quads to be recomputed.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.Arc: The angular arc of the rendered circle. Anything less than 360-degrees will result in a pie-shape. Modifying this property will force the underlying circle quads to be recomputed.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.Z: The Z depth value of the rendered circle. Modifying this property will force the underlying circle quads to be recomputed.
Halfling.Graphics.CircleRenderer.DashRepeat: The number of times that the dashes will repeat. The default is 0.

Halfling.Graphics.Material.ShaderConstants: A collection of constant values for the Shader. This collection may be null if there is no Shader or there are no constants set.

Halfling.Graphics.Sprite.VertexColor: The vertex color of the Sprite.
Halfling.Graphics.Sprite.UVRect: The UV rect on Texture of the Sprite.

Halfling.Graphics.TargetBlendMode.SourceRgbFactor: The SourceRgbFactor parameter for every RGB channel in the equation: RgbOperator([sourceRGB] * SourceRgbFactor, [destRGB] * DestRgbFactor)
Halfling.Graphics.TargetBlendMode.DestRgbFactor: The DestRgbFactor parameter for every RGB channel in the equation: RgbOperator([sourceRGB] * SourceRgbFactor, [destRGB] * DestRgbFactor)
Halfling.Graphics.TargetBlendMode.RgbOperator: The RgbOperator function for every RGB channel in the equation: RgbOperator([sourceRGB] * SourceRgbFactor, [destRGB] * DestRgbFactor)
Halfling.Graphics.TargetBlendMode.SourceAlphaFactor: The SourceAlphaFactor parameter for the alpha channel in the equation: AlphaOperator([sourceAlpha] * SourceAlphaFactor, [destAlpha] * DestAlphaFactor)
Halfling.Graphics.TargetBlendMode.DestAlphaFactor: The DestAlphaFactor parameter for the alpha channel in the equation: AlphaOperator([sourceAlpha] * SourceAlphaFactor, [destAlpha] * DestAlphaFactor)
Halfling.Graphics.TargetBlendMode.AlphaOperator: The AlphaOperator function for the alpha channel in the equation: AlphaOperator([sourceAlpha] * SourceAlphaFactor, [destAlpha] * DestAlphaFactor)

Halfling.Graphics.Text.ColorFontEffect.Color: The color that will be applied to the font.

Halfling.Graphics.Text.Font.File: The font file to load, typically a `.ttf`, resolved relative to this file.
Halfling.Graphics.Text.Font.Files: Several font files loaded together, with later files supplying the glyphs earlier ones lack. Used to chain a fallback font for CJK glyphs.
Halfling.Graphics.Text.Font.Passes: The render passes applied when drawing text, in order. Each pass draws the whole text once with its own offset and [[Halfling.Graphics.Text.FontRenderPass.Effects]], which is how shadow and glow fonts layer a blurred colored copy under the foreground.
Halfling.Graphics.Text.Font.ForceSameWidths: Two equal-length strings read as character pairs: each character in the first string is forced to render with the width of the matching character in the second. Vanilla maps `"123456789"` to `"000000000"` so digits align in columns.
Halfling.Graphics.Text.Font.AntialiasQuality: The antialiasing quality level used when rasterizing glyphs.

Halfling.Graphics.Text.FontRenderPass.SizeRelativeOffset: The position offset of this pass relative to the pixel size of the font.
Halfling.Graphics.Text.FontRenderPass.Effects: A modifiable list of effects to apply to the font during this render pass. The effects will be applied in the order in which they appear in this list. Adding or removing effects only has an effect on characters that have not yet been pre-rendered.

Halfling.Graphics.Text.OuterStrokeFontEf.Color: The color that will be applied to the font's edges.
Halfling.Graphics.Text.OuterStrokeFontEf.RelativeThickness: The thickness of the stroke effect relative to the size of the font. This will be added with AbsoluteThickness.
Halfling.Graphics.Text.OuterStrokeFontEf.AbsoluteThickness: The thickness of the stroke effect in pixels. This will be added with RelativeThickness.
Halfling.Graphics.Text.OuterStrokeFontEf.Falloff: A number that indicates how quickly the stroke effect will disappear as the distance from the edge of the font increases.

Halfling.Gui.Button.StateNormalTextRenderer: The settings for the text shown when this button is in its "Normal" state.
Halfling.Gui.Button.StateHighlightedTextRenderer: The settings for the text shown when this button is in its "Highlighted" state.
Halfling.Gui.Button.StatePressedTextRenderer: The settings for the text shown when this button is in its "Pressed" or "HotkeyPressed" states.
Halfling.Gui.Button.StateDisabledTextRenderer: The settings for the text shown when this button is in its "Disabled" state.
Halfling.Gui.Button.ClickController: The settings for the component that controls the firing of Clicked events.
Halfling.Gui.Button.ClickAudio: The audio sound effect played when this button is clicked.
Halfling.Gui.Button.Text: The text displayed on this button.
Halfling.Gui.Button.TextProvider: An ITextProvider object that, if not null, will keep this Button's Text property updated to match the ITextProvider.
Halfling.Gui.Button.Hotkey: The IInputButton (or combination of buttons) which will put the widget into a "HotkeyPressed" state.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetActi.ActivatedSound: The sound to play when the widget is activated.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetActi.DeactivatedSound: The sound to play when the widget is deactivated.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetActi.AudioGroup: The AudioGroup in which the sounds will be played. If null, the GuiRoot's DefaultAudioGroup will be used.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetFocu.FocusedSound: The sound to play when the widget becomes focused.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetFocu.DefocusedSound: The sound to play when the widget becomes defocused.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetFocu.AudioGroup: The AudioGroup in which the sounds will be played. If null, the GuiRoot's DefaultAudioGroup will be used.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetInpu.NormalSound: The sound to play when the input state reverts to its "Normal" state, or null to play no sound.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetInpu.PressedSound: The sound to play when the input state switches to "Pressed" or "HotkeyPressed", or null to play no sound.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetInpu.AudioGroup: The AudioGroup in which the sounds will be played. If null, the GuiRoot's DefaultAudioGroup will be used.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetSele.SelectedSound: The sound to play when the widget becomes selected.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetSele.DeselectedSound: The sound to play when the widget becomes deselected.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetSele.AudioGroup: The AudioGroup in which the sounds will be played. If null, the GuiRoot's DefaultAudioGroup will be used.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetTrig.Sound: The sound to play when the trigger is triggered.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Audio.WidgetTrig.AudioGroup: The AudioGroup in which the sound will be played. If null, the GuiRoot's DefaultAudioGroup will be used.

Halfling.Gui.Components.AutoSizing.Widge.AutoWidthMode: Whether the width of the widget is automatically sized to match its contents. The desired size of the contents is defined by the DesiredSizeProvider property.
Halfling.Gui.Components.AutoSizing.Widge.AutoHeightMode: Whether the height of the widget is automatically sized to match its contents. The desired size of the contents is defined by the DesiredSizeProvider property.
Halfling.Gui.Components.AutoSizing.Widge.IgnoreForParentAutoSize: Whether the attached widget should be skipped when computing the desired size of its parent widget.
Halfling.Gui.Components.AutoSizing.Widge.WidthPadding: The amount of extra pixels to add when calculating the automatic width of the widget.
Halfling.Gui.Components.AutoSizing.Widge.HeightPadding: The amount of extra pixels to add when calculating the automatic height of the widget.
Halfling.Gui.Components.AutoSizing.Widge.MinWidth: The minimum width to which the widget will be automatically set.
Halfling.Gui.Components.AutoSizing.Widge.MaxWidth: The maximum width to which the widget will be automatically set.
Halfling.Gui.Components.AutoSizing.Widge.WidthValueType: Specifies how the MinWidth and MaxWidth values should be interpreted.
Halfling.Gui.Components.AutoSizing.Widge.MinHeight: The minimum height to which the widget will be automatically set.
Halfling.Gui.Components.AutoSizing.Widge.MaxHeight: The maximum height to which the widget will be automatically set.
Halfling.Gui.Components.AutoSizing.Widge.HeightValueType: Specifies how the MinHeight and MaxHeight values should be interpreted.
Halfling.Gui.Components.AutoSizing.Widge.Anchor: The anchor point around which the widget will be scaled when automatically sizing. The anchor point is a normalized, Widget-relative location with each dimension in the range of [0,1].

Halfling.Gui.Components.Droppers.WidgetD.AutoParentTargetToRoot: Whether the DropTarget should be automatically parented to the attached Widget's GuiRoot as a drop target.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Droppers.WidgetR.RestrictAutoSizeBounds: Whether the attached widget's auto-size bounds will be automatically restricted to its parent's boundaries.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Droppers.WidgetR.SourceCorner: When AutoPosition is true, gets or sets the corner of the source rect to which the attached widget's TargetCorner will be aligned.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Droppers.WidgetR.TargetCorner: When AutoPosition is true, gets or sets the corner of the attached Widget that will be aligned with the source rect's SourceCorner.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Droppers.WidgetR.PositionOffset: When AutoPosition is true, gets or sets the offset to apply to the position of the attached widget once it is aligned with the source rect.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Droppers.WidgetR.PreferredHorizontalSpace: The amount of horizontal space that is preferred when positioning the attached widget. If the amount of space available is less than this value, then the target may be located on the opposite side of the source rect if there is more space there.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Droppers.WidgetR.PreferredVerticalSpace: The amount of vertical space that is preferred when the positioning the attached widget. If the amount of space available is less than this value, then the target may be located on the opposite side of the source rect if there is more space there.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Droppers.WidgetR.MatchWidth: When AutoPosition is true, gets or sets whether the width of the attached widget will be modified to match the width of the source rect.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Droppers.WidgetR.MatchHeight: When AutoPosition is true, gets or sets whether the height of the attached widget wwill be modified to match the height of the source rect.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Focus.WidgetMous.EnableClickInsideToFocus: Whether clicking the inside of the Widget will cause it to be focused.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Focus.WidgetMous.EnableClickInsideToDefocus: Whether clicking inside of the Widget will cause its focus to be relinquished.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Focus.WidgetMous.EnableClickOutsideToDefocus: Whether clicking outside of the Widget will cause its focus to be relinquished.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Focus.WidgetMous.EnableMouseEnterToFocus: Whether moving the mouse inside the Widget will cause it to be focused.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Focus.WidgetMous.EnableMouseLeaveToDefocus: Whether moving the mouse outside of the Widget will cause its focus to be relinquished.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Focus.WidgetMous.EnableChainedWidgetChecking: When EnableClickOutsideToDefocus is true or EnableMouseLeaveToDefocus is true, gets or sets whether clicking on or mousing-over another Widget chained to the attached Widget will prevent the attached Widget from defocusing.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Focus.WidgetMous.MouseButton: The IInputButton that can be used to focus and defocus the Widget.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.GuiSpri.ShaderConstants: A collection of constant values for the Shader. This collection may be null if there is no Shader or there are no constants set.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.GuiSpri.VertexColor: The vertex color of the sprite.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.GuiSpri.UVRect: The UV coordinates on Texture of the sprite.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.GuiSpri.Bleed: The amount by which the sprite will extend passed its rect.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetN.Borders: The sizes of each of the border tiles of this nine-slice.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetN.Bleed: The amount which this nine-slice "bleeds" past the edges of the provided rect.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetN.ProvidedRectPadding: The amount of padding to add to the rectangle provided by this WidgetNineSlice.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetN.Flags: Bitwise-combinable flags that specify which sprites of this nine-slice are rendered.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetS.DesiredSize: The desired size of the sprite, which may then be scaled depending on the ScaleMode property.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetS.ScaleMode: Whether and how the sprite will be stretched or scaled to match the size of its containing rect.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetS.Clipped: Whether any portions of the sprite outside the container rect will be clipped.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetS.Padding: The empty space padding around the sprite.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetS.Enabled: Whether this WidgetSpriteUVManager will adjust the given WidgetSingleSprite's UVRect. Set this to false to not modify its UVRect.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetS.BaseUVRect: The UVRect that will be used when the entire sprite should be displayed.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetS.UMode: How the Sprite's UVRect's X and Width will be calculated.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetS.VMode: How the Sprite's UVRect's Y and Height will be calculated.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetS.RepeatWidth: Distance over which the Sprite's U coordinates will repeat when UMode is Repeat.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetS.RepeatHeight: The distance over which the Sprite's V coordinates will repeat when VMode is Repeat.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.Text: The text to be rendered. If the XmlFormatting property is true, then the text will be parsed for XML format tags.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.TextProvider: An ITextProvider object that, if not null, will keep this WidgetTextRenderer's Text property updated to match the ITextProvider.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.XmlFormatting: Whether the Text property will be parsed for XML format tags.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.Font: The font used to render the text.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.BackgroundColor: The color of the background behind the text.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.Shader: The shader used to render the text, or null to use the default shader.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.ShaderConstants: A collection of constant values for the Shader. This collection may be null if there is no Shader or there are no constants set.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.FontSize: The pixel size of the text font.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.FontStyle: The font style of the text.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.HAlignment: How the text is aligned horizontally within its Rect.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.VAlignment: How the text is aligned vertically within its Rect.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.InternalLineAlignment: How text of different sizes within the same line are aligned within that line.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.OversizeMode: Whether and how text that is too big to fit in its rect will be modified to fit.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.EllipsesText: The text to append when OversizeMode is set to Ellipses and the main text exceeds the maximum width.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.MaxRenderedChars: The maximum number of characters to render. The text will still be positioned and formatted as if all characters were rendered.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.CharacterMask: The optional CharacterMask used to change one character into another during rendering. This can be useful for implementing password fields. If null, then the character will be rendered as-is.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.Padding: The empty space padding around the text.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Graphics.WidgetT.Clip: Whether the text is clipped to the rect provided by RectProvider.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.BaseWidget.MouseButton: The mouse IInputButton to which the widget will respond.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.BaseWidget.DragCursor: The Cursor to show when the mouse is over the attached Widget.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetClic.Holdable: Whether the user can click and hold on the widget to generate repeated Clicked events.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetClic.HoldInitialRepeatInterval: The amount of time between the initial press of the widget and the first repeated Clicked event. Has no effect if Holdable is false.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetClic.HoldRepeatInterval: The amount of time between each Clicked event when the user is holding down the widget. Has no effect if Holdable is false.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetClic.HotkeyMode: Whether the player needs to press or release the hotkey to "click" the widget.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetDrag.MoveWhenBoundsChanged: Whether the DragTarget will be automatically moved to stay within bounds when the BoundsProvider's bounds changes.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetInpu.BlocksInput: Whether user input to widgets underneath will be blocked.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetInpu.Hotkey: The IInputButton (or combination of buttons) which will put the widget into a "HotkeyPressed" state.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetInpu.OverrideInputActive: If not null, overrides the attached widget's IsInputActive state for the purposes of this WidgetInputStateController.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetInpu.AllowHighlighted: Whether the widget is allowed to be in the "Highlighted" input state.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetInpu.AllowPressed: Whether the widget is allowed to be in the "Pressed" input state.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetInpu.AllowHotkeyPressed: Whether the widget is allowed to be in the "HotkeyPressed" input state.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetInpu.HighlightedCursor: The Cursor to show when the input state is "Highlighted".
Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetInpu.PressedCursor: The Cursor to show when the input state is "Pressed".

Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetScro.IncrementAmount: The amount by which the value will be incremented or decremented when using the scroll wheel.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetScro.EnableSmoothScrolling: If true, scrolling will happen smoothly over multiple frames instead of instantly on every click of the scroll wheel.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetScro.SmoothVelocityFraction: The smooth scrolling velocity (in units per second) as a fraction of the delta between the current value and target value.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetScro.MinSmoothVelocity: The minimum smooth scrolling velocity (in units per second) regardless of the delta between the current value and target value.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Input.WidgetScro.DisableInputHandling: Whether the component's input handling should be skipped.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Masking.NineSlic.MaskType: The type of masking technique to use.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Rects.FilledRect.HorizontalFillMode: How this FilledRectProvider will fill its rect horizontally as the provided value increases.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Rects.InnerRectP.ClipInnerRect: Whether the provided inner rect will be clipped so that it doesn't overhang the outer rect.

Halfling.Gui.Components.ScrollBars.Paddl.MouseButton: The mouse IInputButton to which the widget will respond.

Halfling.Gui.Components.ScrollBars.Paddl.PaddleDirection: The direction in which a paddle will move as the value increases.

Halfling.Gui.Components.ScrollBars.Paddl.PaddleValueSize: The size (width or height) of the paddle in value-relative units when the PaddleSizeMode is DynamicValue.
Halfling.Gui.Components.ScrollBars.Paddl.MinPaddlePixelSize: The minimum size (width or height) of the paddle when the PaddleSizeMode is DynamicValue.

Halfling.Gui.Components.ScrollBars.Scrol.RectController: The settings for the object that controls this ScrollBarPaddle's ScreenRect.
Halfling.Gui.Components.ScrollBars.Scrol.DragController: The settings for the object that controls how this ScrollBarPaddle is dragged.

Halfling.Gui.Components.ScrollBars.Widge.ShowHorizontal: The circumstances under which the horizontal ScrollBar is shown.
Halfling.Gui.Components.ScrollBars.Widge.ShowVertical: The circumstances under which the vertical ScrollBar is shown.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Selection.Select.MinSelected: The minimum number of Widgets that may be selected at a time. Calling AttemptToDeselect() will have no effect if the number of selected Widgets is not greater than this value. Modifying this property will have no immediate effect on the currently-selected Widgets.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Selection.Select.MaxSelected: The maximum number of Widgets that may be selected at a time. Calling AttemptToSelect() may fail or cause another Widget to become deselected depending on the value of the MaxSelectedMode property. Modifying this property will have no immediate effect on the currently-selected Widgets.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Selection.Select.MaxSelectedMode: The action to take when AttemptToSelect() is called but the maximum number of Widgets are already selected. Modifying this property will have no immediate effect on the currently-selected Widgets.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Selection.Select.SelectionChangeOrder: The order in which widgets will be selected and deselected when calling AttemptToSelect().

Halfling.Gui.Components.Selection.Widget.IsSelected: Whether the attached Widget is currently selected.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Selection.Widget.DeselectWhenDeactivated: Whether the attached widget will be automatically deselected when it is deactivated or its input is deactivated.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Selection.Widget.EnableSelect: Whether triggering when the Widget is deselected will cause it to be selected.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Selection.Widget.EnableDeselect: Whether triggering when the Widget is already selected will cause it to be deselected.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Text.WidgetKeybo.Holdable: Whether the user can press and hold the key to generate repeated selection changes.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Text.WidgetKeybo.HoldRepeatInterval: The amount of time between each selection change while the user is holding a key. Has no effect if Holdable is false.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Text.WidgetKeybo.SelectAllKey: The IInputButton that can be pressed to select all of the text.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Text.WidgetMouse.MouseButton: The mouse IInputButton that can be used to select text.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Text.WidgetMouse.DisableDuringImeTextComposition: Whether text selection will be disabled while the OS input method editor is composing text.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Text.WidgetTextC.CaretColor: The color of the text caret.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Text.WidgetTextC.CaretWidth: The width of the text caret.
Halfling.Gui.Components.Text.WidgetTextC.BlinkInterval: The amount of time between each blink of the caret.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Text.WidgetTextC.PasteHotkey: The IInputButton that can be used to paste text from clipboard.

Halfling.Gui.Components.Text.WidgetTextE.CharFilter: The CharFilter used to filter out or modify unwanted typed characters.

If the amount of space available is less than this value, then the widget may be located on the opposite side of the tool tip source if there is more space there.
Halfling.Gui.Components.ToolTips.WidgetT.PreferredVerticalSpace: The amount of vertical space that is preferred when positioning the attached widget. Halfling.Gui.Components.ToolTips.WidgetT.MatchHeight: When AutoPosition is true, gets or sets whether the height of the attached widget will be modified to match the height of the tool tip source.
Halfling.Gui.Components.ToolTips.WidgetT.Delay: The amount of time that showing a tool tip will be delayed. This property will implicitly get or set the DelayProvider property.

Halfling.Gui.Widget.Name: The name of this Widget. This property has no effect within the Halfling GUI library, but may be useful to the user program or for debugging purposes.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.AnchorRect: The rect in the parent to which this Widget is anchored. Use the AnchorPresets static class for a list of pre-defined anchor rects.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Rect: The rectangular position of this Widget relative to AnchorRect in pixel coordinates.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Location: The location of the upper-left of this Widget relative to the upper-left corner of AnchorRect in pixel coordinates.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Size: The size of this Widget assuming that the size of AnchorRect is zero.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.X: The x-coordinate of the left edge of this Widget relative to the left edge of AnchorRect in pixel coordinates.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Y: The y-coordinate of the top of this Widget relative to the top edge of AnchorRect in pixel coordinates.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Width: The width of this Widget in pixel coordinates assuming that the widget of AnchorRect is zero.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Height: The height of this Widget in pixel coordinates assuming that the height of AnchorRect is zero.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Left: The left edge of this widget relative to the left edge of AnchorRect in pixel coordinates. The position of the right edge will remain unmodified.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Right: The right edge of this widget relative to the right edge of AnchorRect in pixel coordinates. The position of the left edge will remain unmodified.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Top: The top edge of this widget relative to the top edge of AnchorRect in pixel coordinates. The position of the bottom edge will remain unmodified.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Bottom: The bottom edge of this widget relative to the bottom edge of AnchorRect in pixel coordinates. The position of the top edge will remain unmodified.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.PercentileRect: Sets the rectangular position of this Widget as a 0-100 percentage of its parent. Modifies the Rect and AnchorRect properties as needed.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.PercentileLocation: Sets the upper-left corner of this Widget as a 0-100 percentage of its parent. Modifies the Rect and AnchorRect properties as needed.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.PercentileSize: Sets the size of this Widget as a 0-100 percentage of its parent. Modifies the Rect and AnchorRect properties as needed.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.PercentileX: Sets the x-coordinate of the left edge of this Widget as a 0-100 percentage of its parent. Modifies the Rect and AnchorRect properties as needed.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.PercentileY: Sets the y-coordinate of the top edge of this Widget as a 0-100 percentage of its parent. Modifies the Rect and AnchorRect properties as needed.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.PercentileWidth: Sets the width of this Widget as a 0-100 percentage of its parent. Modifies the Rect and AnchorRect properties as needed.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.PercentileHeight: Sets the height of this Widget as a 0-100 percentage of its parent. Modifies the Rect and AnchorRect properties as needed.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.PercentileLeft: Sets the left edge of this Widget as a 0-100 percentage of its parent without modifying the right edge. Modifies the Rect and AnchorRect properties as needed.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.PercentileRight: Sets the left edge of this Widget as a 0-100 percentage of its parent without modifying the left edge. Modifies the Rect and AnchorRect properties as needed.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.PercentileTop: Sets the top edge of this Widget as a 0-100 percentage of its parent without modifying the bottom edge. Modifies the Rect and AnchorRect properties as needed.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.PercentileBottom: Sets the bottom edge of this Widget as a 0-100 percentage of its parent without modifying the top edge. Modifies the Rect and AnchorRect properties as needed.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Active: Whether this particular Widget is active. This Widget's IsActive property will only be true if its SelfActive property is true, it has a parent, and its parent's IsActive property is true.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.SelfInputActive: Whether this particular Widget is itself set to respond to user input. This Widget's IsInputActive property will only be true its SelfInputActive property is true, it has a parent, and its parent's IsInputActive property is true.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.DockMode: Which edge of its parent this widget will be docked to when placed in a WidgetDockedChildren list.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.IgnoreInLayout: Whether this widget will be ignored during automatic widget layout.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.InputBlockMode: Whether this widget blocks user input to widgets underneath it.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.RenderSettings: The settings that control how this Widget will be rendered. Settings specified here will be inherited by all child components and widgets.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Animator: The object that controls how this widget is animated.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.Triggers: The collection of triggers attached to this widget.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.ToolTips: The settings for displaying mouse-over tool tips for this widget.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.ToolTipDisplay: The object that displays tool tips for this widget and any child widgets that don't themselves have a ToolTipDisplay set.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.AutoSize: The settings for how this widget is automatically sized.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.CustomShapeFunc: The function used to determine whether a mouse cursor is "inside of" this Widget. A null value will simply check against this Widget's ScreenRect.
Halfling.Gui.Widget.UserData: An arbitrary data object attached to this Widget. The user application can attach anything it likes to this property.

Halfling.Particles.ParticleEmitterDef.EmitPerSecond: The number of particles that are spawned every second.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleEmitterDef.EmitPerOneShot: The number of particles that are spawned every time EmitOneShot() is called.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleEmitterDef.PreInitializers: A modifiable list containing the objects used to initialize particle data. These initializers will be run *before* the particle system's own initializers.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleEmitterDef.PostInitializers: A modifiable list containing the objects used to initialize particle data. These initializers will be run *after* the particle system's own initializers.

Halfling.Particles.ParticleSystemDef.EmitPerSecond: The number of particles that are spawned every second.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleSystemDef.EmitPerOneShot: The number of particles that are spawned every time EmitOneShot() is called.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleSystemDef.Initializers: A modifiable list containing the objects used to initialize particle data.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleSystemDef.Updaters: A modifiable list containing the objects used to update particle data.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleSystemDef.Renderer: The object that renders the actual particles.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleSystemDef.Material: The material used to render the particles.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleSystemDef.DeleteMode: The method used to delete particles.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleSystemDef.FpsCompensation: The method used to compensate for FPS during emission.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleSystemDef.FpsCompensationStrength: The strength of the FPS compensation.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleSystemDef.EnableMultithreading: Whether the particle system will run on multiple threads.
Halfling.Particles.ParticleSystemDef.InitCapacity: The initial number of particle that the particle system can store before needing to be resized.

Halfling.Particles.Renderers.BaseParticl.Name: The human-readable name of this object.

Halfling.Particles.Renderers.StandardPar.LocationIn: The data that stores the particle locations.
Halfling.Particles.Renderers.StandardPar.ScaleIn: The data, if any, that stores the particle scale factors.
Halfling.Particles.Renderers.StandardPar.Scale2In: The data, if any, that stores the particle scale factors.
Halfling.Particles.Renderers.StandardPar.RotationIn: The data, if any, that stores the particle rotations.

Halfling.Particles.Renderers.StandardPar.BaseSize: The base size of the particle quads.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.BaseParticle.Name: The human-readable name of this object.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.BaseParticle.Enabled: Whether this particle updater is currently enabled.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.BaseParticle.UpdateAfterInit: Whether this IParticleDataUpdater should be executed immediately after a new particle is initialized.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleLerp.LerpIn: The data that stores the T values used for the lerp.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleLerp.DataOut: The data that will received the lerped values.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleLerp.Invert: Whether the LerpIn value should be inverted.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleOper.DataType: The type of data being operated on.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleOper.BIn: The data that will be used as the "B" (right-hand) operand. This data will not be modified.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleOper.ResultOut: The data that will be set to the result of the operation.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleOper.Operator: The mathematical operator used on DataAInOut and DataBIn.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleOper.AIn: The data that will be used as the "A" (left-hand) operand.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleOper.ResultOut: The data that will be set to the result of the operation.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleOper.Operator: The mathematical operator used on DataAInOut and DataBIn.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleScal.AIn: The data that will be used as the "A" (left-hand) operand.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleScal.DataType: The type of data being operated on.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleScal.BIn: The data that will be used as the "B" (right-hand) operand. This data will not be modified.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleScal.ResultOut: The data that will be set to the result of the operation.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleScal.Operator: The mathematical operator used on DataAInOut and DataBIn.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleSetR.ColorOut: The data that will store the particle colors.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleSetR.HueRange: The possible range of color hues.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleSetR.SaturationRange: The possible range of color saturations.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleSetR.ValueRange: The possible range of color values.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleSetR.AlphaRange: The possible range of color alpha values.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleSetR.Direction: The initial direction of the particles as a random arc.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleSetR.LocationIn: The data that stores the particle locations.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleSetR.VelocityOut: The data that stores the particle velocities. This data will be modified.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleSetR.Speed: The initial speed of particles as a random range.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleSetV.DataOut: The data that will be initialized to the value.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleUvSp.SpriteSize: The size of an individual sprite in the texture.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleUvSp.SpriteCount: The number of sprites in the animation.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleUvSp.SpritesPerRow: The number of sprites in a single row in the texture.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleUvSp.PixelOffset: The offset in pixels of the sprites in the texture.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleUvSp.Looping: Whether the index should loop after reaching the end. If false, the index will be clamped by the sprite count.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleValu.LerpIn: The data that stores the T values used for the lerp.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleValu.CurveOut: The data that will received the lerped colors.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleValu.Invert: Whether the LerpIn value should be inverted.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleValu.Points: The array of points from which the output value will be interpolated.

Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleVari.LifeInOut: The data that stores the particle's energy. This data should be initialized to 1.0.
Halfling.Particles.Updaters.ParticleVari.LifetimeIn: The data that stores the particle's full lifetime.

Halfling.Physics2D.Dynamics.Drag.Exponen.MinDampingCoefficient: The minimum amount of damping applied as a factor of the relative velocity.
The minimum amount of damping applied as a factor of the relative velocity.

Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleLocat.LocationIn: The data that stores the location that should be converted to world space.
Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleLocat.LocationOut: The data that stores the converted location.

Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleRotat.RotationIn: The data that stores the rotation that should be converted to world space.
Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleRotat.RotationOut: The data that stores the converted rotation.

Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleScale.ScaleIn: The data that stores the location that should be converted to world space.
Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleScale.ScaleOut: The data that stores the converted location.

Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleSetWo.LocationOut: The data that stores the converted location.
Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleSetWo.LocalLocation: The local location that will be converted to a world location.

Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleSetWo.RotationOut: The data that stores the converted rotation.
Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleSetWo.LocalRotation: The local rotation that will be converted to a world rotation.

Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleSetWo.ScaleOut: The data that stores the converted location.
Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleSetWo.LocalScale: The the local scale that will be converted to a world scale.

Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleSetWo.VelocityOut: The data that stores the converted velocity.
Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleSetWo.LocalVelocity: The local velocity that will be converted to a world velocity.

Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleVeloc.VelocityIn: The data that stores the velocity that should be converted to world space.
Halfling.Scene2D.Particles.ParticleVeloc.VelocityOut: The data that stores the converted velocity.

Halfling.Windows.Cursor.File: The cursor bitmap, resolved relative to this file. Either this or [[Halfling.Windows.Cursor.OSCursor]] must be present.
Halfling.Windows.Cursor.HotSpot: The pixel inside the bitmap that sits on the exact mouse position, as `[x, y]` from the top-left. Defaults to the top-left corner.
Halfling.Windows.Cursor.Scale: A factor the cursor bitmap is scaled by when displayed.
Halfling.Windows.Cursor.OSCursor: A cursor supplied by the operating system instead of a bitmap, named by its standard role.
